works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: "Jiankang Shilu" is a collection of historical materials from the Six Dynasties compiled by Xu Song in the Tang Dynasty. Volume 4 of this book mentions that "The Emperor of Wu took the throne from Renyin in the first year of Huangwu to Bingshen in the first year of Zhide in the Tang Dynasty, a total of 535 years." It is known that this book was written no earlier than the era of Suzong of the Tang Dynasty, and the book records the establishment of the capital. A separate history of historical facts and anecdotes of the Wu, Eastern Jin, Song, Qi, Liang, and Chen dynasties in Jiankang (now Nanjing). There are 20 volumes in the book, which are incomplete. The author said in the "Preface" that this book "reflects the actions of the kings and ministers of the Six Dynasties. Things are detailed and concise, and the text is essential, and it is not necessary to mention it. The land, mountains, rivers, cities, palaces and gardens were established at that time, or they prospered and destroyed each other. The location is used to preserve the historical relics. If there are strange things that have been heard separately, and the words do not belong to each other, they are all noted to facilitate the understanding. It makes it possible to browse around without being bored, and to make appointments without missing anything." It was written in "Three Kingdoms" , "Book of Jin", "Book of Song", "Book of Nan Qi", "Book of Liang", "Book of Chen", "Shui Jing Zhu" and "Southern History", and records some information that is not included in the official history, so It is often cited by those who later researched the historical events of the Six Dynasties.
works.\u76f8\u5173\u4f5c\u54c1: 《景定建康志》、《六朝事迹编类》、《至正金陵新志》

works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: In the first month of the first year of Jingding (1260), Ma Guangzu organized Zhou Yinghe and others to compile "Jiankang Chronicles" in 50 volumes, compiling the earliest local chronicles of Nanjing that can be seen so far. .
works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: The content covers the establishment of Jiankang Prefecture, territory, mountains and rivers, cities, officials, imperial examinations, wealth, celebrities, Confucianism, literature, martial arts, rivers, canals, passes, epigraphy, famous sites, customs, etc. The style is unique and well-organized. , the text is concise and provides rich and detailed information for future generations to study the history of Nanjing.
works.\u76f8\u5173\u4f5c\u54c1: 《至正金陵新志》、《建康实录》、《六朝事迹编类》、《金陵古今图考》

works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: He was hired to compile 15 volumes of "Jinling New Chronicles", which was completed in half a year. This chronicle comprehensively compiles various documents and supplements the "Jingding Jiankang Chronicle" written by the Song Dynasty. In particular, it adds rich and precious local historical materials of the Yuan Dynasty.
works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: The journal has approximately 800,000 words and is divided into 15 volumes. The geographical scope of collecting historical materials is the three counties of Jiangning, Shangyuan and Jurong and the two prefectures of Lishui and Liyang under the jurisdiction of Jiqing Road (Nanjing) City in the Yuan Dynasty, which is equivalent to the territory under the jurisdiction of Jiankang Road (Nanjing) in the Southern Song Dynasty.

One of the Twenty-Four Histories, written by Zhang Tingyu of the Qing Dynasty and others, is a biographical history book. It records a total of 276 years of Ming Dynasty history from the first year of Hongwu by Zhu Yuanzhang (1368) to the seventeenth year of Chongzhen (1644) by Sizong of the Ming Dynasty. .

works.\u65f6\u671f: middle Ming Dynasty; late Ming Dynasty
works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: Describes the geographical situation, water and land transportation, household registration and taxes, streets, mountains and rivers, places of interest, dialects and common sayings, names of objects, astronomy and calendar, imperial examination system, customs, changes in customs, monks and nuns' temples in Jinling (now Nanjing), the southern capital of the Ming Dynasty. , historical anecdotes, sketches of famous people, anecdotes of famous people, literature and art, treatises on local chronicles, calligraphy, painting, inscriptions and inscriptions from past dynasties, annotations on classics and meanings, textual research and debates, legends and stories, wine, tea and fruits, flowers, birds, insects and fish, clothes and clothing, etc.

works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: After reading the Illustrated Classics and Records, Dunyi suspected that there were errors in the deeds of the Six Dynasties, so he took Wu Zhi, "Book of Jin" and biographies of Qi and Song Dynasties, and compared them with the inscriptions. He studied the historical facts of the Six Dynasties and the Jinling ruins, and divided them into categories Category it and compile it into a book.
works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: About 60,000 words, describing the historical facts of the Six Dynasties and the ruins of Jinling. It is divided into fourteen categories: general description, situation, city towers, towers, rivers, hills, houses, monasteries, temples, tombs, and inscriptions. The citations are quite detailed. It is called 2 volumes, but the pages are rich and the citations are detailed and comprehensive.

There are 15 pictures dedicated to the construction of Jinling from various countries to the Ming Dynasty. Because the regulations of Jinling city walls varied greatly over the past dynasties, diagrams were drawn to distinguish them. There is a self-preface, and each picture is accompanied by a description.
works.\u76f8\u5173\u4f5c\u54c1: 《金陵世纪》、《洪武京城图志》、《景定建康志》、《南畿志》

works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: Wen Renquan went to Nanjing to serve as the censor of Tixue. He started in Gongyuan and hired Chen Yi, a Nanjing native who had resigned, to lead 22 students from various counties and counties to edit "Nanji Chronicles".
works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: Nanji consists of the capital Yingtianfu and 13 prefectures and 004 prefectures, and roughly includes today's Jiangsu and Anhui provinces and Shanghai. The annals are divided into two categories: general annals and branch annals for each prefecture. The general annals include capital maps, southern capital records, geographical maps, Ji Tu Shi representatives, life officials, household registration, land tax, water conservancy, and military preparations. The annals of the prefecture contain the map of the prefecture, history, regions, cities and communities, construction, schools, ancestral tombs, historical sites, official relics, characters, women, foreign affairs, and art.

works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: The Ming Dynasty had long been destroyed when the author was born, and Nanjing, the capital of Hongguang's small court, had long been captured by the Qing army. He had no direct experience of the demise of the Ming Dynasty. But as a Han Chinese, he had an inseparable affection for the Ming Dynasty established by the Han people.
works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: A historical drama about Nanming.

works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: While suppressing the armed uprising, the rulers used methods such as the Daxing Literary Prison, the Eight-Part Part Essay and the Imperial Examination, and advocated Neo-Confucianism to use ruling ideas to imprison the scholars. Wu Jingzi opposed the Eight-Part Part Essay and the imperial examination system, and hated the scholars who were obsessed with crafts and fame and wealth. Custom.
works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: It is the only true satirical novel. When Wu Jingzi wrote this book, he focused on his attitude towards fame, wealth and literary origin. He devalued and criticized fake Confucian scholars and fake celebrities among scholars, praised and praised true Confucian scholars and real celebrities, and warmly praised those who had a complete and healthy personality and moral integrity. Intellectuals with both talent and talent.

works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587: 白发久孤鹦鹉杯，碧梧自老凤凰台。管夷吾亦仅如许，李谪仙今安在哉。城郭是非秋雨外，江山形胜暮潮来。小留只等中秋月，且放青冥万里开。

works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: It is a work on poetic aesthetics and poetry theory created by Yuan Mei. "Suiyuan Poetry Talk" discusses everything from the poet's innate qualifications to acquired moral cultivation, reading, study and social practice; from describing scenes and romances to chanting objects and history; from conception and conception to planning and refining sentences; From diction and rhyme to various expression techniques and artistic styles such as metaphor, sustenance, nature, emptiness, and twists, as well as the revision of poems, the appreciation of poems, the editing and selection of poems, and even the writing of poems, all aspects related to poetry , it can be said to be all-encompassing.

It is a work on poetic aesthetics and poetry theory created by Yuan Mei. works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: The content of this book is quite specific and provides a lot of original and precious information. It is an important historical material for studying the yellow registration system and household registration, taxation, Lijia, finance, officialdom and other aspects of the Ming Dynasty.
works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: A collection of documents documenting the historical events of Huangceku in Ming Dynasty.

works.\u65f6\u671f: early Qing Dynasty; middle Qing Dynasty
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587:
其一
沙漠真人本至尊，青蛇罢祀出梧垣。
孝陵松柏犹樵牧，元庙何妨有泪痕。
其二
想像承平乐事留，履綦陈迹也风流。
轻烟翠柳今何处，十六门如十六楼。
其三
洒扫他时属内官，钟声好句逼人寒。
御沟尽有流红事，尘壁伤心是媚兰。
其四
夜半传呼聚宝门，金蟾啮锁内城昏。
武皇大有南迁意，故遣銮舆宿报恩。
其五
蒙溪石刻表南都，形势居然屹壮图。
白马青丝他日事，仓皇曾补一毫无。
其六
妙选三家入后宫，抱来马上石榴红。
胭脂不到长城外，何必明妃怨画工。
其七
武定桥栏玉磬如，泠泠七尺响清虚。
蜀桐不发岐阳扣，等是昆冈劫火馀。
其八
宗伯奁清世不知，菱花初照月临池。
点妆巾帽俱新样，不用喧传镜背诗。
其九
杏村玉树接春华，太史诗留典客家。
不许绣毬誇独绝，凤台还有紫微花。
其十
薜鸭袁羊匕箸馀，江鲜风味只如初。
年年八月随潮上，柳贯红腮燕子鱼。
其十一
粉竹香尘调不齐，和来雁字斗高低。
即从红豆徵奇丽，便压元人十四题。
其十二
顿老琵琶擅教坊，供筵法曲别歌章。
故须小技通文义，垂老知音付漫郎。
其十三
雷雨随弦四座惊，秀之绝调自泠泠。
隔帘传语催停板，头白扶来制泪听。
其十四
昭文小楷法黄庭，绣佛新诗玉琢成。
不及锦云名句好，断肠芳草断肠莺。
其十五
一月花名簇锦筵，旧家手帕亦因缘。
曾陪盒子春檠会，冷落飘灯四十年。
其十六
鳌足盘龙气象吞，东华扶出尚惊魂。
书生小胆当前破，何取纷纷出大言。
其十七
后村傲兀自奇才，觅句曾过此地来。
春草不生山路白，树阴浓罩雨花台。
其十八
卧游宗炳已伤神，画社秦淮点染新。
怪得江山生色少，少冈老死更无人。
其十九
钞库街头第一坊，游人消渴试新尝。
近来曲巷添茶社，谁记新都是滥觞。
其二十
名士年来已可嗔，骑驴腰扇怕逢人。
李昭竹骨王郎画，难掩西风障扇尘。

works.\u65f6\u671f: early Qing Dynasty; middle Qing Dynasty
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587:
其一
沙漠真人本至尊，青蛇罢祀出梧垣。
孝陵松柏犹樵牧，元庙何妨有泪痕。
其二
想像承平乐事留，履綦陈迹也风流。
轻烟翠柳今何处，十六门如十六楼。
其三
洒扫他时属内官，钟声好句逼人寒。
御沟尽有流红事，尘壁伤心是媚兰。
其四
夜半传呼聚宝门，金蟾啮锁内城昏。
武皇大有南迁意，故遣銮舆宿报恩。
其五
蒙溪石刻表南都，形势居然屹壮图。
白马青丝他日事，仓皇曾补一毫无。
其六
妙选三家入后宫，抱来马上石榴红。
胭脂不到长城外，何必明妃怨画工。
其七
武定桥栏玉磬如，泠泠七尺响清虚。
蜀桐不发岐阳扣，等是昆冈劫火馀。
其八
宗伯奁清世不知，菱花初照月临池。
点妆巾帽俱新样，不用喧传镜背诗。
其九
杏村玉树接春华，太史诗留典客家。
不许绣毬誇独绝，凤台还有紫微花。
其十
薜鸭袁羊匕箸馀，江鲜风味只如初。
年年八月随潮上，柳贯红腮燕子鱼。
其十一
粉竹香尘调不齐，和来雁字斗高低。
即从红豆徵奇丽，便压元人十四题。
其十二
顿老琵琶擅教坊，供筵法曲别歌章。
故须小技通文义，垂老知音付漫郎。
其十三
雷雨随弦四座惊，秀之绝调自泠泠。
隔帘传语催停板，头白扶来制泪听。
其十四
昭文小楷法黄庭，绣佛新诗玉琢成。
不及锦云名句好，断肠芳草断肠莺。
其十五
一月花名簇锦筵，旧家手帕亦因缘。
曾陪盒子春檠会，冷落飘灯四十年。
其十六
鳌足盘龙气象吞，东华扶出尚惊魂。
书生小胆当前破，何取纷纷出大言。
其十七
后村傲兀自奇才，觅句曾过此地来。
春草不生山路白，树阴浓罩雨花台。
其十八
卧游宗炳已伤神，画社秦淮点染新。
怪得江山生色少，少冈老死更无人。
其十九
钞库街头第一坊，游人消渴试新尝。
近来曲巷添茶社，谁记新都是滥觞。
其二十
名士年来已可嗔，骑驴腰扇怕逢人。
李昭竹骨王郎画，难掩西风障扇尘。

works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: One, two, nostalgic for the past, three, chanting things.
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587:
其一：落星冈怀李白
骑鲸仙人钓鳌客，小驻金陵饮春色。鹭洲寂寞凤台荒，醉卧落星冈畔石。淋漓倒著宫锦袍，不独诗豪更酒豪。欲唤东风吹渌水，大江尽化为醇醪。山川佳丽仍如绘，我来望古遥相酹。双屐曾留藓字中，一旗尚曳禽言外。海东飞上白玉盘，无复醉影凌珊珊。光芒剩得诗千首，尚可高吟落星斗。
其二：静海寺
舣棹秋江十里长，招提结伴且寻将。凭谁寻路蹲狮子，怪尔开门叫凤凰。海外灵槎曾揽胜，岩端健笔自流芳。老僧不解观空旨，对客犹然说郑铛。
其三：姚坊门枣
新秋佳果说姚坊，火齐堆盘喜乍尝。甜到十分知过雨，艳生半颊为迎阳。戏抛恰可同心赤，饱掇何须患齿黄。记得吕家山畔路，薰风先递枣花香。

works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: One, two, nostalgic for the past, three, chanting things.
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587:
其一：落星冈怀李白
骑鲸仙人钓鳌客，小驻金陵饮春色。鹭洲寂寞凤台荒，醉卧落星冈畔石。淋漓倒著宫锦袍，不独诗豪更酒豪。欲唤东风吹渌水，大江尽化为醇醪。山川佳丽仍如绘，我来望古遥相酹。双屐曾留藓字中，一旗尚曳禽言外。海东飞上白玉盘，无复醉影凌珊珊。光芒剩得诗千首，尚可高吟落星斗。

works.\u65f6\u671f: Late Ming Dynasty; Early Qing Dynasty
works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: Describes the joy and charm of women immersed in love.

It specifically records the situation of Nanjing, the capital of the early Ming Dynasty, and divides it into 13 categories: palaces, city gates, mountains and rivers, temples, official offices, schools, temples, bridges, markets, buildings, warehouses, stables, and gardens, with "Imperial City" and "Capital City" attached. There are 9 pictures: "Mountains and Rivers", "Great Sacrificial Altar", "Mountains and Rivers Altar", "Temple", "Official Office", "Chinese Studies", "Street Market Bridge" and "Louguan".

works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587: 跻险入幽林，翠微含竹殿。泉声无休歇，山色时隐见。潮来杂风雨，梅落成霜霰。一从方外游，顿觉尘心变。

works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: At the end of the late Tang Dynasty, the politics were dark and the official career was bumpy, and the poet tended to be reclusive.
works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: Describe the emptiness of Qixia Temple and praise the sense of seclusion.
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587:
不见明居士，空山但寂寥。
白莲吟次缺，青霭坐来销。
泉冷无三伏，松枯有六朝。
何时石上月，相对论逍遥。

works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: After Emperor Taizu Zhu Yuanzhang of the Ming Dynasty unified the world, he built Yuejiang Tower on the top of Lion Mountain in Nanjing. He personally wrote a "Record of Yuejiang Tower" and then ordered each of the civil servants to write one. So the author wrote this "Yuejiang Tower".
works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: "The Story of Yuejiang Tower" is a eulogizing prose written by Song Lian, a writer in the Ming Dynasty. In the article, the author writes about the beautiful scenery of Yuejiang Tower in the capital (now Nanjing), which is intended to embellish the prosperous times and praise the virtues of the Ming Dynasty. At the same time, the author also hopes that the emperor can appease both internal and external affairs, be considerate of people's livelihood, and incorporate the idea of loyalty to the emperor and concern for the people. The full text is both narrative and argumentative, both parallel and prose, which further demonstrates the author's extraordinary skill.

works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: In 490 AD, Xie Zhen was 27 years old. The young poet was full of confidence in his fame, career and future. In August of this year, he followed Wang Xiao Zilong as the governor of Jingzhou, and Xie Tao moved to Zhenxi Gong Cao and transferred to literature. On the way to Jingzhou, he followed Wang's instructions and wrote ten pieces of "Drumming Music". This song "Entering the Dynasty Song" is one of them.
works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: It depicts the splendor and prosperity of the imperial capital Jinling (now Nanjing) and the enterprising spirit of pursuing fame. It is both an ode and a magnificent picture. No matter what kind of emotion and interest it is, it can be expressed through mountains and rivers, and the scene can be blended together. The majestic environment, beautiful and neat writing, and fresh and fluent language reflect young Xie Tiao's enterprising spirit and extraordinary artistic skills. The first couplet summarizes the situation and fills the chapter with a virtual pen. The middle three couplets are written in the name of "Emperor State" and describe the current situation of "Emperor State" in detail. The poet pays special attention to changes in perspective. Looking along the majestic imperial capital, you can see the city walls surrounding the winding moat, rippling green waves, and beautiful scenery; looking up into the distance, you can see high-rise buildings, row after row, which look brilliant and brilliant under the sunlight. This is a couplet, describing a distant view. Sanlian takes a closer look. The last couplet wraps up the whole story. As has been the custom in ancient times, when one achieves success and is famous, one goes on stage to receive rewards.
works.\u76f8\u5173\u4e8b\u4ef6: Xie Tiao went to Jingzhou
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587:
江南佳丽地，金陵帝王州。
逶迤带绿水，迢递起朱楼。
飞甍夹驰道，垂杨荫御沟。
凝笳翼高盖，叠鼓送华辀。
献纳云台表，功名良可收。

works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: The first chronicle is "Mountain Names", and the second chronicles are "Caves", "Ponds and Springs", "Palaces", "Grass and Trees", "Magic Weapons", "Traveling", and "Lizao". His writing is quite close to a travel note, but does not follow the conventions of chronicles.

works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587:
桃叶歌，歌宛转，旧日秦淮水清浅，此曲之兴自早晚。
青溪桥边日欲斜，白土冈下驱虞车，越州女子颜如花。
中官采取来天家，可怜马上弹琵琶。三月桃花四月叶，巳报北兵屯六合。
两宫塞上行，日逐江东猎。桃叶复桃根，残英委白门。
相逢冶城下，犹有六朝魂。

works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: Zhang Zhu once lived in Jinling and experienced the rise and fall of the Yuan Dynasty, so many of his poems were written about sadness and chaos.
works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: Using scenery to express feelings, through the rendering of scenery with symbolic meaning, it conveys the desolate feeling of despair.
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587:
运尽黄旗晋祚开，天星俄复坼三台。
方惊掘地双鹅起，已见浮江五马来。
草暗离宫碑仆土，雨残战地骨生苔。
当时一掬新亭泪，犹带寒潮日暮回。

works.\u65f6\u671f: Late Ming Dynasty; Early Qing Dynasty
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587: 形胜当年百战收，子孙容易失神州。金川事去家还在，《玉树》歌残恨未休。徐邓功勋谁甲第，方黄骸骨总荒丘。可怜一片秦淮月，曾照降幡出石头。

works.\u65f6\u671f: Late Ming Dynasty; Early Qing Dynasty
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587: 形胜当年百战收，子孙容易失神州。金川事去家还在，《玉树》歌残恨未休。徐邓功勋谁甲第，方黄骸骨总荒丘。可怜一片秦淮月，曾照降幡出石头。

works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: The concept of "Taicheng" focuses on the symbol of imperial power in the ancient capital, where the old palaces and gardens were located. The poet uses the image of the Tang Dynasty that "thousands of households and thousands of doors have become weeds" to question and hold the author accountable for the work "Flowers in the Backyard of the Yushu" written by Empress Chen, who lived in drunken dreams and died. The historical facts of debauchery and debauchery that harmed the country can be understood at a glance, and they are as clear as a fire.
works.\u76f8\u5173\u4f5c\u54c1: 《金陵五题》、《西塞山怀古》、《金陵怀古》
works.\u76f8\u5173\u4e8b\u4ef6: Liu Yuxi was appointed governor of Hezhou
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587: 台城六代竞豪华，结绮临春事最奢。万户千门成野草，只缘一曲后庭花。
晚云阴映下空城，六代累累夕照明。玉井已干龙不起，金瓯虽破虎曾争。亦知霸世才难得，却是蒙尘事最平。深谷作陵山作海，茂弘流辈莫伤情。

works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: Pay homage to the historical sites of the Six Dynasties and nostalgic for the past.
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587: 江雨霏霏江草齐，六朝如梦鸟空啼。无情最是台城柳，依旧烟笼十里堤。

works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: The six short-lived dynasties from Wu to Chen, one by one, declined and overturned. The sad historical facts and the silent ruins of the walls made many poets sigh and chant repeatedly. They painted old trees and cold clouds in Jinling and wrote about the past - all sad. Those who take "Taicheng" as their title include Liu Yuxi in the front and Wei Zhuang in the back. When chanting "Taicheng", each one leads the way. This poem by Zhang Qiao also chants Taicheng. Although the "name" is not as good as that of Liu and Wei, it is still written with charm and profound meaning.
works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: This poem describes the desolate scene of Taicheng in the late Tang Dynasty. There are flowers and trees growing at the base of the palace, and fishermen and woodcutters are surrounded by battlements, which is quite stimulating and thought-provoking.
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587:
宫殿余基长草花，景阳官树噪村鸦。
云屯雉堞依然在，空绕渔樵四五家。

works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: Kong Zhigui used the tone of fictional mountains and forests to sharply satirize the so-called hermits who pretended to live in seclusion in the mountains but sincerely yearned for glory and wealth. There is also a theory that it is a joke to ridicule people who pretend to live in seclusion in the mountains.
works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: The whole article uses personification to describe the humiliating and angry mood of the mountain scenery. The description of the mountains, rivers and vegetation is vivid, the language is refined and beautiful, the dialogues are neat, the phonology is harmonious, and it is full of strong lyrical charm. The author brings out all the mountain and forest scenery, and makes them full of human emotions, such as joy, anger, sorrow, and joy, talking, laughing, cursing, teasing, and ridiculing, pointing the finger at the fake hermit Zhou Yong, thus profoundly exposing the fact that the scholar-bureaucrats are pretending to be noble and noble, but they are actually working hard. The ugly soul of striving for fame and gain has profound anti-judgment and practical significance.

works.\u4f5c\u54c1\u540d: 《玉树后庭花（宝床香重春眠觉）》
works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: This poem is a poem that pays tribute to history and pays tribute to ancient times. The poet visited Taicheng, which was prosperous and beautiful in the past but is now desolate and ruined. He chanted the historical tragedy of Chen Houzhu, the last emperor of the Six Dynasties, who was dissolute and subjugated his country. He lamented the past and present and expressed his nostalgia for the past.
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587:
宝床香重春眠觉，魫窗难晓。新声丽色千人，歌后庭清妙。
青骢一骑来飞鸟，靓妆难好。至今落日寒蟾，照台城秋草。

works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: In the fourth year of Chunxi reign of Emperor Xiaozong of the Song Dynasty (1177), the author sent an envoy from Sichuan to summon him back. He set out from Chengdu in May, took the waterway eastward, and arrived in Lin'an (today's Hangzhou, Zhejiang) in October. He wrote this book along with his diary experiences. . The title is taken from the meaning of the sentence "The ship is moored at the door of Eastern Wu Wanli" in Du Fu's poem "The First Four Sentences".
works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: "Wu Chuan Lu" recorded his itinerary day by day, with specific and detailed descriptions of every place he passed along the way, including the local monuments, people and geography. Fan Chengda stayed in Jiankang for six days, mainly inspecting places such as the Heart-Appreciating Pavilion, Yulin Hall, Fugui Tower, Qingxi Pavilion, and Liuhe Changlu Temple. He had a thorough understanding of the beautiful scenery and road traffic conditions of these places. The vivid description and beautiful writing not only have literary value, but also have historical value for understanding Nanjing, the capital of the Southern Song Dynasty.
works.\u76f8\u5173\u4f5c\u54c1: 《宝颜堂秘笈》、《四库全书》、《知不足斋丛书》、《笔记小说大观》、《丛书集成初编》、《续百川学海》、《稗乘》、《说郛》

works.\u65f6\u671f: Late Ming Dynasty; Early Qing Dynasty; Middle Qing Dynasty
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587: 栖鸦流水点秋光，爱此萧疏树几行。不与行人绾离别，赋成谢女雪飞香。

布幔朱阑饰彩舟，少年箕踞放中流。三弦拨动《凉州》调，故老听来尽白头。
端阳节近说青溪，芳草门前踏作泥。到得秋来人寂寞，月明衰柳夜乌啼。
北人才得解征鞍，也学吴侬事事酸。金碗银盘都不用，素磁月下试龙团。

works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: I have a considerable degree of sympathy for Empress Chen, especially Zhang Lihua.
works.\u76f8\u5173\u4e8b\u4ef6: Empress Chen and her concubine threw themselves into a well
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587: 铁锁横江，琼枝歌阙，繁华王气同消歇。无愁帝子不归来，宫花落尽胭脂色。争似芳魂，还依故国，灵旗潜下青溪侧。风声吹树总为云，婵娟冷挂南朝月。

蜡炬高高，龙烟细细。玉楼十二门初闭。疏帘不卷水晶寒，小屏半掩琉璃翠。桃叶新声，榴花美味。南山宾客东山妓。利名不肯放人闲，忙中偷取工夫醉。

works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: He collected extensive mountain scriptures and geographical records, carefully studied the original story, and compiled two hundred volumes of the masterpiece "Taiping Huanyu Ji".
works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: The book describes in detail the establishment history, place names, city governance and migration, mountain and river situation, economic products, population increase and decrease, customs and culture, surnames, characters, art, historical sites and legends of various prefectures, states and counties across the country, as well as It lists the place names of the sixteen states of Yanyun ceded to Khitan by the Later Jin Dynasty, which is of great reference value for future generations to study historical geography, society, economy, and culture.

works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587:
牙签插架和云散，数卷惊重见。开编见我问书知，犹忆秋窗分坐校仇时。
先生手泽增身价，墨迹如新写。他年归送小仓山，化鹤人归可念浦珠还。

works.\u65f6\u671f: middle Ming Dynasty; late Ming Dynasty
works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: It records in detail the history, distribution of temples, public land properties, landscapes and historical sites, famous Taoist deeds, land rent and taxes, Taoist rules and regulations of various Taoist temples in the Ming Dynasty.

works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: In the 16th year of the Yuan Dynasty (1279), Deng Yan was captured in Yashan and escorted north together with Wen Tianxiang. Deng Yan wrote this poem while passing through Jiankang.
works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: This poem contains a deep and affectionate feeling of nostalgic for the past and the present, and the language is extremely bright. It can be called a masterpiece among Deng Shan's existing poems. The first part of the poem reads, "The sparse rain washes the sky, and the pillow mat is cool. A single leaf of the Tung tree makes the sound of autumn." The replacement of solar terms with the retreat of summer and cold illustrates the philosophy of life of prosperity and decline. The Song Dynasty fell, and the homeland was no longer there. It was like a cold autumn, which made people feel discouraged and lazy. "Sparse rain washes the sky and makes it clear." However, the sky is clear and the world is unclear, so what can be done about it. The coolness of the pillows and mats indoors is a realistic representation of the cooler weather and climate change in autumn, and the fallen leaves of the outdoor well trees not only signify autumn, but also evoke the poet's lament about his own life experience. It sets off the atmosphere of the whole poem and contains deep nostalgic emotions. "Who would think that a guest's body is as light as a leaf, floating thousands of miles away?" The leaves drifting in the wind illustrates the uncontrollability of personal destiny, and also shows the author's sadness for the fall of the country. "Thousands of Miles" summarizes the journey from being captured in Guangdong to Jiankang. The next part of the poem reads, "The dream breaks through the ancient city of Taicheng. The moon is pale and the tide is flat." The infinite grief is difficult to resolve. Deng Yan took it to sleep, but when he woke up, he found that the dream in Gutai City was desolate. The poet's mood was already very sad, but when he woke up and saw the dim moonlight and the rising tide, he couldn't help but feel excited, and his heart became even more miserable. After waking up from the dream, he can only go to "visit the new pavilion with wine". At the Xinting meeting that year, Director Wang also advocated "strengthening the royal family and conquering China", but both the poet and Wen Tianxiang were captured, and the Song Dynasty was completely destroyed. "I don't see the Wang Xie's house at that time, and the tobacco is green." Deng Yan's emotion about the impermanence of the world and the rise and fall of dynasties is particularly profound because of his real experience. The conclusion empathizes with the scene, embodying the euphemistic and tragic sound of the country's subjugation. The curls are endless.
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587: 疏雨洗天清，枕簟凉生。井桐一叶做秋声。谁念客身轻似叶，千里飘零？梦断古台城、，月淡潮平。便须携酒访新亭。不见当时王谢宅，烟草青青。

works.\u65f6\u671f: Middle Qing Dynasty; Late Qing Dynasty; Republic of China
works.\u76f8\u5173\u4f5c\u54c1: 《运渎桥道小志》、《凤麓小志》、《金陵通传》

works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: After being appointed as the prefect of Jiangning, Wang Anshi presided over the construction project of converting Xuanwu Lake into farmland in the eighth year of Xining (1075).
works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: He suggested "opening a cross river source and draining out the remaining water" in Xuanwu Lake, "so that the poor and hungry people can fully enjoy the benefits of snails, clams, fish and shrimps; after the water recedes, they can be distributed to the poor people and serve as officials." Planting is also a plan for next year."

works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587:
三台令
春色，春色，依旧青门紫陌。日斜柳暗花蔫，醉卧谁家少年。年少，年少，行乐直须及早。
明月，明月，照得离人愁绝。更深影入空床，不道帏屏夜长。长夜，长夜，梦到庭花阴下。
南浦，南浦，翠鬟离人何处。当时携手高楼，依旧楼前水流。流水，流水，中有伤心双泪。
上行杯
落梅着雨消残粉，云重烟轻寒食近。罗幕遮香，柳外秋千出画墙。
春山颠倒钗横凤，飞絮入檐春睡重。梦里佳期，只许庭花与月知。
玉楼春
雪云乍变春云簇，渐觉年华堪纵目。北枝梅蕊犯寒开，南浦波纹如酒绿。
芳菲次第长相续，自是情多无处足。尊前百计见春归，莫为伤春眉黛蹙。
如梦令
尘拂玉台鸾镜，凤髻不堪重整。绡帐泣流苏，愁掩玉屏人静。多病，多病，自是行云无定。
更漏子
金剪刀，青丝发，香墨蛮笺亲札。和粉泪，一时封，此情千万重。
垂蓬鬓，尘青镜，已分今生薄命。将远恨，上高楼，寒江天外流。
更漏子
秋水平，黄叶晚，落日渡头云散。抬朱箔，挂金钩，暮潮人倚楼。
欢娱地，思前事，歌罢不胜沉醉。消息远，梦魂狂，酒醒空断肠。
更漏子
风带寒，秋正好，蕙兰无端先老。云杳杳，树依依，离人殊未归。
搴罗幕，凭朱阁，不独堪悲寥落。月东出，雁南飞，谁家夜捣衣。
更漏子
夜初长，人近别，梦断一窗残月。鹦鹉睡，蟪蛄鸣，西风寒未成。
红蜡烛，半棋局，床上画屏山绿。搴绣幌，倚瑶琴，前欢泪满襟。
更漏子
雁孤飞，人独坐，看却一秋空过。瑶草短，菊花残，萧条渐向寒。
帘幕里，青苔地，谁信闲愁如醉。星移后，月圆时，风摇夜合枝。
抛球乐
酒罢歌余兴未阑，小桥秋水共盘桓。波摇梅蕊当心白，风入罗衣贴体寒。且莫思归去，须尽笙歌此夕欢。
抛球乐
尽日登高兴未残，红楼人散独盘桓。一钩冷露悬珠箔，满面西风凭玉阑。归去须沉醉，小院新池月乍寒。
抛球乐
逐胜归来雨未晴，楼前风重草烟轻。谷莺语软花边过，水调声长醉里听。款举金觥劝，谁是当筵最有情。
抛球乐
坐对高楼千万山，雁飞秋色满阑干。烧残红烛暮云合，飘尽碧梧金井寒。咫尺人千里，犹忆笙歌昨夜欢。
抛球乐
梅落新春入后庭，眼前风物可无情。曲池波晚冰还合，芳草迎船绿未成。且上高楼望，相共凭栏看月生。
抛球乐
霜积秋山万树红，倚帘楼上挂朱栊。白云天远重重恨，黄草烟深淅淅风。仿佛梁州曲，吹在谁家玉笛中。
抛球乐
莫怨登高白玉杯，茱萸微绽菊花开。池塘水冷鸳鸯起，帘幕烟寒翡翠来。重待烧红烛，留取笙歌莫放回。
抛球乐
年少王孙有俊才，登高欢醉夜忘回。歌阑赏尽珊瑚树，情厚重斟琥珀杯。但愿千千岁，金菊年年秋解开。
芳草渡
梧桐落，蓼花秋。烟初冷，雨才收。萧条风物正堪愁。人去后，多少恨，在心头。
燕鸿远，羌笛怨，渺渺澄江一片。山如黛，月如钩。笙歌散，魂梦断，倚高楼。
采桑子
樱桃谢了梨花发，红白相催。燕子归来，几度香风绿户开。
人间乐事知多少，且酹金杯。管咽弦哀，慢引萧娘舞袖回。
金错刀
日融融，草芊芊，黄莺求友啼林前。柳条袅袅拖金线，花蕊茸茸簇锦毡。
鸠逐妇，燕穿帘，狂蜂浪蝶相翩翩。春光堪赏还堪玩，恼煞东风误少年。
金错刀
双玉斗，百琼壶，佳人欢饮笑喧呼。麒麟欲画时难偶，鸥鹭何猜兴不孤。
歌宛转，醉模糊，高烧银烛卧流苏。只销几觉懵腾睡，身外功名任有无。
长命女
春日宴，绿酒一杯歌一遍。再拜陈三愿：一愿郎君千岁，二愿妾身常健，三愿如同梁上燕，岁岁长相见。
长相思
红满枝，绿满枝，宿雨厌厌睡起迟。闲庭花影移。
忆归期，数归期，梦见虽多相见稀。相逢知几时。
南乡子
细雨泣秋风，金凤花残满地红。闲蹙黛眉慵不语，情绪，寂寞相思知几许。
玉枕拥孤衾，挹恨还同岁月深。帘卷曲房谁共醉，憔悴，惆怅秦楼弹粉泪。
思越人
酒醒情怀恶，金缕褪，玉肌如削。寒食过却，海棠零落。
乍倚遍栏杆，烟澹薄，翠幕帘栊笼画阁。春睡着，觉来失、秋千期约。
相见欢
晓窗梦到昭华，阿琼家。欹枕残妆一朵，卧枝花。
情极处，却无语，玉钗斜。翠阁银屏回首，已天涯。
酒泉子
庭下花飞，月照妆楼春事晚。珠帘风，兰烛烬，怨空闺。
迢迢何处寄相思，玉箸零零肠断。屏帏深，更漏永，梦魂迷。
酒泉子
云散更深，堂上孤灯阶下月。早梅香，残雪白，夜沉沉。
栏边偷唱系瑶簪，前事总堪惆怅。寒风生，罗衣薄，万般心。
酒泉子
庭树霜凋，一夜愁人窗下睡。绣帏风，兰烛焰，梦遥遥。
金笼鹦鹉怨长宵，笼畔玉筝弦断。陇头云，桃源路，两魂销。
酒泉子
春色融融，飞燕乍来莺未语。小桃寒，垂杨晚，玉楼空。
天长烟远恨重重，消息燕鸿归去。枕前灯，窗外月，闭朱栊。
酒泉子
深院空帏，廊下风帘惊宿燕。香印灰，兰烛小，觉来时。
月明人自捣寒衣，刚爱无端惆怅。阶前行，栏畔立，欲鸡啼。
酒泉子
芳草长川，柳映危桥桥下路。归鸿飞，行人去，碧山边。
风微烟澹雨萧然，隔岸马嘶何处。九回肠，双脸泪，夕阳天。
清平乐
深冬寒月，庭户凝霜雪。风雁过时魂断绝，塞管数声呜咽。
披衣独立披香，流苏乱结愁肠。往事总堪惆怅，前欢休要思量。
清平乐
雨晴烟晚，绿水新池满。双燕飞来垂柳院，小阁画帘高卷。
黄昏独倚朱栏，西南新月眉弯。砌下落花风起，罗衣特地春寒。
清平乐
西园春早，夹径抽新草。冰散漪澜生碧沼，寒在梅花先老。
与君同饮金杯，饮余相取徘徊。次第小桃将发，轩车莫厌频来。
莫思归
花满名园酒满觞，且开笑口对穠芳。秋千风暖鸾钗嚲，绮陌春深翠袖香。莫惜黄金贵，日日须教贳酒尝。
喜迁莺
宿莺啼，乡梦断，春树晓朦胧。残灯吹烬闭朱栊，人语隔屏风。
香已寒，灯已绝，忽忆去年离别。石城花雨倚江楼，波上木兰舟。
喜迁莺
雾蒙蒙，风淅淅，杨柳带疏烟。飘飘轻絮满南园，墙下草芊绵。
燕初飞，莺已老，拂面春风长好。相逢携手且高歌，人生得几何。
菩萨蛮
金波远逐行人去，疏星时作银河渡。花影卧秋千，更长人不眠。
玉筝弹未彻，凤髻鸾钗脱。忆梦翠蛾低，微风凉绣衣。
菩萨蛮
沉沉朱户横金锁，纱窗月影随花过。烛泪欲阑干，落梅生晚寒。
宝钗横翠凤，千里香屏梦。云雨已荒凉，江南春草长。
菩萨蛮
画堂昨夜西风过，绣帘时拂朱门锁。惊梦不成云，双蛾枕上颦。
金炉烟袅袅，烛暗纱窗晓。残月尚弯环，玉筝和泪弹。
菩萨蛮
欹鬟堕髻摇双桨，采莲晚出清江上。顾影约流萍，楚歌娇未成。
相逢颦翠黛，笑把珠珰解。家住柳阴中，画桥东复东。
菩萨蛮
梅花吹入谁家笛，行云半夜凝空碧。欹枕不成眠，关山人未还。
声随幽怨绝，云断澄霜月。月影下重帘，轻风花满檐。
菩萨蛮
娇鬟堆枕钗横凤，溶溶春水杨花梦。红烛泪阑干，翠屏烟浪寒。
锦壶催画箭，玉佩天涯远。和泪试严妆，落梅飞晓霜。
菩萨蛮
西风袅袅凌歌扇，秋期正与行人远。花叶脱霜红，流莺残月中。
兰闺人在否，千里重楼暮。翠被已消香，梦随寒漏长。
菩萨蛮
回廊远砌生秋草，梦魂千里青门道。鹦鹉怨长更，碧笼金锁横。
罗帏中夜起，霜月清如水。玉露不成圆，宝筝悲断弦。
贺圣朝
金丝帐暖牙床稳，怀香方寸。轻颦轻笑，汗珠微透，柳沾花润。
云鬟斜坠，春应未已，不胜娇困。半欹犀枕，乱缠珠被，转羞人问。
虞美人
画堂新霁情萧索，深夜垂珠箔。洞房人睡月婵娟，梧桐双影上朱轩，立阶前。
高楼何处连宵宴，塞管吹幽怨。一声已断别离心，旧欢抛弃杳难寻，恨沉沉。
虞美人
碧波帘幕垂朱户，帘下莺莺语。薄罗依旧泣青春，野花芳草逐年新，事难论。
凤笙何处高楼月，幽怨凭谁说。须臾残照上梧桐，一时弹泪与东风，恨重重。
虞美人
玉钩弯柱调鹦鹉，宛转留春语。云屏冷落画堂空，薄晚春寒无奈落花风。
搴帘燕子低飞去，拂镜尘鸾舞。不知今夜月眉弯，谁佩同心双结倚栏杆。
虞美人
春山拂拂横秋水，掩映遥相对。只知长坐碧窗期，谁信东风吹散绿霞飞。
银屏梦与飞鸾远，只有珠帘卷。杨花零落月溶溶，尘掩玉筝弦柱画堂空。
寿山曲
铜壶滴漏初尽，高阁鸡鸣半空。催启五门金锁，犹垂三殿帘栊。阶前御柳摇绿，仗下宫花散红。鸳瓦数行晓日，鸾旗百尺春风。侍臣舞蹈重拜，圣寿南山永同。
舞春风
严妆才罢怨春风，粉墙画壁宋家东。蕙兰有恨枝犹绿，桃李无言花自红。
燕燕巢时帘幕卷，莺莺啼处凤楼空。少年薄幸知何处，每夜归来春梦中。
醉花间
独立阶前星又月，帘栊偏皎洁。霜树尽空枝，肠断丁香结。
夜深寒不彻，凝恨何曾歇。凭栏杆欲折。两条玉箸为君垂，此宵情，谁共说。
醉花间
月落霜繁深院闭，洞房人正睡。桐树倚雕檐，金井临瑶砌。
晓风寒不啻，独立成憔悴。闲愁浑未已。人心情绪自无端，莫思量，休退悔。
醉花间
晴雪小园春未到，池边梅自早。高树鹊衔巢，斜月明寒草。
山川风景好，自古金陵道。少年看却老。相逢莫厌醉金杯，别离多，欢会少。
醉花间
林雀归栖撩乱语，阶前还日暮。屏掩画堂深，帘卷萧萧雨。
玉人何处去，鹊喜浑无据。双眉愁几许。漏声看却夜将阑，点寒灯，扃绣户。
醉桃源
南园春半踏青时，风和闻马嘶。青梅如豆柳如眉，日长蝴蝶飞。
花露重，草烟低，人家帘幕垂。秋千慵困解罗衣，画梁双燕归。
醉桃源

寻断梦，掩香闺，行人去路迷。门前杨柳绿阴齐，何时闻马嘶。
忆江南
去岁迎春楼上月，正是西窗，夜凉时节。玉人贪睡坠钗云，粉消香薄见天真。
人非风月长依旧，破镜尘筝，一梦经年瘦。今宵帘幕扬花阴，空馀枕泪独伤心。
忆江南
今日相逢花未发，正是去年，别离时节。东风次第有花开，恁时须约却重来。
重来不怕花堪折，只怕明年，花发人离别。别离若向百花时，东风弹泪有谁知。
忆秦娥
风淅淅，夜雨连云黑。滴滴，窗下芭蕉灯下客。
除非魂梦到乡国，免被关山隔。忆忆，一句枕前争忘得。
谒金门
圣明世，独折一枝丹桂。学着荷衣还可喜，春狂不啻□。
年少都来有几，自古闲愁无际。满盏劝君休惜醉，愿君千万岁。
应天长
石城山下桃花绽，宿雨初收云未散。南去棹，北归雁，水阔天遥肠欲断。
倚楼情绪懒，惆怅春心无限。忍泪蒹葭风晚，欲归愁满面。
应天长
朱颜日日惊憔悴，多少离愁谁得会。人事改，空追悔，枕上夜长只如岁。
红绡三尺泪，双结解时心醉。魂梦万重云水，觉来还不睡。
应天长
石城花落江楼雨，云隔长洲兰芷暮。花草岸，和烟雾，谁在绿杨深处住。
旧游时事故，岁晚离人何处。杳杳兰舟西去，魂归巫峡路。
应天长
兰房一宿还归去，底死谩生留不住。枕前语，记得否，说尽从来两心素。
同心牢记取，切莫等闲相许。后会不知何处，双栖人莫妒。
应天长
当时心事偷相许，宴罢兰堂肠断处。挑银灯，扃珠户，绣被微寒值秋雨。
枕前和泪语，惊觉玉笼鹦鹉。一夜万般情绪，朦胧天欲曙。
临江仙
南园池馆花如雪，小塘春水涟漪。夕阳楼上绣帘垂，酒醒无寐，独自倚栏时。
绿杨风静凝闲恨，千言万语黄鹂。旧欢前事杳难追，高唐暮雨，空只觉相思。
点绛唇
荫绿围红，梦琼家在桃源住。画桥当路，临水双朱户。
柳径春深，行到关情处。颦不语，意凭风絮，吹向郎边去。
鹊踏枝
烦恼韶光能几许，肠断魂销，看却春还去。只喜墙头灵鹊语，不知青鸟全相误。
心若垂杨千万缕，水阔花飞，梦断巫山路。开眼新愁无问处，珠帘锦帐相思否。
鹊踏枝
芳草满园花满目，帘外微微，细雨笼庭竹。杨柳千条珠簏簌，碧池波绉鸳鸯浴。
窈窕人家颜似玉，弦管泠泠，齐奏云和曲。公子欢筵犹未足，斜阳不用相催促。
河畔青芜堤上柳，为问新愁，何事年年有。独立小桥风满袖，平林新月人归后。
鹊踏枝

独立荒池斜日岸，墙外遥山，隐隐连天汉。忽忆当年歌舞伴，晚来双脸啼痕满。
鹊踏枝
粉映墙头寒欲尽，宫漏长时，酒醒人犹困。一点春心无限恨，罗衣印满啼妆粉。
柳岸花飞寒食近，陌上行人，杳不传芳信。楼上重檐山隐隐，东风尽日吹蝉鬓。
鹊踏枝

满眼游丝兼落絮，红杏开时，一霎清明雨。浓睡觉来莺乱语，惊残好梦无寻处。

叵耐为人情太薄，几度思量，真拟浑抛却。新结同心香未落，怎生负得当初约。

鹊踏枝
萧索清秋珠泪坠，枕簟微凉，展转浑无寐。残酒欲醒中夜起，月明如练天如水。
阶下寒声啼络纬，庭树金风，悄悄重门闭。可惜旧欢携手地，思量一夕成憔悴。
鹊踏枝
花外寒鸡天欲曙，香印成灰，起坐浑无绪。檐际高桐凝宿雾，卷帘双鹊惊飞去。
屏上罗衣闲绣缕，一晌关情，忆遍江南路。夜夜梦魂休漫语，已知前事无寻处。
鹤冲天
晓月坠，宿云披，银烛锦屏帏。建章钟动玉绳低，宫漏出花迟。
春态浅，来双燕，红日初长一线。严妆欲罢啭黄鹂，飞上万年枝。
浣溪沙
春到青门柳色黄，一梢红杏出低墙。莺窗人起未梳妆。
绣帐已阑离别梦，玉炉空袅寂寥香。闺中红日奈何长。
浣溪沙
醉忆春山独倚楼，远山回合暮云收。波间隐隐仞归舟。
早是出门长带月，可堪分袂又经秋。晚风斜日不胜愁。
浣溪沙
转烛飘蓬一梦归，欲寻陈迹怅人非。天教心愿与身违。
待月池台空逝水，荫花楼阁漫斜晖。登临不惜更沾衣。

三台令
春色，春色，依旧青门紫陌。日斜柳暗花蔫，醉卧谁家少年。年少，年少，行乐直须及早。
明月，明月，照得离人愁绝。更深影入空床，不道帏屏夜长。长夜，长夜，梦到庭花阴下。
南浦，南浦，翠鬟离人何处。当时携手高楼，依旧楼前水流。流水，流水，中有伤心双泪。
上行杯
落梅着雨消残粉，云重烟轻寒食近。罗幕遮香，柳外秋千出画墙。
春山颠倒钗横凤，飞絮入檐春睡重。梦里佳期，只许庭花与月知。
玉楼春
雪云乍变春云簇，渐觉年华堪纵目。北枝梅蕊犯寒开，南浦波纹如酒绿。
芳菲次第长相续，自是情多无处足。尊前百计见春归，莫为伤春眉黛蹙。
如梦令
尘拂玉台鸾镜，凤髻不堪重整。绡帐泣流苏，愁掩玉屏人静。多病，多病，自是行云无定。
更漏子
金剪刀，青丝发，香墨蛮笺亲札。和粉泪，一时封，此情千万重。
垂蓬鬓，尘青镜，已分今生薄命。将远恨，上高楼，寒江天外流。
更漏子
秋水平，黄叶晚，落日渡头云散。抬朱箔，挂金钩，暮潮人倚楼。
欢娱地，思前事，歌罢不胜沉醉。消息远，梦魂狂，酒醒空断肠。
更漏子
风带寒，秋正好，蕙兰无端先老。云杳杳，树依依，离人殊未归。
搴罗幕，凭朱阁，不独堪悲寥落。月东出，雁南飞，谁家夜捣衣。
更漏子
夜初长，人近别，梦断一窗残月。鹦鹉睡，蟪蛄鸣，西风寒未成。
红蜡烛，半棋局，床上画屏山绿。搴绣幌，倚瑶琴，前欢泪满襟。
更漏子
雁孤飞，人独坐，看却一秋空过。瑶草短，菊花残，萧条渐向寒。
帘幕里，青苔地，谁信闲愁如醉。星移后，月圆时，风摇夜合枝。
抛球乐
酒罢歌余兴未阑，小桥秋水共盘桓。波摇梅蕊当心白，风入罗衣贴体寒。且莫思归去，须尽笙歌此夕欢。
抛球乐
尽日登高兴未残，红楼人散独盘桓。一钩冷露悬珠箔，满面西风凭玉阑。归去须沉醉，小院新池月乍寒。
抛球乐
逐胜归来雨未晴，楼前风重草烟轻。谷莺语软花边过，水调声长醉里听。款举金觥劝，谁是当筵最有情。
抛球乐
坐对高楼千万山，雁飞秋色满阑干。烧残红烛暮云合，飘尽碧梧金井寒。咫尺人千里，犹忆笙歌昨夜欢。
抛球乐
梅落新春入后庭，眼前风物可无情。曲池波晚冰还合，芳草迎船绿未成。且上高楼望，相共凭栏看月生。
抛球乐
霜积秋山万树红，倚帘楼上挂朱栊。白云天远重重恨，黄草烟深淅淅风。仿佛梁州曲，吹在谁家玉笛中。
抛球乐
莫怨登高白玉杯，茱萸微绽菊花开。池塘水冷鸳鸯起，帘幕烟寒翡翠来。重待烧红烛，留取笙歌莫放回。
抛球乐
年少王孙有俊才，登高欢醉夜忘回。歌阑赏尽珊瑚树，情厚重斟琥珀杯。但愿千千岁，金菊年年秋解开。
芳草渡
梧桐落，蓼花秋。烟初冷，雨才收。萧条风物正堪愁。人去后，多少恨，在心头。
燕鸿远，羌笛怨，渺渺澄江一片。山如黛，月如钩。笙歌散，魂梦断，倚高楼。
采桑子
樱桃谢了梨花发，红白相催。燕子归来，几度香风绿户开。
人间乐事知多少，且酹金杯。管咽弦哀，慢引萧娘舞袖回。
金错刀
日融融，草芊芊，黄莺求友啼林前。柳条袅袅拖金线，花蕊茸茸簇锦毡。

金错刀
双玉斗，百琼壶，佳人欢饮笑喧呼。麒麟欲画时难偶，鸥鹭何猜兴不孤。
歌宛转，醉模糊，高烧银烛卧流苏。只销几觉懵腾睡，身外功名任有无。
长命女
春日宴，绿酒一杯歌一遍。再拜陈三愿：一愿郎君千岁，二愿妾身常健，三愿如同梁上燕，岁岁长相见。
长相思
红满枝，绿满枝，宿雨厌厌睡起迟。闲庭花影移。
忆归期，数归期，梦见虽多相见稀。相逢知几时。
南乡子
细雨泣秋风，金凤花残满地红。闲蹙黛眉慵不语，情绪，寂寞相思知几许。
玉枕拥孤衾，挹恨还同岁月深。帘卷曲房谁共醉，憔悴，惆怅秦楼弹粉泪。
思越人
酒醒情怀恶，金缕褪，玉肌如削。寒食过却，海棠零落。
乍倚遍栏杆，烟澹薄，翠幕帘栊笼画阁。春睡着，觉来失、秋千期约。
相见欢
晓窗梦到昭华，阿琼家。欹枕残妆一朵，卧枝花。
情极处，却无语，玉钗斜。翠阁银屏回首，已天涯。
酒泉子
庭下花飞，月照妆楼春事晚。珠帘风，兰烛烬，怨空闺。
迢迢何处寄相思，玉箸零零肠断。屏帏深，更漏永，梦魂迷。

栏边偷唱系瑶簪，前事总堪惆怅。寒风生，罗衣薄，万般心。
酒泉子
庭树霜凋，一夜愁人窗下睡。绣帏风，兰烛焰，梦遥遥。

春色融融，飞燕乍来莺未语。小桃寒，垂杨晚，玉楼空。
天长烟远恨重重，消息燕鸿归去。枕前灯，窗外月，闭朱栊。
酒泉子
深院空帏，廊下风帘惊宿燕。香印灰，兰烛小，觉来时。
月明人自捣寒衣，刚爱无端惆怅。阶前行，栏畔立，欲鸡啼。
酒泉子
芳草长川，柳映危桥桥下路。归鸿飞，行人去，碧山边。
风微烟澹雨萧然，隔岸马嘶何处。九回肠，双脸泪，夕阳天。

深冬寒月，庭户凝霜雪。风雁过时魂断绝，塞管数声呜咽。
披衣独立披香，流苏乱结愁肠。往事总堪惆怅，前欢休要思量。
清平乐
雨晴烟晚，绿水新池满。双燕飞来垂柳院，小阁画帘高卷。
黄昏独倚朱栏，西南新月眉弯。砌下落花风起，罗衣特地春寒。
清平乐
西园春早，夹径抽新草。冰散漪澜生碧沼，寒在梅花先老。
与君同饮金杯，饮余相取徘徊。次第小桃将发，轩车莫厌频来。
莫思归
花满名园酒满觞，且开笑口对穠芳。秋千风暖鸾钗嚲，绮陌春深翠袖香。莫惜黄金贵，日日须教贳酒尝。
喜迁莺
宿莺啼，乡梦断，春树晓朦胧。残灯吹烬闭朱栊，人语隔屏风。
香已寒，灯已绝，忽忆去年离别。石城花雨倚江楼，波上木兰舟。
喜迁莺
雾蒙蒙，风淅淅，杨柳带疏烟。飘飘轻絮满南园，墙下草芊绵。
燕初飞，莺已老，拂面春风长好。相逢携手且高歌，人生得几何。
菩萨蛮
金波远逐行人去，疏星时作银河渡。花影卧秋千，更长人不眠。
玉筝弹未彻，凤髻鸾钗脱。忆梦翠蛾低，微风凉绣衣。
菩萨蛮
沉沉朱户横金锁，纱窗月影随花过。烛泪欲阑干，落梅生晚寒。
宝钗横翠凤，千里香屏梦。云雨已荒凉，江南春草长。
菩萨蛮
画堂昨夜西风过，绣帘时拂朱门锁。惊梦不成云，双蛾枕上颦。
金炉烟袅袅，烛暗纱窗晓。残月尚弯环，玉筝和泪弹。
菩萨蛮
欹鬟堕髻摇双桨，采莲晚出清江上。顾影约流萍，楚歌娇未成。
相逢颦翠黛，笑把珠珰解。家住柳阴中，画桥东复东。
菩萨蛮
梅花吹入谁家笛，行云半夜凝空碧。欹枕不成眠，关山人未还。
声随幽怨绝，云断澄霜月。月影下重帘，轻风花满檐。
菩萨蛮
娇鬟堆枕钗横凤，溶溶春水杨花梦。红烛泪阑干，翠屏烟浪寒。
锦壶催画箭，玉佩天涯远。和泪试严妆，落梅飞晓霜。
菩萨蛮
西风袅袅凌歌扇，秋期正与行人远。花叶脱霜红，流莺残月中。
兰闺人在否，千里重楼暮。翠被已消香，梦随寒漏长。
菩萨蛮
回廊远砌生秋草，梦魂千里青门道。鹦鹉怨长更，碧笼金锁横。
罗帏中夜起，霜月清如水。玉露不成圆，宝筝悲断弦。
贺圣朝
金丝帐暖牙床稳，怀香方寸。轻颦轻笑，汗珠微透，柳沾花润。
云鬟斜坠，春应未已，不胜娇困。半欹犀枕，乱缠珠被，转羞人问。
虞美人
画堂新霁情萧索，深夜垂珠箔。洞房人睡月婵娟，梧桐双影上朱轩，立阶前。
高楼何处连宵宴，塞管吹幽怨。一声已断别离心，旧欢抛弃杳难寻，恨沉沉。
虞美人
碧波帘幕垂朱户，帘下莺莺语。薄罗依旧泣青春，野花芳草逐年新，事难论。
凤笙何处高楼月，幽怨凭谁说。须臾残照上梧桐，一时弹泪与东风，恨重重。
虞美人
玉钩弯柱调鹦鹉，宛转留春语。云屏冷落画堂空，薄晚春寒无奈落花风。
搴帘燕子低飞去，拂镜尘鸾舞。不知今夜月眉弯，谁佩同心双结倚栏杆。
虞美人
春山拂拂横秋水，掩映遥相对。只知长坐碧窗期，谁信东风吹散绿霞飞。
银屏梦与飞鸾远，只有珠帘卷。杨花零落月溶溶，尘掩玉筝弦柱画堂空。
寿山曲
铜壶滴漏初尽，高阁鸡鸣半空。催启五门金锁，犹垂三殿帘栊。阶前御柳摇绿，仗下宫花散红。鸳瓦数行晓日，鸾旗百尺春风。侍臣舞蹈重拜，圣寿南山永同。
舞春风
严妆才罢怨春风，粉墙画壁宋家东。蕙兰有恨枝犹绿，桃李无言花自红。
燕燕巢时帘幕卷，莺莺啼处凤楼空。少年薄幸知何处，每夜归来春梦中。
醉花间
独立阶前星又月，帘栊偏皎洁。霜树尽空枝，肠断丁香结。
夜深寒不彻，凝恨何曾歇。凭栏杆欲折。两条玉箸为君垂，此宵情，谁共说。
醉花间
月落霜繁深院闭，洞房人正睡。桐树倚雕檐，金井临瑶砌。
晓风寒不啻，独立成憔悴。闲愁浑未已。人心情绪自无端，莫思量，休退悔。
醉花间
晴雪小园春未到，池边梅自早。高树鹊衔巢，斜月明寒草。
山川风景好，自古金陵道。少年看却老。相逢莫厌醉金杯，别离多，欢会少。
醉花间
林雀归栖撩乱语，阶前还日暮。屏掩画堂深，帘卷萧萧雨。
玉人何处去，鹊喜浑无据。双眉愁几许。漏声看却夜将阑，点寒灯，扃绣户。
醉桃源
南园春半踏青时，风和闻马嘶。青梅如豆柳如眉，日长蝴蝶飞。
花露重，草烟低，人家帘幕垂。秋千慵困解罗衣，画梁双燕归。
醉桃源
角声吹断陇梅枝，孤窗月影低。塞鸿无限欲惊飞，城乌休夜啼。
寻断梦，掩香闺，行人去路迷。门前杨柳绿阴齐，何时闻马嘶。
忆江南
去岁迎春楼上月，正是西窗，夜凉时节。玉人贪睡坠钗云，粉消香薄见天真。
人非风月长依旧，破镜尘筝，一梦经年瘦。今宵帘幕扬花阴，空馀枕泪独伤心。
忆江南
今日相逢花未发，正是去年，别离时节。东风次第有花开，恁时须约却重来。
重来不怕花堪折，只怕明年，花发人离别。别离若向百花时，东风弹泪有谁知。
忆秦娥
风淅淅，夜雨连云黑。滴滴，窗下芭蕉灯下客。
除非魂梦到乡国，免被关山隔。忆忆，一句枕前争忘得。
谒金门
圣明世，独折一枝丹桂。学着荷衣还可喜，春狂不啻□。
年少都来有几，自古闲愁无际。满盏劝君休惜醉，愿君千万岁。
应天长
石城山下桃花绽，宿雨初收云未散。南去棹，北归雁，水阔天遥肠欲断。
倚楼情绪懒，惆怅春心无限。忍泪蒹葭风晚，欲归愁满面。
应天长
朱颜日日惊憔悴，多少离愁谁得会。人事改，空追悔，枕上夜长只如岁。
红绡三尺泪，双结解时心醉。魂梦万重云水，觉来还不睡。
应天长
石城花落江楼雨，云隔长洲兰芷暮。花草岸，和烟雾，谁在绿杨深处住。
旧游时事故，岁晚离人何处。杳杳兰舟西去，魂归巫峡路。
应天长
兰房一宿还归去，底死谩生留不住。枕前语，记得否，说尽从来两心素。
同心牢记取，切莫等闲相许。后会不知何处，双栖人莫妒。
应天长
当时心事偷相许，宴罢兰堂肠断处。挑银灯，扃珠户，绣被微寒值秋雨。
枕前和泪语，惊觉玉笼鹦鹉。一夜万般情绪，朦胧天欲曙。
临江仙
南园池馆花如雪，小塘春水涟漪。夕阳楼上绣帘垂，酒醒无寐，独自倚栏时。
绿杨风静凝闲恨，千言万语黄鹂。旧欢前事杳难追，高唐暮雨，空只觉相思。
点绛唇
荫绿围红，梦琼家在桃源住。画桥当路，临水双朱户。
柳径春深，行到关情处。颦不语，意凭风絮，吹向郎边去。
鹊踏枝
烦恼韶光能几许，肠断魂销，看却春还去。只喜墙头灵鹊语，不知青鸟全相误。
心若垂杨千万缕，水阔花飞，梦断巫山路。开眼新愁无问处，珠帘锦帐相思否。
鹊踏枝
芳草满园花满目，帘外微微，细雨笼庭竹。杨柳千条珠簏簌，碧池波绉鸳鸯浴。
窈窕人家颜似玉，弦管泠泠，齐奏云和曲。公子欢筵犹未足，斜阳不用相催促。
河畔青芜堤上柳，为问新愁，何事年年有。独立小桥风满袖，平林新月人归后。
鹊踏枝
霜落小园瑶草短，瘦叶和风，惆怅芳时换。懊恨年年秋不管，朦胧如梦空肠断。
独立荒池斜日岸，墙外遥山，隐隐连天汉。忽忆当年歌舞伴，晚来双脸啼痕满。
鹊踏枝
粉映墙头寒欲尽，宫漏长时，酒醒人犹困。一点春心无限恨，罗衣印满啼妆粉。
柳岸花飞寒食近，陌上行人，杳不传芳信。楼上重檐山隐隐，东风尽日吹蝉鬓。
鹊踏枝
六曲阑干偎碧树，杨柳风轻，展尽黄金缕。谁把钿筝移玉柱，穿帘海燕双飞去。
满眼游丝兼落絮，红杏开时，一霎清明雨。浓睡觉来莺乱语，惊残好梦无寻处。
鹊踏枝
叵耐为人情太薄，几度思量，真拟浑抛却。新结同心香未落，怎生负得当初约。
休向尊前情索莫，手举金罍，凭仗深深酌。莫作等闲相斗作，与君保取长欢乐。
鹊踏枝
萧索清秋珠泪坠，枕簟微凉，展转浑无寐。残酒欲醒中夜起，月明如练天如水。
阶下寒声啼络纬，庭树金风，悄悄重门闭。可惜旧欢携手地，思量一夕成憔悴。
鹊踏枝
花外寒鸡天欲曙，香印成灰，起坐浑无绪。檐际高桐凝宿雾，卷帘双鹊惊飞去。
屏上罗衣闲绣缕，一晌关情，忆遍江南路。夜夜梦魂休漫语，已知前事无寻处。
鹤冲天
晓月坠，宿云披，银烛锦屏帏。建章钟动玉绳低，宫漏出花迟。
春态浅，来双燕，红日初长一线。严妆欲罢啭黄鹂，飞上万年枝。
浣溪沙
春到青门柳色黄，一梢红杏出低墙。莺窗人起未梳妆。
绣帐已阑离别梦，玉炉空袅寂寥香。闺中红日奈何长。
浣溪沙
醉忆春山独倚楼，远山回合暮云收。波间隐隐仞归舟。
早是出门长带月，可堪分袂又经秋。晚风斜日不胜愁。
浣溪沙
转烛飘蓬一梦归，欲寻陈迹怅人非。天教心愿与身违。
待月池台空逝水，荫花楼阁漫斜晖。登临不惜更沾衣。

works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: This poem was probably written when Li Bai visited Jinling in the eighth year of Emperor Xuanzong's Tianbao reign (749). At that time, Li Bai had been away from Chang'an for many years, and he was still concerned about the fate of the country and attached to the imperial court, so he used Laolao Pavilion to say goodbye to express his sadness of being unappreciated.
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587: 金陵劳劳送客堂，蔓草离离生道旁。古情不尽东流水，此地悲风愁白杨。我乘素舸同康乐，朗咏清川飞夜霜。昔闻牛渚吟五章，今来何谢袁家郎。苦竹寒声动秋月，独宿空帘归梦长。

works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: The author visited Waguan Temple.
works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: Describes the impressions of visiting Waguan Temple.

works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587: 美人迟暮谁容悦，兰芷秋江只自芳。无数南朝新乐府，千秋独唱郁金堂。

works.\u76f8\u5173\u4f5c\u54c1: 《万里行记》、《中国学术思想史随笔》、《现代中国近鉴》
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587:
湖水千秋有断霞,池边树冷暮啼鸦。
柳条攀折愁谁诉?帆影沿江几片斜。

works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: The words between the lines reveal how happy he is that his life will be stable after the catastrophe in his later years. His attitude is peaceful and his mood is harmonious, just like the calm waters of Lake Mochou under the afterglow of spring.
works.\u76f8\u5173\u4e8b\u4ef6: In the fifty-eighth year of the Qianlong reign of the Qing Dynasty (1793), Li Xiaodong, the prefect of Jiangning, rebuilt the Yujin Hall and other buildings, but they were later destroyed.
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587: 郁金堂不在，湖上剩佳名。虎气腾钟阜，波声撼石城。低飞双燕子，细语一仓庚。寂历楼阴直，残阳入水明。

works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: The words between the lines reveal how happy he is that his life will be stable after the catastrophe in his later years. His attitude is peaceful and his mood is harmonious, just like the calm waters of Lake Mochou under the afterglow of spring.
works.\u76f8\u5173\u4e8b\u4ef6: In the fifty-eighth year of the Qianlong reign of the Qing Dynasty (1793), Li Xiaodong, the prefect of Jiangning, rebuilt the Yujin Hall and other buildings, but they were later destroyed.
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587: 郁金堂不在，湖上剩佳名。虎气腾钟阜，波声撼石城。低飞双燕子，细语一仓庚。寂历楼阴直，残阳入水明。

works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: First, the first sentence uses "beauty grows old" to describe the decline of Mochou Lake. The second line of the poem affirms that it has its own fragrance that does not require recognition from others. Three or four lines of poems express Lin Zhixia's yearning for beautiful legends, and at the same time reflect his outstanding character that is very different from the times. Secondly, the first and second sentences slightly satirize Zhu and Xu for overly caring about utilitarian gains and losses - even playing games like chess. The following two sentences express dissatisfaction with Zhu Yuanzhang's granting the building to Xu Da.
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587:
其一
美人迟暮谁容悦？兰芷秋江只自芳。无数南朝新乐府，千秋独唱郁金堂！
其二
事功劫后一微尘，著手输赢总认真。何必私为汤沐邑？黄金寸土属齐民！

works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: First, the first sentence uses "beauty grows old" to describe the decline of Mochou Lake. The second line of the poem affirms that it has its own fragrance that does not require recognition from others. Three or four lines of poems express Lin Zhixia's yearning for beautiful legends, and at the same time reflect his outstanding character that is very different from the times. Secondly, the first and second sentences slightly satirize Zhu and Xu for overly caring about utilitarian gains and losses - even playing games like chess. The following two sentences express dissatisfaction with Zhu Yuanzhang's granting the building to Xu Da.
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587:
其一
美人迟暮谁容悦？兰芷秋江只自芳。无数南朝新乐府，千秋独唱郁金堂！
其二
事功劫后一微尘，著手输赢总认真。何必私为汤沐邑？黄金寸土属齐民！

works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: Mochou Lake in the Ming Dynasty was a ten-mile flat lake. It can be seen that the current lake surface has shrunk a lot. This poem says that Mochou Lake was the place where Mochou girls sang and danced in the past. Looking back now, it seems that the green mountains in the distance and the weeping willows around the embankment still leave people behind.
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587: 十里平湖带画桥，当年歌舞惜妖娇。空馀菱镜明秋水，无复兰兵荡晚潮。隔岸远山分翠黛，绕堤垂柳学纤腰。风流未觉秾华歇，一望烟波醉不消。

其一
何处能令客思消，沿流出郭到红桥。
更无丽瞩堪娱悦，随苑风光是柳条。
其二
千丝吐尽尚为蚕，花月心情事事堪。
前夕家园争乞巧，穿针小妇忆江南。
其三
游侠髯麻柳敬亭，恢谐笑骂不曾停。
重逢快说隋家事，又向河亭一日听。

works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: This poem is based on a true story. On September 16, the third year of Tongzhi (1864), Huang Wanli, a woman from the south of the Yangtze River, committed suicide while traveling against the river in Xiangtan. She wrote ten quatrains on the wall and wrote a poem preface to describe the whole story. The long poem was processed based on this incident. This poem by Jin He accuses the officers and soldiers of hunting for beauty and killing the people from a side.
works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: This poem exposed the crimes of the Qing army, commended Huang Wanli for her bravery, and inspired women to fight against violence. "There are no bloody beauties in this world, and the beauty of gold and green is really mud. I don't see you, Huang Wanli." Therefore, he chose the tragic ending of Huang Wanli killing two powerful men and then committing suicide.
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587: 君不见黄婉梨，生不甘为仇人妻。虎狼累月相提携。一夕杀之如杀犬与鸡。贞魂烈魄虽下地，浩气上与青天齐。一解。婉梨金陵人，儒风旧家是。癸丑陷贼中，女生五龄耳。有母有弟有兄嫂，全家种菜隐乡里。阿母教针线，阿兄授书史。门外污者尘，门内清如水。二解。朝朝盼官兵，十有二年久。官兵既收城，全家开笑口。叩门来一兵，状貌比贼丑。搜屋无一钱，怒掣刀在手。女前跪致词，请以身代母。兵曰不杀汝，杀汝全家人，汝能飞去否。三解。全家被杀时，女木立若痴。兵徐缚女出，鞭马还怒驰。江干舣有船，驱女使上之。告以归湘南，妻汝汝勿疑。四解。女心默自计，我死宁有他。我固不惜死。全家仇则那。忍泪向仇语，我方身有疴。随汝到汝家，嫁汝缔茑萝。今有同船人，男妇数十多。汝若苦逼我，我惟沈江波。不见金家妇，汝奈江波何。时有金眉姑．亦金陵人，上船时投江死。仇竟帖耳听，不敢相诋诃。朝夕敬事女，水程累月过。五解。水程累月尽，舍舟当就陆。同舟人各行，同行一仇独。女心摇摇撞小鹿，此去不知何处宿。何日诛仇死瞑目。六解。行未数里，横来一人。伴仇而走，甚狎且亲。数数目女道女美，彼此虐谑纷笑嗔。女闻无言眉暗颦，两恶男子竟不训，我一弱女宁其伦，事急惟有死，保我金玉身。报仇在今夕，万一沉冤伸．不报亦今夕，含悲报千春。逆旅急偷闲，留诗壁问尘。后有读之者，为我聊酸辛。七解。倚装儿何时，白日暗平楚。两伧买酒肴，烧烛照门户。呼女陪壶觞，教女伴歌舞。鴂音恣号呶，时杂莺燕语。逆旅夫何知，夜寐各宾主。八解。明日之日日正中．房门不启人无踪。破扃睨视生悲风，一男中鸩死，口鼻皆青红。一男毒较轻，自刃洞在胸。一女挂罗中，遍身穷裤穷。细读壁间诗，了了陈始终。乃知女所为。辣手真从容。万口啧啧称女雄。此女毋乃人中龙。九解。噫吁嘻。女事虽幸成，女心尤惨凄。色身饵人饿虎蹊，一日未死忧噬脐。欲死不死呼天啼，至诚所动天听低。乃以杯酒为媒梯，仇雠刃毕月未西，青溪之水魂归兮。世无血性诸红闺，绮罗金翠真土泥。君不见，黄婉梨。十解。

works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: "Ascending the Phoenix Terrace of Jinling" is one of the few seven-character poems in Li Bai's collection. One theory is that this poem was written when the author was ordered to "give gold back to the mountain" in the sixth year of Tianbao (747) and was expelled from Chang'an to travel to Jinling in the south. Another theory is that it was written by the author after he was exiled to Yelang and returned after being pardoned. Some people say that this poem was written by Li Bai during his tour. Yellow Crane Tower, and wrote after "There is a view in front of me, but Cui Hao wrote a poem on it", because he wanted to compete with Cui Hao's "Yellow Crane Tower".
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587: 凤凰台上凤凰游，凤去台空江自流。吴宫花草埋幽径，晋代衣冠成古丘。三山半落青天外，二水中分白鹭洲。总为浮云能蔽日，长安不见使人愁。

works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: Fuzhou Mountain, now called Jiuhua Mountain, is the first hill on the west side of the Zhongshan Mountains approaching the city. Because the side facing Xuanwu Lake is as steep as a cut, it looks like an overturned boat, so it was called Fuzhou Mountain in ancient times. The author listed in the preface to the poem all the famous places of interest during the Six Dynasties period. The highlight of this poem is the use of the word "capture the boat" as an article that borrows from the past and satirizes the present, comparing the foolish kings of the past dynasties to the same raccoon dog. Fuck the snake god: the mountain god, it is also a witness to history. Xinting: Located in the southwest of Jiankang City, it was a place where people who migrated south during the Eastern Jin Dynasty mourned the memory of the Central Plains. Luobin: refers to Luoyang, also refers to the Central Plains. Jiangzuo: Jiangnan. The Southern Song Dynasty was relatively peaceful, and the dignitaries "wanted to miss Shu". The author reminded the world through the story of "Xinting". Jing Gong: An honorific title given to Wang Anshi because he was granted the title Duke of Jing. Research: The title of the chapter in "The Book of Songs: Wei Feng" sings about the free life among the mountains and rivers, and expresses the praise of the hermit. Tianjin Bridge: In the ancient capital of Luoyang, it is a symbol of history and culture. Passengers on Tianjin Bridge mostly refer to courtiers. The purpose of this article is that Wang Anshi lived in seclusion in Zhongshan in his later years and was still worried about state affairs.
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587:
《覆舟山》
六代兴亡貉一丘，繁华梦逐水东流。
操蛇神向山前笑，三百年来几覆舟。
《新亭》
青山四合绕天津，风景依然似洛滨。
江左于今成乐土，新亭垂泪亦无人。
《王荆公书堂》
致君尧舜事何难，投老钟山赋考架。
愁杀天津桥上客，杜鹃声里两眉攒。

works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: Bulguksa Temple: This poem retains the historical data about Bulguksa Temple, and the poem also has a Zen meaning. Mengbiyi: Wentong is the calligraphy name of Jiang Yan, a writer of the Southern Dynasties. "Hate Fu" and "Farewell Fu" were all written by him. The idioms "Mengbi produces flowers" and "Jiang Lang's talents are exhausted" are all legends about him. The latter story took place in the post house in Yecheng. Jiang Yan dreamed that an immortal who called himself Guo Pu wanted to take back the colored pen given to him in the early years. From then on, his writing was not as good as before. The author said, "I am not as talented as Jiang, but I need to search for the source." The insight is profound and he hits the nail on the head. Xinxin: Duke Yang Zhongxiang, whose name was Bangyi and whose courtesy name was Xiji, was from Jishui (now Ji'an, Jiangxi Province). In the third year of Jianyan's reign in the Song Dynasty (1129), Jin soldiers invaded Jiankang. At that time, he served as the general magistrate of Jiankang Prefecture. He was captured because Du Chong, who stayed behind, defected. The state refused to surrender. He wrote in blood, "I would rather be a ghost of the Zhao family than a minister of another state." Wanyan Zongbi ordered The man had his heart scraped from his abdomen at the age of 44. In memory of his loyalty to the country, Emperor Gaozong Zhao gave him the posthumous title of "Zhongxiang" and built a tomb, a temple and a stele for him. Today, the memorial site is located on the mountain behind the "Second Spring in the South of the Yangtze River" behind Dong Gang of Yuhuatai. There is also the "Er Zhong Temple" built in Yuhuatai to commemorate him and his fellow countryman Wen Xinguo Gong (Wen Tianxiang). Tangshan: Tangshan is known as "Hot Spring" in ancient times and is named after its hot springs. According to some data, it has been designated as a royal hot spring since the Xiaoliang period of the Southern Dynasties. It has a history of more than 1,500 years and is one of the four major natural hot springs in the country. This poem of five unique poems personifies "hot springs" and says "this mountain has hot intestines". It is a strange imagination and full of fun.
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587:
佛国寺
在太平门外板仓，古华藏庵。
佛不可思议，莫问寺小大。
国在寺之中，亦在寺之外。
梦笔驿
在冶城。
才如江文通，也惜梦中笔。
我才不如江，但向源头索。
剖心里
在长干里，宋时金人入建康，杨忠襄公邦乂不屈，金人剖腹，取其心。
杨公剖心处，心赤血同赤。
至今墓上草，红心无断绝。
汤山
氤氲气满野，泉水流汤汤。
或者通地肺，此山有热肠。

works.\u65f6\u671f: Northern Song Dynasty; Southern Song Dynasty
works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: Lu You was appointed general magistrate of Kuizhou (now Fengjie, Chongqing). He left his hometown with his family in early summer and set off for Sichuan. He passed through Jiankang and stayed for five days, visiting places of interest.
works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: The earliest travelogue about Qingliang Mountain was written in the form of a diary in "Entering Shu".

Lu Fangweng, 46 years old, entered the river from the canal, then went west along the Yangtze River, passing through present-day Jiangsu, Anhui, Jiangxi, Hubei, Hunan and other provinces, passing through the Three Gorges with steep mountains and rapid waves, and arrived in Sichuan. Along the way, he enjoyed the sights of mountains and rivers, paid homage to historical sites, and inspected customs and sentiments. He recorded all his experiences on this journey in the prose "Entering Shu" written in a diary style.

Later generations said that my sister-in-law was the third sister of Jiang Ziwen, the Moling captain in the late Han Dynasty. Ziwen was injured and died during the battle. Wu Sunquan once built a temple for him in Zhongshan, and my sister-in-law was also worshiped as a god. Qingxi originates from Zhongshan Mountain. There is a saying in the song "where my sister-in-law lives, she is alone without a husband", which is the origin of the idiom "my sister-in-law lives alone" in later generations. The story takes place near Sixiang Bridge in Xianbaixia District.
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587:
【版本一】
开门白水，侧近桥梁，
小姑所居，独处无郞。
【版本二】
日暮风吹，叶落依枝。
丹心寸意，愁君未知。

据阜高台俯井闾，讲经因以得名诸？维摩已是为糟粕，梁武真成拾唾余。

works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: Yuhuatai is located in the south of Jinling City. According to legend, Master Yunguang gave lectures here during the reign of Emperor Wu of Liang Dynasty, which moved the gods and caused flowers to fall like rain. "The Lotus Sutra" says: "The Buddha taught the Dharma that mandala flowers rained down from the sky, so it was called the Rain Flower Terrace." This poem expresses the endless emotions of the past and present and the embrace of sorrow for the country through the description of the sunset scenery of the Rain Flower Terrace.
works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: The first couplet starts from the legend of Yuhuatai. There should have been a rain of flowers on the Yuhuatai, but when I came today, there was no rain of flowers to be found, only ancient moss everywhere. Comparing these two scenes, without any explanation, the rise and fall of Yuhuatai can be clearly seen, and the poet's sense of the past and present is also implicit in it. The couplet continues to mourn the past and hurt the present. "Qingfan" originally refers to the sound of monks chanting sutras. Here it means that the sound of Master Yunguang's lectures can no longer be heard. The sentence "The chaotic mountains still hold cups against each other" implicitly uses two Buddhist scriptures. One is that "The Biography of Gao Xian of Lotus Society" says that Master Daosheng came to Huqiu Mountain, gathered stones as disciples, and taught the "Nirvana Sutra", and all the stones nodded. The second one is found in the "Biography of Eminent Monks": "The cup-holder, whose name is unknown, often crosses the river in a wooden cup, because of his name." If you do not cultivate carefully and do not practice diligently, you will drink alcohol and eat meat, just like ordinary people. "The word "holding the cup" in the poem refers to this. Combining these two sentences, it means that the eminent monk like Yun Guang whose sermons can make Tianyuhua no longer exists, so these stubborn rocks in the mountains can only face each other. Laity monks like Beidu are still a pile of lifeless rocks in the mountains, expressing their great regret that Yuhuatai is not what it used to be. And lamenting Yuhuatai actually reflects the declining situation of the Ming Dynasty. From the description of the scene below It can be seen more clearly in the middle. The neck couplet describes the scenery seen from a distance. "Crawling grass" means creeping grass. "Crawling grass goes to the horizon" means that the creeping grass of Yuhuatai extends to far away places, implying that desolation like Yuhuatai is everywhere. The scene. The poet added the word "Six Dynasties" before "Mancao", and the metaphor became clearer. The Six Dynasties were all short-term dynasties that established their capitals in Jinling and perished one after another. Needless to say, the poet was worried about state affairs. "Wanli Yangtze River" "Sitting down", Yuhuatai was originally a platform dune formed by the sedimentation of the ancient Yangtze River. It is still very close to the Yangtze River, so you can naturally see it from the stage. "Sitting" refers to the place where Master Yunguang originally lectured, that is Refers to the Yuhuatai. "The Yangtze River of thousands of miles has come down", reading it seems to feel the momentum of the Yangtze River rolling in, shaking the Yuhuatai. It is like Du Fu's "I suspect it is coming from Kongtong, fearing that it will touch the sky pillar and break" in "From Beijing to Fengxian County" The worries of "Yong Huai Five Hundred Words". These two lines have a desolate and majestic state, and the sigh is far away. The last couplet is two lines, and the upper neck couplet writes the dusk scene of Changqian to express the sigh of current events. Changqian, under the rain flower terrace. "Wu Du Fu" "Note on "Changgan Continuation": "There are hills (i.e. Yuhuatai) five miles south of Jianye, with flat land in between, where officials and people live together. There are big and small Changgan in the Changgan, all connected. "Jiagu" is a musical instrument used in the ancient army to record time. During the reign of Emperor Wuzong of the Ming Dynasty, Ning Wang Chenhao rebelled against Nanchang and was captured. After that, Jinling and other places strengthened their military preparedness, so there was the sound of Jiagu in Changqian at dusk. The sentence starts from the distance and looks back at the near distance. The first sentence uses the sound of "笳 drum" to cover the desolate atmosphere and hints at the emergency of the military situation; the second sentence uses the bright lights of the "tower" to describe the drunken life, dreams and death of the nobles. The contrast is sharp and ironic. strong.
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587:
雨花台上无花雨，此日登临踏古苔。
清梵不闻重说法，乱山依旧对衔杯。
六朝蔓草天边去，万里长江席下来。
回首长干笳鼓动，城南灯火映楼台。

works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: This poem describes the spectacular scenery of Yuhuatai. "The king's qi has completely disappeared, and the mountain shape is still gloomy." This means that although the king's qi of Jinling has completely disappeared, the atmosphere of the famous mountain is still the same as before.
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587:
行尽长干道，崇台得壮观。
秋天多雨势，江上更风寒。
王气全消歇，山形尚郁盘。
松楸弥望处，寂寞葬千官。

铁血旗翻扫虏尘，神州如晦一时新。
雨花台下添新泪，白骨青磷旧党人。

works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: When Zhou Bangyan was in charge of the Lishui Order, he visited Wuxiang Mountain in the summer and wrote the poem "Man Ting Fang·Wuxiang Mountain in Lishui in Summer" to express his feelings about life.
works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: The first three sentences of the first film of the work describe the early summer scenery in extremely delicate detail, full of fresh and wild beauty. The poet in the next piece compares himself to Sheyan, who has been drifting in the officialdom for many years and temporarily lives here in Lishui. For the time being, he did not think about things outside his own body, but temporarily used wine to drown his sorrows, seeking joy in banquets, and wrote about the helpless depression and depression of drinking to drown his sorrows.
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587:
风老莺雏，雨肥梅子，午阴嘉树清圆。地卑山近，衣润费炉烟。人静乌鸢自乐，小桥外、新绿溅溅。凭栏久，黄芦苦竹，拟泛九江船。
年年，如社燕，漂流瀚海，来寄修椽。且莫思身外，长近尊前。憔悴江南倦客，不堪听、急管繁弦。歌筵畔，先安簟枕，容我醉时眠。

年年，如社燕，漂流瀚海，来寄修椽。且莫思身外，长近尊前。憔悴江南倦客，不堪听、急管繁弦。歌筵畔，先安簟枕，容我醉时眠。
晨登瓦官阁，极眺金陵城。钟山对北户，淮水入南荣。漫漫雨花落，嘈嘈天乐鸣。两廊振法鼓，四角吟风筝。杳出霄汉上，仰攀日月行。山空霸气灭，地古寒阴生。寥廓云海晚，苍茫宫观平。门馀阊阖字，楼识凤凰名。雷作百山动，神扶万栱倾。灵光何足贵，长此镇吴京。
晨登瓦官阁，极眺金陵城。钟山对北户，淮水入南荣。漫漫雨花落，嘈嘈天乐鸣。两廊振法鼓，四角吟风筝。杳出霄汉上，仰攀日月行。山空霸气灭，地古寒阴生。寥廓云海晚，苍茫宫观平。门馀阊阖字，楼识凤凰名。雷作百山动，神扶万栱倾。灵光何足贵，长此镇吴京。
晨登瓦官阁，极眺金陵城。钟山对北户，淮水入南荣。漫漫雨花落，嘈嘈天乐鸣。两廊振法鼓，四角吟风筝。杳出霄汉上，仰攀日月行。山空霸气灭，地古寒阴生。寥廓云海晚，苍茫宫观平。门馀阊阖字，楼识凤凰名。雷作百山动，神扶万栱倾。灵光何足贵，长此镇吴京。
绝壁寒云外，孤亭落照间。六朝流水急，终古白鸥闲。树暗江城雨，天青吴楚山。矶头谁把钓，向夕未知还。

works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: It reproduces the scenery of Yanziji, Nanjing.

works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: This poem was written by the author during his trip to Jinling. Jinling was the ancient capital of the Six Dynasties where cultural relics flourished. After cultivation during the Sui and Tang Dynasties and reconstruction during the Five Dynasties, Jinling once again emerged as a metropolis in the south. Tourists looking around the mountains and rivers of their homeland here can easily feel the emotion of things changing and people changing. "Jinling Nostalgia" has therefore become a long-lasting theme in Chinese classical literature. The author has studied Chinese classics for many years and has been deeply influenced by Han culture. When he was in Jinling, he naturally resonated emotionally and created this nostalgic masterpiece among Japanese Chinese poems.
works.\u76f8\u5173\u4f5c\u54c1: 《文明轩杂谈》、《中岩和尚语录》、《东海一沤集》、《中正子》
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587:
人物频迁地未磨，六朝咸破有山河。
金华旧址商渔宅，玉树残声樵牧歌。
列壑云连常带雨，大江风定尚生波。
当年佳丽今何在，远客苍茫感慨多。

works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: The characteristic of this poem is that it does not feel desolate because Jinling is no longer the capital, but also affirms Jinling's contribution to the development of southern culture, which is superior to those poems that only focus on the mountains and rivers.
works.\u76f8\u5173\u4e8b\u4ef6: The legend of Qin Shihuang’s southern tour to eliminate the aura of the King of Jinling
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587: 飞楼阁水飞霞，冷落春城宿乳鸦。天阙双峰铦似戟，海门高浪白如花。?空劳玉辇销王气，曾划长江作帝家。五马渡边龙化后，南方文物掩中华。

寥落寒云蔽旧京，歌残《玉树》听凄清。并无铁锁沉天堑，遽见金舆出石城。
一马尚能龙变化，千门谁使草纵横？乌衣巷陌寻常在，可是夷吾浪得名？

works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: In the winter of the second year of Baoli (826), Liu Yuxi returned to Luoyang from Hezhou and passed through Jinling.
works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: The first half of the poem describes the scenery seen and points out the scenic spots and historic sites in Jinling related to the Six Dynasties to hint at the reasons for the rise and fall of the ages; the second half uses discussion and emotion to use the past to satirize the present, revealing the main theme of the whole poem.
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587: 潮满冶城渚，日斜征虏亭。蔡洲新草绿，幕府旧烟青。兴废由人事，山川空地形。后庭花一曲，幽怨不堪听。

works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587: 潮满冶城渚，日斜征虏亭。蔡洲新草绿，幕府旧烟青。兴废由人事，山川空地形。后庭花一曲，幽怨不堪听。

晋末英雄起，神器沦荒服。胡月蚀中原，白日升旸谷。
金陵实形胜，关山固重复。巨壑隍北壖，长江堑西隩。
凿山拟嵩华，穿地象伊谷。草昧席罗图，荜路戴黄屋。
一时因地险，五世享天禄。礼乐何煌煌，文章纷郁郁。
多士春林秀，作颂清风穆。出入三百年，朝事几翻覆。
搀抢如云勃，鲸鲵旋自曝。倦闻金鼎移，骤睹灵龟卜。
吁嗟王气尽，坐悲天运倏。天道何茫茫，善淫乃相复。
行路偏衣半，遂亡大梁族。日隐汀洲上，登舻[舟鹿]川陆。
月回吴山树，风闻楚江鹄。因依兰蕙丛，采襭不盈掬。
栖霞高岭翠烟凝，玄武湖光似镜澄。古渡远迎兰桨妓，名山深闭瓦棺僧。藻苹岁岁功臣庙，松桧葱葱太祖陵。欲问六朝何处所，都人唯解说龙兴。
恃险不种德，兴亡叹数穷。石城几换主，天堑谩连空。御路叠民冢，台基聚牧童。折碑犹有字，多记晋英雄。

黄道天清拥珮珂，东南王气秣陵多。
江吞彭蠡来三蜀，地接昆仑带九河。
凤阙晓霞红散绮，龙池春水绿生波。
华夷混一归真主，端拱无为乐太和。
伯业随流水，寒芜上古城。长空横海色，断岸落潮声。八极悲扶拄，五湖来止倾。东南天子气，扫地入函京。

works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: The high-rise building is majestic and desolate. The predecessors commented: "The mountains are there, the tide is there, and the moon is there, but the Six Kingdoms are not there, and the empty city ears also hurt the ancient times ("Tang Yin Gui Sign")."
works.\u76f8\u5173\u4f5c\u54c1: 《金陵五题》、《西塞山怀古》、《金陵怀古》
works.\u76f8\u5173\u4e8b\u4ef6: Liu Yuxi was appointed governor of Hezhou
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587: 山围故国周遭在，潮打空城寂寞回。淮水东边旧时月，夜深还过女墙来。

works.\u76f8\u5173\u4f5c\u54c1: 《金陵五题》、《西塞山怀古》、《金陵怀古》
works.\u76f8\u5173\u4e8b\u4ef6: Liu Yuxi was appointed governor of Hezhou
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587: 山围故国周遭在，潮打空城寂寞回。淮水东边旧时月，夜深还过女墙来。
石头城上小徘徊，世换僧残寺已灰。地接汴淮山北去，江吞吴越水东来。健鱼奋鬣随蛟舞，快鹘翻身猎雁回。一片降旗千古泪，前人留与后人哀。

works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: This book takes the Baili Qinhuai River as the main line, from the origin to the river mouth. It records in detail the celebrities and beauties of the past dynasties, as well as interesting anecdotes, etc. in the Qinhuai River Basin, especially on both sides of the Qinhuai River in the Ten Miles.
By singing the osmanthus tree, it expresses the noble character that never fades in the cold weather.
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587: 洞庭水上一株桐，经霜触浪困严风。昔时抽心曜白日，今旦卧死黄沙中。洛阳名工见咨嗟，一翦一刻作琵琶。白璧规心学明月，珊瑚映面作风花。帝王见赏不见忘，提携把握登建章。掩抑摧藏张女弹，殷勤促柱楚明光。年年月月对君王，遥遥夜夜宿未央。未央彩女弃鸣篪，争先拂拭生光仪。茱萸锦衣玉作匣，安念昔日枯树枝。不学衡山南岭桂，至今千载犹未知。青琐门外安石榴，连枝接叶夹御沟。金墉城西合欢树，垂条照彩拂凤楼。游侠少年游上路，倾心颠倒相恋慕。摩顶至足买片言，开胸沥胆取一顾。自言家在赵邯郸，翩翩舌杪复剑端。青骊白驳的卢马，金羁绿控紫丝鞶。躞蹀横行不止进，夜夜汗血至长安。长安城中诸贵臣，争贵儒者席上珍。复闻梁王好学问，轻弃剑客如埃尘。吾丘寿王始得意，司马相如适被申。大才大辩尚如此，何况我辈轻薄人。君不见西陵田，从横十字成陌阡君不见东郊道，荒凉芜没起寒烟。尽是昔日帝王处，歌姬舞女达天曙。今日翩妍少年子，不知华盛落前去。吐心吐气许他人，今旦回惑生犹豫。山中桂树自有枝，心中方寸自相知。何言岁月忽若驰，君之情意与我离。还君玳瑁金雀钗，不忍见此便心危。君不见上林苑中客，冰罗雾縠象牙席。尽是得意忘言者，探肠见胆无所惜。白酒甜盐甘如乳，绿觞皎镜华如碧。少年持名不肯尝，安知白驹应过隙。博山炉中百和香，郁金苏合及都梁。逶迤好气佳容貌，经过青琐历紫房。已入中山冯后帐，复上皇帝班姬床。班姬失宠颜不开，奉帚供养长信台。日暮耿耿不能寐，秋风切切四面来。玉阶行路生细草，金炉香炭变成灰。得意失意须臾顷，非君方寸逆所裁。

works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: By singing the osmanthus tree, it expresses the noble character that never fades in the cold weather.
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587: 洞庭水上一株桐，经霜触浪困严风。昔时抽心曜白日，今旦卧死黄沙中。洛阳名工见咨嗟，一翦一刻作琵琶。白璧规心学明月，珊瑚映面作风花。帝王见赏不见忘，提携把握登建章。掩抑摧藏张女弹，殷勤促柱楚明光。年年月月对君王，遥遥夜夜宿未央。未央彩女弃鸣篪，争先拂拭生光仪。茱萸锦衣玉作匣，安念昔日枯树枝。不学衡山南岭桂，至今千载犹未知。青琐门外安石榴，连枝接叶夹御沟。金墉城西合欢树，垂条照彩拂凤楼。游侠少年游上路，倾心颠倒相恋慕。摩顶至足买片言，开胸沥胆取一顾。自言家在赵邯郸，翩翩舌杪复剑端。青骊白驳的卢马，金羁绿控紫丝鞶。躞蹀横行不止进，夜夜汗血至长安。长安城中诸贵臣，争贵儒者席上珍。复闻梁王好学问，轻弃剑客如埃尘。吾丘寿王始得意，司马相如适被申。大才大辩尚如此，何况我辈轻薄人。君不见西陵田，从横十字成陌阡君不见东郊道，荒凉芜没起寒烟。尽是昔日帝王处，歌姬舞女达天曙。今日翩妍少年子，不知华盛落前去。吐心吐气许他人，今旦回惑生犹豫。山中桂树自有枝，心中方寸自相知。何言岁月忽若驰，君之情意与我离。还君玳瑁金雀钗，不忍见此便心危。君不见上林苑中客，冰罗雾縠象牙席。尽是得意忘言者，探肠见胆无所惜。白酒甜盐甘如乳，绿觞皎镜华如碧。少年持名不肯尝，安知白驹应过隙。博山炉中百和香，郁金苏合及都梁。逶迤好气佳容貌，经过青琐历紫房。已入中山冯后帐，复上皇帝班姬床。班姬失宠颜不开，奉帚供养长信台。日暮耿耿不能寐，秋风切切四面来。玉阶行路生细草，金炉香炭变成灰。得意失意须臾顷，非君方寸逆所裁。

works.\u65f6\u671f: Late Ming Dynasty; Early Qing Dynasty

works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587: 到郡席不暖，居民空惘然。好山无十里，遗恨恐他年。欲款南朝寺，同登北郭船。朱门收画戟，绀宇出青莲。夹路苍髯古，迎人翠麓偏。龙腰蟠故国，鸟爪寄层巅。竹杪飞华屋，松根泫细泉。峰多巧障日，江远欲浮天。略彴横秋水，浮图插暮烟。归来踏人影，云细月娟娟。

works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: Patriots in the early Southern Song Dynasty all valued the geographical advantages of Jinling's scenic spot for military offense and defense in the Southern Song Dynasty to cheer up the Southern Song Dynasty.
works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: "Pillow on Zhongfu, Lake Xuanwu. This tiger is born, really squatting. Look at the four mountains surrounding it, and rest next to Jiangzhu. It's ridiculous that the Tang Dynasty people didn't care about it, but they said that this tiger is flying across the waves." Full of confidence to regain lost ground.
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587: 颇恨登临，浪自作、骚人愁语。石城上、何须苦说，死袁生褚。当日卧龙商略处，秦淮王气真何许。与君来、萧瑟北风寒，黄云暮。枕钟阜，湖玄武。生此虎，真蹲踞。看四山环合，休临江渚。可笑唐人无意度，却言此虎凌波去。君且住、明月为人来，潮生浦。
二水中分，浮一点、钟山翠尖。芦苇夹、趁风移过，白舫青帘。老鹳飞边矶觜凹，苍云没处瓦亭嵌。望黄天荡里石邮狂，无数帆。
二水中分，浮一点、钟山翠尖。芦苇夹、趁风移过，白舫青帘。老鹳飞边矶觜凹，苍云没处瓦亭嵌。望黄天荡里石邮狂，无数帆。
清溪有桃叶，流水载佳人。名以王郎久，花犹古渡新。楫摇秦代月，枝带晋时春。莫谓供凭览，因之可结邻。

works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: This poem describes the beautiful scenery of Taoye Ferry in those days, with green locust trees and tall willows, blurred grass, a bay of flowing water, and a group of orioles chirping, making it appear beautiful and quiet.
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587: 桃叶渡前芳草迷，绿槐高柳暗东西。停舟日暮行人尽，流水一湾莺乱啼。

works.\u65f6\u671f: Northern Song Dynasty; Southern Song Dynasty
works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: Zhao Mingcheng died of illness in Jinling, and Li Qingzhao created "Linjiang Immortal".
works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: It vividly shows the desolate and desolate inner world after the death of the husband.
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587: 庭院深深深几许，云窗雾阁常扃。柳梢梅萼渐分明。春归秣陵树，人客远安城。感月吟风多少事，如今老去无成。谁怜憔悴更凋零。试灯无意思，踏雪没心情。

聊追邺城友，躧步出兰宫。
法侣殊人世，天花异俗中。
鸟声不测处，松吟未觉风。
此时超爱网，还复洗尘蒙。
凭高一览。紫翠相围光照眼。下瞰平湖。鹭立鸥飞意自如。天清境胜。云澹烟横鱼动镜。不是公诗。谁写新亭一段奇。

works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: Deng Jiankang Shangxin Pavilion was left behind on Shizhidao.
works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: It evokes the nostalgia of the past and expresses the leisurely sorrow of Qianhu, "I want to die in service to my country, but there is no battlefield."
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587: 我来吊古，上危楼、赢得闲愁千斛。虎踞龙蟠何处是，只有兴亡满目。柳外斜阳，水边归鸟，陇上吹乔木。片帆西去，一声谁喷霜竹。却忆安石风流，东山岁晚，泪落哀筝曲。儿辈功名都付与，长日惟消棋局。宝镜难寻，碧云将暮，谁劝杯中绿。江头风怒，朝来波浪翻屋。

六朝人物，真可惜、都向愁中错过。今日烟花犹道是，千古繁华未破。画舫箫声，青溪蟾影，夜夜知怜我。中元到也，盂兰盆会灯火。
今年秋暑偏迟，苎衫浑未脱，馀炎难躲。夜半西风初起处，吹出红莲万朵。人尽贪痴，鬼无形影，岂亦啼寒饿。三更水榭，卷帘看月斜堕。
逶迤曲巷，在春城斜角，绿杨荫里。赭白青黄墙砌石，门映碧溪流水。细雨饧箫，斜阳牧笛，一径穿桃李。风吹花落，落花风又吹起。更兼处处缲车，家家社燕，江介风光美。四月樱桃红满市，雪片鲥鱼刀。淮水秋清，钟山暮紫，老马耕闲地。一丘一壑，吾将终老于此。
逶迤曲巷，在春城斜角，绿杨荫里。赭白青黄墙砌石，门映碧溪流水。细雨饧箫，斜阳牧笛，一径穿桃李。风吹花落，落花风又吹起。更兼处处缲车，家家社燕，江介风光美。四月樱桃红满市，雪片鲥鱼刀。淮水秋清，钟山暮紫，老马耕闲地。一丘一壑，吾将终老于此。

works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: This poem was written in the spring of the seventh year of Yuanfeng in the Song Dynasty (1084), when Huang Tingjian passed through Jinling and Yangzhou to take office in Deping Town, Dezhou. Wang Anshi once wrote a collection of poems "Shi Yuan Ferry" when he was living in Banshan Garden in the fifth year of Yuanfeng in the Song Dynasty (1082). The poet once criticized Wang Anshi's poem as "the clothes of a hundred families" and thought it was "just a smile" (see the first collection of "Tiaoxi Yuyincong Hua"). Later, he followed Wang Anshi's example and wrote the lyrics of this poem.
works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: "Bodhisattva Man: Half Smoke and Half Rain by the Bridge of the Stream" is a poem written by Huang Tingjian, a poet of the Northern Song Dynasty. This is a collection of poems. The first part of the poem depicts a picture of fishing in the wild at the bridge, showing the subtle pleasure of the fisherman's blending with nature, and the second part describes the beautiful scenery of the river and mountains. This poem follows Wang Anshi's example and cleverly adopts the writing technique of grafting ideas from the past. It collects a few lines of poems from Tang Dynasty poet Du Fu through delicate writing style, describing the natural beauty such as mountains, rivers, rivers and bridges, and expressing the poet's desire to follow in the footsteps of his sages and hide his traces. determination.
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587:
王荆公新筑草堂于半山，引八功德水作小港，其上垒石作桥，为集句云：“数间茅屋闲临水，窄衫短帽垂杨里。花是去年红，吹开一夜风。梢梢新月偃，午醉醒来晚。何物最关情，黄鹂三两声。”戏效荆公作。
半烟半雨溪桥畔，渔翁醉着无人唤。疏懒意何长，春风花草香。
江山如有待，此意陶潜解。问我去何之，君行到自知。
轻舟夜剪秋江，西风鳞甲生江面。瓦官阁下，方山亭外，惊涛雪片。一带蒋州，千寻铁锁，等閒烧断。只波间皓月，流光欲下，旧曾照，金陵县。何处回帆挝鼓，更玉笛、数声哀怨。回思旧事，永嘉南渡，流人何限。如此江山，几人怜惜，斜阳断岸。正江南烟水，濛濛飞尽，楚天新雁。
轻舟夜剪秋江，西风鳞甲生江面。瓦官阁下，方山亭外，惊涛雪片。一带蒋州，千寻铁锁，等閒烧断。只波间皓月，流光欲下，旧曾照，金陵县。何处回帆挝鼓，更玉笛、数声哀怨。回思旧事，永嘉南渡，流人何限。如此江山，几人怜惜，斜阳断岸。正江南烟水，濛濛飞尽，楚天新雁。
暮云低锁荒台，凭阑四望天垂地。曼花半夜，蜚香缭绕，昔人曾记。往事悠悠，物华非旧，江山仍丽。怅斜阳芳草，长安不见，谁共洒、新亭泪。开放青峦旧址。动骚人、一番词意。青油幕里，相忘鱼鸟，水边云际。却恨清游，未能追逐，区区僚底。问何时，脱了尘埃墨绶，为虚斋醉。

works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: Liu Kezhuang, who lived in the late Southern Song Dynasty, faced the harsh political reality of the fall of the Northern Territory and the Southern Song Dynasty rulers who settled on the left side of the Yangtze River with no hope of recovery. He expressed the sorrow of the times by chanting history and remembering the past.
works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: Looking back at the present and recalling the past, touching the scene with sadness, reveals a deep sigh of the rise and fall of the country.
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587:
塔庙当年甲一方，千层金碧万缁郎。
开山佛已成胡鬼，住院僧犹说李王。
遗像有尘龛坏壁，断碑无首立斜阳。
惟应驻马坡头月，曾见金舆夜纳凉。

works.\u65f6\u671f: Late Ming Dynasty; Early Qing Dynasty

works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587: 白寺荒湾略舣舟，携筇来作上方游。何年巧匠开山骨，自古精兵聚石头。故垒无人空向久，高堂问话凛生秋。云庵快望穷千里，一借澄江洗客忧。

works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: Under the title of the original poem, there is a small preface: "At that time, I was called to suppress the left soldiers." In the spring of the first year of Hongguang (1645), the Qing army was approaching the Jianghuai River, but the Nanming general Zuo Liangyu led hundreds of thousands of troops from Wuhan eastward to attack Nanjing in the name of "Qing Jun side, except Ma Ruan". Ma Shiying, who was in power, ordered Shi Kefa in Jiangbei to withdraw his troops to prevent Zuo Liangyu. Shi Kefa had to send reinforcements to Yanziji, leaving Huaihe defense empty. Later, Zuo Liangyu was defeated by Huang Degong and died of vomiting blood. His son Zuo Mengeng led his tribe to surrender to the Qing Dynasty. Shi Kefa was ordered to return north. At this time, Xuyi surrendered to the Qing Dynasty and the city of Sizhou fell. Shi Kefa then went to Yangzhou and fought desperately to resist the Qing army's attack. Judging from this historical fact, "Yanzijikou Zhan" should have been written when Shi Kefa's "human aid" arrived in Nanjing at Yanziji.
works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: The military situation is extremely urgent, and Nanming is troubled internally and externally. The whole family was in the city, and Shi Kefa, who was so close that he could not return, climbed to Yanzijitou. Faced with the critical situation of war, he leaned over the river and sighed with emotion. This five-character quatrain improvised by Wanduan not only expresses his feelings for The deep thoughts of his mother and family reflect his deep sense of justice and courage to devote himself to the country at a critical moment. It is said that "men don't shed tears easily", but the line in the poem "the clear tears shed on the sandstone, and the drops sink to the bottom of the river" is so shocking!
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587:
来家不面母，咫尺犹千里。
矶头洒清泪，滴滴沉江底。

works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: The place sung by "Wuyi Lane" got its name because it was the Wuyi Camp in the Wu Dynasty. After the Jin Dynasty moved eastward, the famous clans lived together for a while. Now, "the swallows are still here, and the kings are scattered, and it has become ordinary." The common people are filled with endless emotions and their pens are extremely curved."

works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: Du Mu was very concerned about politics in the early days and expressed concern about the Tang Dynasty, which was riddled with holes at that time. He saw the decadence and mediocrity of the ruling group, the self-defense of the vassal towns, and the frequent border troubles. He felt deeply that social crises were everywhere, and the future of the Tang Dynasty was pathetic. This kind of sorrowful thought prompted him to write many poems with practical significance. "Bo Qinhuai" was produced on the basis of this idea.
works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: "Po Qinhuai" writes both reality and history. It writes history based on reality. The poem describes the scenery he saw while mooring at Qinhuai at night, lamenting the fall of the previous dynasty and the country. It contains a concise and bitter satire on the behavior of the rulers of the late Tang Dynasty who indulged in sex and lived in dreams. There are only four sentences in the whole poem, but it integrates description of scenery, discussion and lyricism, and it is very natural.
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587: 烟笼寒水月笼沙，夜泊秦淮近酒家。商女不知亡国恨，隔江犹唱后庭花。

works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: Yuan Mei's "Hongwu Big Stone Stele Song" is the earliest poetic work in history to describe and express this "historical facts of the early Ming Dynasty" and "the wonders of Yangshan". From its scale, momentum, connotation, theme and historical value, it is well-deserved. "Inscriptions" and "Epics". The poet uses a song style and a poetic style that is both natural and simple, heroic and vigorous. He uses a large stroke of wine and crisscrosses it. First, he expresses the surprise of the Yangshan stele "cutting straight through the strange peaks into a sitting position. He is lying down even if he wants to bear the burden". The world image and "unfinished mission" were announced to the world, and then the historical facts of "the imperial edict exacting responsibilities" and "cutting off the heroes" were written down. In the chant that travels through time and space and surveys the past and present, the current situation of the Yangshan stele and the rich imagination caused by it are also carefully carved; finally, in the words "the material will not be collected in the afterlife, this stele will be empty for thousands of years" and "the majestic The passage is like a light smoke. There are only three to five feet of Maoci soil steps." The whole article ends with the exclamation. It can be said that it is a masterpiece of poetry that matches and complements the "Yangshan Stele". It is majestic, both spiritual and physical, but also meaningful and thought-provoking.
works.\u76f8\u5173\u4f5c\u54c1: 《小仓山房诗文集》、《随园诗话》、《随园食单》、《子不语》
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587:
青龙山前石一方，弓尺量之十丈长，两头未截空中央。
旁有屃赑形更大，直斩奇峰为一坐，欲负不负身尚卧。
相传高皇开创气概雄，欲移此碑陵寝中。
大书功德告祖宗，压倒唐汉惊羲农。
碑如长剑青天倚，十万骆驼拉不起。
诏书切责下欧刀，工匠虞衡井中死。
芟刈群雄苔八荒，一拳顽石敢如此。
周颠仙人大笑来，天威到此几穷哉!
但赦青山留太朴，胜扶赤子上春台。
丁丁从此亭开凿，夜深无复山灵哭。
牧竖宵眠五十牛，村氓尽晒三千谷。
材大由来世莫收，此碑千载空悠悠。
昭陵石马无能战，汉代铜仙泪不流。
吁嗟呼!君不见，项王拔，始皇鞭，山石何尝不可迁?
威风一过如轻烟。惟有茅茨土阶三五尺，至今神功圣德高于天。

碑如长剑青天倚，十万骆驼拉不起。
诏书切责下欧刀，工匠虞衡井中死。
芟刈群雄苔八荒，一拳顽石敢如此。
周颠仙人大笑来，天威到此几穷哉!
但赦青山留太朴，胜扶赤子上春台。
丁丁从此亭开凿，夜深无复山灵哭。
牧竖宵眠五十牛，村氓尽晒三千谷。
材大由来世莫收，此碑千载空悠悠。
昭陵石马无能战，汉代铜仙泪不流。
吁嗟呼!君不见，项王拔，始皇鞭，山石何尝不可迁?
威风一过如轻烟。惟有茅茨土阶三五尺，至今神功圣德高于天。

works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: This is a song about parting and sad, describing the true love for a beautiful and moving woman, showing a scene of attachment and attachment that cannot bear to be apart. The different place is on the Jinling tidal ditch. The tide ditch is now unknown in Nanjing, but before the Song Dynasty, it was an important water transportation route second only to the Qinhuai River. The ditch was very lively with lights, shadows, paddles, and boats going back and forth. According to legend, this ditch was dug during the Chiwu period of Emperor Wu of the Three Kingdoms to divert the tide of the river, reaching Qingxi in the east, Yundu in the west, and Houhu in the north.
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587:
粉艳明，秋水盈。柳样纤柔花样轻，笑前双靥生。
寒江平，江橹鸣。谁道潮沟非远行，回头千里情。

其一
君家何处住？妾住在横塘。停船暂借问，或恐是同乡。
其二
家临九江水，来去九江侧。同是长干人，自小不相识。
其三
下渚多风浪，莲舟渐觉稀。那能不相待？独自逆潮归。
其四
三江潮水急，五湖风浪涌。由来花性轻，莫畏莲舟重。

其一
君家何处住？妾住在横塘。停船暂借问，或恐是同乡。
其二
家临九江水，来去九江侧。同是长干人，自小不相识。

works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: This poem was written in the second year of Hongwu (1369), the first year of the Ming Dynasty, not long after the founding of the Ming Dynasty. The author was born in the late Yuan Dynasty and early Ming Dynasty, and suffered from war and chaos. At that time, the poet was drafted to participate in the compilation of "History of the Yuan Dynasty". He had ideals and wanted to do something for the country. When he climbed up to the Rain Flower Terrace in Jinling and looked at the Yangtze River shrouded in smoke and sunset, he followed the ups and downs of the river's waves, his thoughts and feelings, and he composed the poem with emotion.
works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: "Climbing the Rain Flower Terrace in Jinling and Looking at the River" is a seven-character ancient poem written by Gao Qi, a poet of the Ming Dynasty. This poem is a hymn for the reunification of the motherland. The author climbed to the Rain Flower Terrace in Jinling and looked at the Yangtze River rolling eastward. He couldn't help but be moved by the scene, remembering the past and thinking about the present. He wrote this poem to express his yearning for the unification of the country and the peace of the world. The first eight sentences describe the scenery seen when looking from the platform, the next four sentences describe one's nostalgia for the past, the next eight sentences describe the many wars that took place in this place from the Three Kingdoms to the Six Dynasties, and the last four sentences praise. Zhu Yuanzhang's new dynasty and ended with a prayer for peace. The whole poem has strong writing power, sonorous rhyme, easy scrolling and vertical and horizontal momentum.
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587:
大江来从万山中，山势尽与江流东。
钟山如龙独西上，欲破巨浪乘长风。
江山相雄不相让，形胜争夸天下壮。
秦皇空此瘗黄金，佳气葱葱至今王。
我怀郁塞何由开，酒酣走上城南台；
坐觉苍茫万古意，远自荒烟落日之中来！
石头城下涛声怒，武骑千群谁敢渡？
黄旗入洛竟何祥，铁锁横江未为固。
前三国，后六朝，草生宫阙何萧萧。
英雄乘时务割据，几度战血流寒潮。
我生幸逢圣人起南国，祸乱初平事休息。
从今四海永为家，不用长江限南北。

works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: After visiting his hometown of Chu, Li Bai continued his eastward journey and reached places such as Nanjing and Yangzhou in present-day Jiangsu, and Shaoxing in Zhejiang. Nanjing was once the ancient capital of six dynasties, known as the "beautiful land in the south of the Yangtze River and the imperial state of Jinling". Both sides of the Qinhuai River in the city have been prosperous commercial areas and residential areas since Soochow. Some businessmen go out on business and often do not return for several years, leaving their wives at home watching over them. I don’t know if Li Bai knew some business women, but he must have heard their stories. "Changganxing" is a miscellany of Yuefu songs from the Six Dynasties. "Changqian" means Changqianli, an alley south of Qinhuai River in Nanjing today. This name still exists. "Xing", song Xing, is a genre of ancient poetry. This poem draws on some expression methods of folk songs of the Six Dynasties, takes the love and parting of business women as the theme, and writes it in the voice of a woman's self-narration. In the narration of happy past events and parting emotions, she expresses her longing for her distant husband.
works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: "Two Poems on Changganxing" is a collection of poems by the great poet Li Bai of the Tang Dynasty, and was selected into "Three Hundred Tang Poems". These two love narrative poems are written through the voice of a business woman, describing her nostalgia for her husband who is away on business, reflecting the life and emotions of ancient business wives. The whole poem combines narrative, scenery and lyricism, with vivid and full images and deep style. Soft and graceful. [1] The first poem depicts various life aspects of a merchant woman at various stages of her life, showing vivid and vivid pictures, creating an artistic image of a merchant woman who persistently pursues and yearns for an ideal life; the third poem The two poems start with a business woman looking forward to her husband but not returning home, and go through the layers until she ends with self-pity and self-hate. They express her deep love and longing for her husband who is away on business. They are sad, resentful, and touching.
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587:
其一
妾发初覆额，折花门前剧。郎骑竹马来，绕床弄青梅。同居长干里，两小无嫌猜，十四为君妇，羞颜未尝开。低头向暗壁，千唤不一回。十五始展眉，愿同尘与灰。常存抱柱信，岂上望夫台。十六君远行，瞿塘滟滪堆。五月不可触，猿声天上哀。门前迟行迹，一一生绿苔。苔深不能扫，落叶秋风早。八月蝴蝶黄，双飞西园草。感此伤妾心，坐愁红颜老。早晚下三巴，预将书报家。相迎不道远，直至长风沙。
其二
忆妾深闺里，烟尘不曾识。嫁与长干人，沙头候风色。五月南风兴，思君下巴陵。八月西风起，想君发扬子。去来悲如何，见少离别多。昨夜狂风度，吹折江头树。湘潭几日到，妾梦越风波。淼淼暗无边，行人在何处。好乘浮云骢，佳期兰渚东。鸳鸯绿蒲上，翡翠锦屏中。自怜十五余，颜色桃花红。那作商人妇，愁水复愁风。

works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: Talented men in the Tang Dynasty were looking for flowers and willows, causing misfortune to many women.
works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: Expressing sympathy for women's misfortunes.
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587: 妾家白蘋浦，日上芙蓉楫。轧轧摇桨声，移舟入茭叶。溪长茭叶深，作底难相寻。避郎郎不见，鸂鶒自浮沉。拾萍萍无根，采莲莲有子。不作浮萍生，宁作藕花死。岸傍骑马郎，乌帽紫游缰。含愁复含笑，回首问横塘。妾住金陵步，门前朱雀航。流苏持作帐，芙蓉持作梁。出入金犊幰，兄弟侍中郎。前年学歌舞，定得郎相许。连娟眉绕山，依约腰如杵。凤管悲若咽，鸾弦娇欲语。扇薄露红铅，罗轻压金缕。明月西南楼，珠帘玳瑁钩。横波巧能笑，弯蛾不识愁。花开子留树，草长根依土。早闻金沟远，底事归郎许。不学杨白花，朝朝泪如雨。

works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: Talented men in the Tang Dynasty were looking for flowers and willows, causing misfortune to many women.
works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: Expressing sympathy for women's misfortunes.
