works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: Praise to Zhongshan as the most scenic spot in Jinling. Then, changing scenery step by step, we will introduce the scenic spots in Zhongshan one by one. The author looks at the smoke-shrouded Phoenix Tower, Bailuzhou, ordinary people's homes, the Forbidden City of the Six Dynasties, and the head of the Yangtze River. The feeling of the rise and fall of dynasties arises spontaneously. The former dragons and tigers, and the Jinling King's spirit suddenly disappear, which makes people feel sad.

works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: "A Journey to Zhongshan" is a prose written by Song Lian in 1361. At that time, the author was hired by Zhu Yuanzhang, the Taizu of the Ming Dynasty, and participated in Zhu Yuanzhang's shogunate with Liu Ji and others. He had the opportunity to come to Zhongshan and wrote this travelogue.
works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: Zhongshan, with its precipitous terrain and beautiful scenery, has attracted literati of all ages to write about it. It is easy to repeat the same scenery when writing about Zhongshan. Song Lian, on the other hand, captured the scenery with his delicate brushstrokes, and relied on his strong painting language ability to describe the sounds, colors, and movements of the scenery, achieving the artistic state of "seeing the words and seeing the appearance", and achieved the effect of "conquering more with less."

works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587: 城头野风吹白旗，十丈大书中堂伊。天潢宫保飞马至，奉旨金陵勾当事。总督太牢瘖不鸣，吴淞车债原馀生。九拜夷舟十不耻，黄侯自分已生死。十万居民空献芹，香花迎跽诸将军。掩泪默无语，周自请，郑不许：声言架炮钟山颠，严城顷刻灰飞烟；不则尽决后湖水，灌入青溪六十里。最后许以七马头，浙江更有羁縻州；白金二千一百万，三年分偿先削券。券书首请帝玺丹，大臣同署全权官。冒死入奏得帝命，江水汪汪和议定。

works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: The poet often visits Jiming Temple and overlooks Zhongshan Mountain and Xuanwu Lake. He suddenly feels refreshed, showing his heroic and open mind and his lively interest in being close to nature.
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587: 地拔凭观豁，廊回引步深。晚云山有态，秋寺树多阴。徙倚弥成趣，频来为写心。平生奇尚在，随处具登临。

works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: Jiming Temple is located on the foothills of Jilong Mountain on the side of Taicheng. It is one of the oldest Buddhist temples in Nanjing. The history can be traced back to the Qixuan Temple in the Eastern Wu Dynasty. The temple was located in the rear garden of the Kingdom of Wu. In the eighth year of the Liang Dynasty in the Southern Dynasties (527), Emperor Liang Wu built Tongtai Temple in Jimingdai, and it has since become a holy place for Buddhism. In the 20th year of Hongwu in the Ming Dynasty (1387), Zhu Yuanzhang rebuilt it and inscribed it as "Jiming Temple".
works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: In the five-character ancient poem "Jiming Temple", Zhang Zhidong praised the extraordinary sight of the "landscape, city and forest" integrated from far and near on the Jiming Temple Mountain, and criticized "the white gate to wander around, dragging and lifeless" "I am fascinated by Qin Huainan and will not step on the back of Zhongshan Mountain", which expresses the ambition and courage of a famous official and reformist leader in the late Qing Dynasty to work hard and be enterprising. At the end of the poem, Du Fu's poem is quoted, once again implicitly recording that unforgettable and inspiring moment in the stormy late Qing Dynasty when the cock crows endlessly.
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587:
雨暗覆舟山，泉响鸡鸣埭，
埭流北湖水，僧住南朝寺。
当时造宫城，选此陵阿地,
朝市皆下临，江山充环卫。
白门游冶子，沓拖无生气，
心醉秦淮南，不踏钟山背。
一朝辟僧楼，雄秀发其秘，
城外湖皓白，湖外山苍翠。
南岸山如马，饮江驻鞍辔,
北岸山如屏，萦青与天际，
鹭洲沙出没，浦口塔标识。
烟中万楼台，渺若蚁蛭细，
亦有杜老忧，今朝豁蒙蔽。

朝别朱雀门，暮栖白鹭洲。波光摇海月，星影入城楼。望美金陵宰，如思琼树忧。徒令魂入梦，翻觉夜成秋。绿水解人意，为余西北流。因声玉琴里，荡漾寄君愁。
鸡笼山上鸡鸣寺，绀宇凌霞鸟路长。古埭尚传齐武帝，风流空忆竟陵王。白门柳色残秋雨，元武湖波澹夕阳。下界销沉陵谷异，枫林十庙晚苍苍。
鸡笼山上鸡鸣寺，绀宇凌霞鸟路长。古埭尚传齐武帝，风流空忆竟陵王。白门柳色残秋雨，元武湖波澹夕阳。下界销沉陵谷异，枫林十庙晚苍苍。
始发碧江口，旷然谐远心。风清舟在鉴，日落水浮金。瓜步逢潮信，台城过雁音。故乡何处是，云外即乔林。

建业曾传半壁安，秦淮呜咽暮涛寒。六朝寂寞青山在，十庙阴沈画壁残。
禾黍故墟屯铁马，烟花南部失雕阑。隔江《玉树》歌声断，更有哀弦向月弹。

works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: This poem was written three years after the signing of the Treaty of Nanjing. The author passed by Nanjing, thinking about the past and looking back at the present, worrying about the fate of the country, feeling emotional about it, and writing a poem to express his feelings.
works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: The poem starts with the historical status and topography of Nanjing. Cui Wei describes the towering city wall. The second couplet of the poem is a commentary on the Nanjing Peace Treaty. Then he switched from actual description to description of scenery. The epilogue pushes the emotional waves of sadness and indignation to a climax.
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587: 崔巍雉堞尚前朝，形胜东南第一标。惊见羽书传昨夜，忽闻和议出崇朝。秦淮花柳添憔悴，玄武旌旗空寂寥。往事何人更愤切，不堪呜咽独江潮。

works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: The Southern Song Dynasty perished.
works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: It expresses the poet's mourning for the demise of the Southern Song Dynasty and his pain of becoming a slave to the subjugated country. It expresses the emotion of the uncertainty of rise and fall and the sorrow of being homeless.
works.\u76f8\u5173\u4e8b\u4ef6: The Southern Song Dynasty was destroyed, the Eastern Wu Dynasty was destroyed, and the powerful officials of the Eastern Jin Dynasty fought against each other.
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587: 只见空城不见台，客行搔首重徘徊。风云旧日龙南渡，宇宙新秋雁北来。三国衣冠同草莽，六朝宫殿总尘埃。交游相见休相问，把手江头且一杯。

works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: Zhou Bangyan was appointed as the Lishui Order of Jiangning. Lishui is very close to Jinling, so the poet took the opportunity to visit and created a nostalgic masterpiece.
works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: The first poem recalls the grand events of the Southern Dynasties in Jinling, and through the moonlight of the old days and the eternity of kings and swallows, it sets off the vicissitudes of things and people, and the rise and fall of world affairs. It is majestic, desolate and tragic.
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587:
佳丽地，南朝盛事谁记。山围故国绕清江，髻鬟对起。怒涛寂寞打孤城，风樯遥度天际。
断崖树、犹倒倚，莫愁艇子曾系。空馀旧迹郁苍苍，雾沉半垒。夜深月过女墙来，伤心东望淮水。
酒旗戏鼓甚处市？想依稀、王谢邻里，燕子不知何世，入寻常、巷陌人家，相对如说兴亡，斜阳里。
驿亭三杨树，正当白下门。吴烟暝长条，汉水啮古根。向来送行处，回首阻笑言。别后若见之，为余一攀翻。

works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: According to Zhan Ang's "Li Bai Poems and Prose Series", this poem was written in the ninth year of Tianbao. At that time, Li Bai was living in Jinling, and his poem goes: "In the fifth month of the month in the west of Jinling, my ancestors were at Baixia Pavilion. If you want to find the top of Lu Peak, you first go around the Han River." Even when Li Bai was about to visit Mount Lu in May, friends from Jinling said goodbye to Baixia. Pavilion, because of this poem.
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587: 海水昔飞动，三龙纷战争。钟山危波澜，倾侧骇奔鲸。黄旗一扫荡，割壤开吴京。六代更霸王，遗迹见都城。至今秦淮间，礼乐秀群英。地扇邹鲁学，诗腾颜谢名。五月金陵西，祖余白下亭。欲寻庐峰顶，先绕汉水行。香炉紫烟灭，瀑布落太清。若攀星辰去，挥手缅含情。

works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: "Suiyuan Essays" is a collection of essays written by Yuan Mei after decades of accumulation.

works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587: 关势接苍穹，登临四望空。钟声何处寺，帆影大江风。薄雾山腰白，斜阳鸟背红。此身何处着，端在画图中。
关势接苍穹，登临四望空。钟声何处寺，帆影大江风。薄雾山腰白，斜阳鸟背红。此身何处着，端在画图中。

works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: In the third year of Shunzhi, Mao Qiling joined Mao Youlun's anti-Qing rebels. Because he opposed Mao Youlun's work with Ma Shiying and Fang Guoan, he denounced Ma and Fang as national traitors. They were the grievances of Fang and wanted to kill them many times. Mao Qiling changed his name and surname, fled to avoid revenge, and lived in the Jianghuai River.
works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: Describe the ruins of the Ming Dynasty and express the thoughts of the motherland.
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587: 建业重关控上游，龙盘虎踞旧神州。荆门西扼江流下，越徼东连海气浮。故国山河天堑合，夕阳宫阙石城秋。登临转觉乡关远，日暮苍茫涕泗流。
天下伤心处，劳劳送客亭。春风知别苦，不遣柳条青。

works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: The first six sentences use delicate brushwork to vividly describe the mountains and rivers before dawn, revealing the author's inner emotional waves. The last four sentences, accompanied by the approaching scene of his hometown, increasingly reveal the author's subtle and complex thoughts.
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587: 寒鸟树间响，落星川际浮。繁霜白晓岸，苦雾黑晨流。鳞鳞逆去水，弥弥急还舟。望乡行复立，瞻途近更修。谁能百里地，萦绕千端愁？

works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: Wang Qi (also known as Junyu) was an official in the south of the Yangtze River and composed the Qilu "Jinling Heart-Appreciating Pavilion".
works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: It sings about the majestic scenery of the Qinhuai River thousands of miles away, thus expressing the feeling of wandering in life as "the years go slowly to Beijing, and Xiao Xiaohua grows up in the world".
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587:
千里秦淮在玉壶，江山清丽壮吴都。
昔人已化辽天鹤，旧画难寻卧雪图。
冉冉流年去京国，萧萧华发老江湖。
残蝉不会登临意，又噪西风入座隅。

works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: Wang Qi (also known as Junyu) was an official in the south of the Yangtze River and composed the Qilu "Jinling Heart-Appreciating Pavilion".
works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: It sings about the majestic scenery of the Qinhuai River thousands of miles away, thus expressing the feeling of wandering in life as "the years go slowly to Beijing, and Xiao Xiaohua grows up in the world".
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587:
千里秦淮在玉壶，江山清丽壮吴都。
昔人已化辽天鹤，旧画难寻卧雪图。
冉冉流年去京国，萧萧华发老江湖。
残蝉不会登临意，又噪西风入座隅。
绝俗俗无侣，修心心自斋。连崖夕气合，虚宇宿云霾。卧藤新接户，欹石久成阶。树声非有意，禽戏似忘怀。故人市朝狎，心期林壑乖。唯怜对芳杜，可以为吾侪。

其一
永王正月东出师，天子遥分龙虎旗。
楼船一举风波静，江汉翻为雁鹜池。
其二
三川北虏乱如麻，四海南奔似永嘉。
但用东山谢安石，为君谈笑静胡沙。
其三
雷鼓嘈嘈喧武昌，云旗猎猎过寻阳。
秋毫不犯三吴悦，春日遥看五色光。
其四
龙蟠虎踞帝王州，帝子金陵访古丘。
春风试暖昭阳殿，明月还过鳷鹊楼。
其五
二帝巡游俱未回，五陵松柏使人哀。
诸侯不救河南地，更喜贤王远道来。
其六
丹阳北固是吴关，画出楼台云水间。
千岩烽火连沧海，两岸旌旗绕碧山。
其七
王出三山按五湖，楼船跨海次陪都。
战舰森森罗虎士，征帆一一引龙驹。
其八
长风挂席势难回，海动山倾古月摧。
君看帝子浮江日，何似龙骧出峡来。
其九
祖龙浮海不成桥，汉武寻阳空射蛟。
我王楼舰轻秦汉，却似文皇欲渡辽。
其十
帝宠贤王入楚关，扫清江汉始应还。
初从云梦开朱邸，更取金陵作小山。
其十一
试借君王玉马鞭，指挥戎虏坐琼筵。
南风一扫胡尘静，西入长安到日边。

works.\u65f6\u671f: Late Ming Dynasty; Early Qing Dynasty
works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: Through the falling flowers and catkins and the faded youth, it reveals the sorrow of the women in the boudoir who are confused by spring thoughts.
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587:
眉淡衫轻春思乱。不怪无情，翻受多情绊。怕上层楼凝望眼。落花飞絮终朝见。
钗凤暗敲双股断。划损雕栏，一一相思遍。香袅兽炉空作篆，荼縻开谢闲庭院。

其一石头城
山围故国周遭在，潮打空城寂寞回。
淮水东边旧时月，夜深还过女墙来。
其二乌衣巷
朱雀桥边野草花，乌衣巷口夕阳斜。
旧时王谢堂前燕，飞入寻常百姓家。
其三台城
台城六代竞豪华，结绮临春事最奢。
万户千门成野草，只缘一曲后庭花。
其四生公讲堂
生公说法鬼神听，身后空堂夜不扃。
高坐寂寥尘漠漠，一方明月可中庭。
其五江令宅
南朝词臣北朝客，归来唯见秦淮碧。
池台竹树三亩馀，至今人道江家宅。

works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: This poem expresses the author's deep interest and deep attachment to the scenery of Jinling over the past decades. Sansu: "Sansu Love" is a Buddhist term that refers to the love for the secular world. Li Xian of the Tang Dynasty noted: "Those who sent mulberry trees to the pagoda would be removed without spending three nights there, which shows that they have no love." Later, "three nights in love" was used to refer to the attachment to people or things.
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587:
八十
城西二里楚金陵，吴帝名为石首城。
如今土坞无青草，笑煞当时所必争。
九十二
雨花台下百花香，驻马坡前百草长。
若还谷熟人民乐，依旧风流好建康。
一九五
廿栽金陵九往返，何如三宿在桑间。
还家更有江南兴，只取诗囊子细看。

works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: This poem was probably written around the time of the Anshi Rebellion in Tianbao's last years. Jue Ren associates the crisis of the Tang Dynasty at that time with the Yongjia Rebellion in the Western Jin Dynasty, and associates Xie Andun with the story that Shu'an rose up to save the crisis. The poet lamented Xie An's heroic feat of saving the country in troubled times, and hoped to serve the country like Xie An. In the midst of a national crisis, I realized my long-cherished wish, and thought of my own experience of being unappreciated and full of ambitions, so I wrote this poem to express my beliefs.
works.\u76f8\u5173\u4e8b\u4ef6: Rebellion of Eight Kings
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587: 晋室昔横溃，永嘉遂南奔。沙尘何茫茫，龙虎斗朝昏。胡马风汉草，天骄蹙中原。哲匠感颓运，云鹏忽飞翻。组练照楚国，旌旗连海门。西秦百万众，戈甲如云屯。投鞭可填江，一扫不足论。皇运有返正，丑虏无遗魂。谈笑遏横流，苍生望斯存。冶城访古迹，犹有谢安墩。凭览周地险，高标绝人喧。想像东山姿，缅怀右军言。梧桐识嘉树，蕙草留芳根。白鹭映春洲，青龙见朝暾。地古云物在，台倾禾黍繁。我来酌清波，于此树名园。功成拂衣去，归入武陵源。

works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: The poetry style is clear and beautiful, and the syllables are harmonious. It is both euphemistic and implicit, yet refreshing and bright.
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587:
王气消沉石子冈，放鹰调马蒋陵旁。
金棺移塔思原庙，玉匣藏衣记奉常。
杨柳重栽驰道改，樱桃莫荐寝园荒。
圣公没后无抔土，姑孰江声空夕阳。

works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: It describes the mystery and solemnity of the Ming Xiaoling Mausoleum, the mausoleum of Zhu Yuanzhang, the founder of the Ming Dynasty.

works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: The "Xikun style" poets specialized in poetry creation from Li Shangyin of the late Tang Dynasty. They paid attention to exquisite carving, elegance and fine craftsmanship, which initiated the Song people's trend of "taking talent and learning as poetry". Yang Yi highly admired Li Shangyin, a poet from the Tang Dynasty.
works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: Through a large number of stories of the Southern Dynasties, it is revealed that the corrupt nature of the emperor of the Southern Dynasties was spent on hunting and lusting after wine and sex, which ultimately led to the historical fact of successive defeats.
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587:
五鼓端门漏滴稀，夜签声断翠华飞。
繁星晓埭闻鸡度，细雨春场射雉归。
步试金莲波溅襪，歌翻玉树涕沾衣。
龙盘王气终三百，犹得澄澜对敞扉。

works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: large number of songs are sung about the many scenic spots and romantic figures in the ancient capital of Jinling, showing the extremely luxurious and luxurious scene in the heyday of the Southern Dynasty. However, the extremes of joy give rise to sorrows, and the peaks and declines. The last couplet expresses a strong sigh of rise and fall.
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587:
结绮临春映夕霏，景阳钟动曙星稀。
潘妃宝钏光如昼，江令花牋落似飞。
舴艋凌波朱火度，觚稜拂汉紫烟微。
自从饮马秦淮水，蜀柳无因对殿帏。

舴艋凌波朱火度，觚稜拂汉紫烟微。
自从饮马秦淮水，蜀柳无因对殿帏。

works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: large number of songs are sung about the many scenic spots and romantic figures in the ancient capital of Jinling, showing the extremely luxurious and luxurious scene in the heyday of the Southern Dynasty. works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: This is an epic poem. On the surface, this poem is criticizing the emperors of the Southern Dynasties. In fact, it is using the past to satirize the present. It is criticizing the injustice of the rulers at that time and showing the poet's concern for politics.
works.\u76f8\u5173\u4e8b\u4ef6: Emperor Qi Fei of the Southern Dynasty once made lotus flowers out of gold and placed them on the ground for his beloved Concubine Pan to walk on.

works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: There are many scenic spots in Jinling, but Mei Zengliang and his entourage were particularly fond of it, and they visited the little-known Xiaopan Valley with great interest. This trip was unusual, and the "Travel to Xiaopangu" written about it can be regarded as a small wonder.
works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: The first sentence at the beginning seems simple, but in fact it is closely related to the whole text. It is the general direction and clue of the author's "seeking Xiaopangu". One word "Qiu" governs the whole article, and the whole article is centered on this. The article goes from hope to hopelessness to hope and then to disappointment, with many twists and turns. The author couldn't find it the first time, so he went to the vicinity of Guiyuntang. The second time he couldn't find it, he went to Lulong Mountain for the third time. The article advances layer by layer with twists and turns. The author's name is Jiyou, but he actually writes about traveling for the sake of seeking. In the process of seeking but not getting, he cleverly and naturally demonstrates the unique charm of Jinghewen. Xiaopangu is everywhere, but looking back, it is Xiaopangu. It seems real yet fictitious, seems fictitious yet real, creates suspense, and ends with doubt. The idea is unique and bold.

works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587: 峨峨石头城，下有长短亭。城前江茫茫，亭边柳青青。行人重离别，词客倾醁醽。东望莫愁湖，西连白鹭汀。华辀送叠鼓，危橹挂飞舲。隔岸商女歌，歌声君莫听。

works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: In the eighth year of Yongming Dynasty, he followed Wang Xiao Zilong as the general of Zhenxi and the assassin of Jingzhou, and joined the army with Xie Tiao as the meritorious cao of Zhenxi. Xie Tiao therefore went to Jiangling in the spring of the following year. He traveled westward along the Yangtze River from Jiankang (today's Nanjing). He passed through three mountains in the evening, climbed up and looked over, and looked back at the capital. He was moved by the scene and expressed the poet's feeling of nostalgia for his country and his hometown.
works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: The whole poem is divided into three organic parts. The first two sentences are the first part. Borrowing ancient poems as inspiration to sing your own voice is the so-called "borrowing guests to accompany you" method (Fang Dongshu's language), which is actually a kind of metonymy. The second part describes the scenery. The poet captured the most characteristic image with his unique personal feelings. He also looked at Jingyi, dozens of miles away in Jiankang, which was currently illuminated by the bright sunset. The third part is lyrical. It shows that he has not forgotten his love and still knows how to look back home. Who can help but have gray hair? The whole poem has smooth language, harmonious tones, natural allusions, and exquisite counterpoints, all of which are in line with the characteristics of "Yongming style".
works.\u76f8\u5173\u4e8b\u4ef6: Xie Tiao went to Jiangling

灞涘望长安，河阳视京县。白日丽飞甍，参差皆可见。馀霞散成绮，澄江静如练。
喧鸟覆春洲，杂英满芳甸。去矣方滞淫，怀哉罢欢宴。佳期怅何许，泪下如流霰。
有情知望乡，谁能鬒不变！
天外秋云四散飞。波间风艇一时归。他年淮水东边月，犹为登临替落晖。夸客胜，数星稀。晚寒拂拂动秋衣。酒行不尽清光意，输与渔舟睡钓矶。

works.\u65f6\u671f: Northern Song Dynasty; Southern Song Dynasty
works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: Facing the surging river, the poet laments his life of medical practice that has gone through wars, and thinks of the ancient city of Jinling behind him that has experienced the rise and fall of six dynasties.
works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: Describing the prosperity of Jinling, it actually selects some typical examples of emperors from several short dynasties in several countries who lived in extravagance and debauchery. Through the connections among them, it points out the essential reasons for the successive defeats of the Six Dynasties in order, and laments that Jinling has been plundered by ice and fire for many years. The grand scenery of the past has long since disappeared, and the contrast between the past and the present expresses the sense of vicissitudes of life.

works.\u76f8\u5173\u4eba\u7269: 刘禹锡、周邦彦、李商隐、陈后主
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587: 岁晚念行役，江阔渺风烟。六朝文物何在，回首更凄然。倚尽危楼杰观，暗想琼枝璧月，罗袜步承莲。桃叶山前鹭，无语下寒滩。潮寂寞，浸孤垒，涨平川。莫愁艇子何处，烟树杳无边。王谢堂前双燕，空绕乌衣门巷，斜日草连天。只有台城月，千古照婵娟。

works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: In the 17th year of Gengchen (1280), the 17th year of the Yuan Dynasty, which was the second year after the Southern Song Dynasty was destroyed by the Yuan Dynasty, Bai Pu moved to Jiankang at the age of 55. Bai Pu once said: "Gengchen predicted that he lived in Jiankang and visited ancient times in his spare time." (See "The Collection of Tianlai. Preface to Winning the Championship") Sun Daya's "Postscript to the Collection of Tianlai" said: "I moved to Jinling, where the elders indulged in the mountains and rivers. Travel with poetry and wine." This poem was written by Bai Pu when he was having a banquet with the elders at the beginning of his settlement in Jiankang. We know that Bai Pu was born in Jin Mo and belongs to a Jin family. He experienced chaos as a child and witnessed the destruction of the Jin Dynasty by the Mongols. He suffered the "Renchen Disaster" when he was seven years old. In March of the first year of Jin Aizong's Tianxing reign (1232), Mongolian soldiers besieged Bianjing, the capital of Jin. The following year, the city was destroyed and his mother was taken prisoner. Bai Pu and her sister went north with their father's friend Yuan Haowen. This experience had a profound impact on Bai Pu's life. It not only determined his life path, but also determined the sad and sad tone of his creations. Wang Bowen said that Bai Pu "has suffered from chaos since he was a child. Emperor Cang lost his mother, and he sighed with sorrow over the mountains and rivers." ("Preface to the Collection of Tianlai") Bai Pu's life experience is quite representative among Yuan opera families. His dissatisfaction with the rule of Yuan and Meng Class cooperation, willing to be a remnant and a hermit, living in the southeast, and relying on the quiet grass, are exactly the reflection of the hardship and depression of a generation of intellectuals. Bai Pu's creations, whether poems, lyrics, music, or dramas, are all clearly marked with the special era, A deep imprint of a special mentality. Only when you understand this can you read Bai Pu's lyrics and music.
works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: This poem was written by Bai Pu when he first came to Jinling. It has a fresh feeling about the beautiful scenery of Jinling, and also has a feeling of his hometown - Bai Pu's heart is contradictory and painful. The sky is filled with smoke, the trees are dim, and the cold current is blowing. Pheasant Cloud Village reveals a desolation and loneliness. There are also sparse pedestrians pointing out the prosperity of the six generations and the ruins of the palaces of the previous dynasties, which makes people suddenly think of their homeland. From the word "good" below, it seems to have changed, and it seems that I was infected by the momentum of the dragons and tigers in Shicheng Zhongfu, and I felt a burst of joy; however, when I taste it carefully, it is clear that I have inherited the great rivers and mountains in front of me. Who controls the momentum? The country remains the same, the map changes, and the feeling of vicissitudes and the sigh of the country cannot be suppressed. Looking around at all the princes and elders here, they are all very old, with waning temples, sighing, and pouring wine to drink away their sorrows. How can this scene not make people deeply moved? The next part talks about drinking to drown one's sorrows. Just like after three rounds of drinking, when the travel pleasure is getting better and better, the author writes poems, wine, roars and songs to relieve the depression in his heart. Although these words are words of openness, they are actually words of sadness and pain. If we say that the emotions expressed in the first film are tortuous and tactful, I admire the majestic mountains and rivers of Jinling and feel a little comfort that I am destined to live in the south of the Yangtze River. At the same time, I look around at the elderly people sitting here and feel doubly sad. Every time a literary sentiment changes, it becomes deeper and deeper. Then, the next film is almost straight from the heart. Here, the allusion to "weeping in a new pavilion" is used, which should be said to be very appropriate. In the fourth year of Jianxing reign of Emperor Min of the Jin Dynasty (316), Liu Yao captured Chang'an and Emperor Min was captured. The following year, Emperor Yuan succeeded to the throne in Jianye (now Nanjing, Jiangsu) and established the Eastern Jin Dynasty. At that time, the vast area of the Yellow River Basin was ruled by ethnic minority rulers. Most of the Zhongzhou gentry crossed the river and went south. Whenever they had free time, the gentry who went south and the old officials of the imperial court gathered in Xinting, located in the southwest of today's Nanjing City, to have a banquet. Once during a banquet, Zhou Fen sighed and said: "The scenery is not special, just because the mountains and rivers are different." This made everyone present look at each other and shed tears (see "Shishuo Xinyu. Words"). Bai Pu's use of allusions in reverse writing makes the feeling of sadness even stronger. The sense of disillusionment about the irreversible rise and fall, the longing for the homeland, and the sorrow of separation overflows between the lines. Due to the author's strong sentimentality, the landscapes and scenic spots seem to be tainted with infinite sadness. The conclusion is like a translucent curtain, looking at the famous mountains and rivers in the hazy mist, as if you are still awake from a dream, as if you are awake and still drunk, but you can see that you are particularly sober in the drunkenness, the great song ends with everyone gone, and the peaks on the river are green. .
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587:
苍烟拥乔木，粉雉倚寒空。行人日暮回首，指点旧离宫。好在龙蟠虎踞，试问石城钟阜，形势为谁雄？慷慨一尊酒，南北几衰翁。
赋朝云，歌夜月，醉春风。新亭何苦流涕，兴废古今同。朱雀桥边野草，白鹭洲边江水，遗恨几时终。唤起六朝梦，山色有无中。

It has a fresh feeling about the beautiful scenery of Jinling, and also has a feeling of his hometown - Bai Pu's heart is contradictory and painful. The sky is filled with smoke, the trees are dim, and the cold current is blowing. Pheasant Cloud Village reveals a desolation and loneliness. There are also sparse pedestrians pointing out the prosperity of the six generations and the ruins of the palaces of the previous dynasties, which makes people suddenly think of their homeland. From the word "good" below, it seems to have changed, and it seems that I was infected by the momentum of the dragons and tigers in Shicheng Zhongfu, and I felt a burst of joy; however, when I taste it carefully, it is clear that I have inherited the great rivers and mountains in front of me. Who controls the momentum? The country remains the same, the map changes, and the feeling of vicissitudes and the sigh of the country cannot be suppressed. Looking around at all the princes and elders here, they are all very old, with waning temples, sighing, and pouring wine to drink away their sorrows. How can this scene not make people deeply moved? The next part talks about drinking to drown one's sorrows. Just like after three rounds of drinking, when the travel pleasure is getting better and better, the author writes poems, wine, roars and songs to relieve the depression in his heart. Although these words are words of openness, they are actually words of sadness and pain. If we say that the emotions expressed in the first film are tortuous and tactful, I admire the majestic mountains and rivers of Jinling and feel a little comfort that I am destined to live in the south of the Yangtze River. At the same time, I look around at the elderly people sitting here and feel doubly sad. Every time a literary sentiment changes, it becomes deeper and deeper. Then, the next film is almost straight from the heart. Here, the allusion to "weeping in a new pavilion" is used, which should be said to be very appropriate. In the fourth year of Jianxing reign of Emperor Min of the Jin Dynasty (316), Liu Yao captured Chang'an and Emperor Min was captured. The following year, Emperor Yuan succeeded to the throne in Jianye (now Nanjing, Jiangsu) and established the Eastern Jin Dynasty. At that time, the vast area of the Yellow River Basin was ruled by ethnic minority rulers. Most of the Zhongzhou gentry crossed the river and went south. Whenever they had free time, the gentry who went south and the old officials of the imperial court gathered in Xinting, located in the southwest of today's Nanjing City, to have a banquet. Once during a banquet, Zhou Fen sighed and said: "The scenery is not special, just because the mountains and rivers are different." This made everyone present look at each other and shed tears (see "Shishuo Xinyu. Words"). Bai Pu's use of allusions in reverse writing makes the feeling of sadness even stronger. The sense of disillusionment about the irreversible rise and fall, the longing for the homeland, and the sorrow of separation overflows between the lines. Due to the author's strong sentimentality, the landscapes and scenic spots seem to be tainted with infinite sadness. The conclusion is like a translucent curtain, looking at the famous mountains and rivers in the hazy mist, as if you are still awake from a dream, as if you are awake and still drunk, but you can see that you are particularly sober in the drunkenness, the great song ends with everyone gone, and the peaks on the river are green. .
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587:
苍烟拥乔木，粉雉倚寒空。行人日暮回首，指点旧离宫。好在龙蟠虎踞，试问石城钟阜，形势为谁雄？慷慨一尊酒，南北几衰翁。
赋朝云，歌夜月，醉春风。新亭何苦流涕，兴废古今同。朱雀桥边野草，白鹭洲边江水，遗恨几时终。唤起六朝梦，山色有无中。

works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: In the 17th year of Gengchen (1280), the 17th year of the Yuan Dynasty, which was the second year after the Southern Song Dynasty was destroyed by the Yuan Dynasty, Bai Pu moved to Jiankang at the age of 55. Bai Pu once said: "Gengchen predicted that he lived in Jiankang and visited ancient times in his spare time." (See "The Collection of Tianlai. Preface to Winning the Championship") Sun Daya's "Postscript to the Collection of Tianlai" said: "I moved to Jinling, where the elders indulged in the mountains and rivers. Travel with poetry and wine." This poem was written by Bai Pu when he was having a banquet with the elders at the beginning of his settlement in Jiankang. We know that Bai Pu was born in Jin Mo and belongs to a Jin family. He experienced chaos as a child and witnessed the destruction of the Jin Dynasty by the Mongols. He suffered the "Renchen Disaster" when he was seven years old. In March of the first year of Jin Aizong's Tianxing reign (1232), Mongolian soldiers besieged Bianjing, the capital of Jin. The following year, the city was destroyed and his mother was taken prisoner. Bai Pu and her sister went north with their father's friend Yuan Haowen. This experience had a profound impact on Bai Pu's life. It not only determined his life path, but also determined the sad and sad tone of his creations. Wang Bowen said that Bai Pu "has suffered from chaos since he was a child. Emperor Cang lost his mother, and he sighed with sorrow over the mountains and rivers." ("Preface to the Collection of Tianlai") Bai Pu's life experience is quite representative among Yuan opera families. His dissatisfaction with the rule of Yuan and Meng Class cooperation, willing to be a remnant and a hermit, living in the southeast, and relying on the quiet grass, are exactly the reflection of the hardship and depression of a generation of intellectuals. Bai Pu's creations, whether poems, lyrics, music, or dramas, are all clearly marked with the special era, A deep imprint of a special mentality. Only when you understand this can you read Bai Pu's lyrics and music.

苍烟拥乔木，粉雉倚寒空。行人日暮回首，指点旧离宫。好在龙蟠虎踞，试问石城钟阜，形势为谁雄？慷慨一尊酒，南北几衰翁。
赋朝云，歌夜月，醉春风。新亭何苦流涕，兴废古今同。朱雀桥边野草，白鹭洲边江水，遗恨几时终。唤起六朝梦，山色有无中。

works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: In the second year of Emperor Yingzong's rule (1323), Xu Youren served as the supervisory censor of Jiangnan Xingtai, and the government was in Jinling. He visited many historical sites in Jinling and wrote many historical poems.
works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: Focusing on the "Rouge Well" relics of the Chen Dynasty, it reveals the historical lessons of rulers who did not care about the lives of the people and were extravagant and lustful, which led to the destruction of the country, and thus came to the theory of the rise and fall of "extreme color leads to desolation".
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587: 他山一卷石，何意效时妆。天生偶然斑驳，兰麝不能香。＊作陈家宫井，浇出后庭玉树，直使国俱亡。故邑久眢废，陈迹草茫茫。叹人间，才璇室，又阿房。丽华＊发如鉴，曾此笑相将。一旦江山瓶坠，犹欲夫妻同穴，甚矣色成荒。五色补天缺，万世仰娲皇。

works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: The poet passed by Guazhou by boat and remembered what he had done in his former residence in Jinling.
works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: It reveals his nostalgia for his hometown and his strong desire to return to the political stage and implement the New Deal.
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587: 京口瓜洲一水间，钟山只隔数重山。春风又绿江南岸，明月何时照我还？

works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: The artistic value of Buddhist temple poetry in the Southern Dynasties is not very high. Frankly speaking, most of them are indifferent to deal with the emperor. Praising and flattering Buddhist temples is actually indirectly flattering the emperor, and there is no real feeling and nature. Revealing is, at most, just a show off of talent. But Liu Lingxian is different. In addition to using the Buddhist temple as a background for the characters' activities in a deeper level, she also boldly shows real human feelings without any pretense or concealment.
works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: It seems that not a single word in this poem is intended to refer to the Buddhist temple, but there is not a single word that does not wish to write about the Buddhist temple. The noisy "long corridor" and "wide hall" are just nothingness that is flaunted on the surface, but the real thing may be when there is "no sound" in the "qufang". The contrast between movement and stillness, the contrast between noise and silence, the contrast between nothingness and reality, not only vividly reflects reality, but also is full of profound philosophy. The scenes written in the poem can indeed give people infinitely rich and obscure imagination space.
works.\u76f8\u5173\u4e8b\u4ef6: Guangzhai Temple was built in the seventh year of Liang Tianjian (508) in the Southern Dynasty. It was originally the former residence of Xiao Yan, Emperor Wu of Liang Dynasty.
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587: 长廊欣目送，广殿悦逢迎。何当曲房里，幽隐无人声？

works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: The feelings of family and country, the pain of the remaining people.
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587: 一片花含一片愁，愁随江水不东流。飞飞长榜景阳楼。六代只馀芳草在，三园空有乳莺留。白门容易白人头。

works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: In the spring of 1932, Shen Zupen, who was studying in the Chinese Department of the School of Liberal Arts of Central University, wrote her famous early work "Huanxi Sand" in the poetry course taught by Mr. Wang Dong, dean of literature and director of the Chinese Department: "The grass records the wonderful travels every year. The mountains and rivers still open their eyes. The sound of drums and rivets is filled with long thoughts. Who wrote the poem about the orioles in March? One day, the wind blew alone on the building. There is spring sorrow in the setting sun!" Mr. Wang Dong read this word, "For the September 18th Incident in 1931 I was surprised that the post-war national crisis was reflected in such a subtle and profound way in the writings of a young girl, so I asked her to talk and encourage her. From then on, she became more interested in learning ci and became more confident." This is Mr. Cheng Qianfan's account in "The Biography of Shen Zufen". Cheng Wen also recalled that after the outbreak of the Anti-Japanese War in 1937, the suffering of the nation and the hardship of personal displacement caused the female poet to write a series of phrases with deeper connotations. Express the feeling of the rise and fall of family and country.
works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: The three poems of "Huanxisha" were written while teaching at Jinling University and West China University in Chengdu from 1942 to 1946. "In the late period of the Anti-Japanese War, the ugly face of the Kuomintang reactionaries was gradually exposed. The two completely different lives of reveling in wealth and hunger and crying in cold weather made the female poet extremely indignant. She took up her pen to fight. In her poems during this period, she wrote faithfully It showed some aspects of the political and social life at that time. These four years were her most creative period." (Quoted from Cheng Qianfan's "The Biography of Shen Zufen") In the 1960s and 1970s, Tai Jing, a professor at National Taiwan University, a famous writer and calligrapher Mr. Nong once copied the poem "Huanxi Sha" written by the female poet in his own handwriting, and added a note beside the copy: "This was written by Shen Zufen during the Anti-Japanese War. Li Yian was on his way to the south, but he never felt this way." It can be seen from this group of "Huanxi Sha" 》The spread and influence are wide.
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587:
浣溪沙（三首）
客有以渝州近事见告者，感成小词。
岁岁新烽续旧烟，人间几见海成田，新亭风景异当年！
如此山河输半壁，依然歌舞当长安，危阑北望泪如川。

莫向西川问杜鹃，繁华争说小长安，涨波脂水自年年。
筝笛高楼春酒暖，兵戈远塞铁衣寒，尊前空唱念家山。

辛苦征人百战还，渝州非复旧临安，繁华疑是梦中看。
彻夜笙歌新贵宅，连江灯火估人船，可怜万灶渐无烟。

works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: This is a nostalgic poem written by Bai Pu after he settled in Jinling. Generally speaking, any nostalgic work is not simply about nostalgic for the sake of the past. Nostalgia for the past and nostalgia for the present are almost the same as shadow and shape, and are always closely related. In a sense, Crouch's "all history is contemporary history" is not completely unreasonable. Collingwood, another historical philosopher who is equally analytical and critical as Crotch, puts it more interestingly: “Every historian centers himself and observes history from his own perspective, so he sees He sees certain issues that others cannot see, and each historian observes each issue from his own unique point of view, that is, from his own unique aspect." ("Modern Western History and Philosophy Translation Collection" ) Although we cannot completely agree with the views of these two Western historians and philosophers, we cannot deny the enlightenment of their views. Bai Pu is not a historian, but he writes historical dramas and nostalgic poems. His "Wutong Rain" drama is basically based on old and new Tang books and miscellaneous biographies, but it has a very strong subjective lyrical color. It can be said that Bai Pu has his own unique understanding of history. Knowing this, let's look at this nostalgic poem again, and it will be easier for us to get the gist of it.
works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: The poem begins with majesty, but ends with loneliness. Only from the perspective of nostalgic feelings and expressing emotions of rise and fall, Bai Ci can neither be as good as Li Bai's nostalgic poems, nor can it be compared with Su and Xinhuai's ancient poems. However, Bai Ci has its own characteristics. First of all, what deserves our attention is Bai Pu's persistent concern for reality. He never thinks about it again and can't stop thinking about it. The author recounts the past events in a sparse and sparse manner, and the conception of his words has not surpassed those of his predecessors. He revived the Eastern Jin Dynasty and captured the Southern Tang Dynasty. He slightly embellished the main words and sentences in the middle and later periods, but he has not yet shown his mastery. And "What day is it today", "Returning to my hometown today", and "Asking about the old house of Wu Yi", listening to the sound of fishing boats (when fishermen are fishing, they knock on the boat planks, startling the fish and people's nets), I want to cry, and I feel sad. Extremely painful. This made a particularly deep impression on us. Secondly, the poet's sense of loneliness and disillusionment is particularly strong, and the contrast between history and reality is also very clear. We know that Bai Pu lived in the changing dynasties during his life, "growing up among soldiers and wandering around" (Chen Ting's "Zhu Shan Tang Ci Hua"), but when he became an adult, he "cannot achieve his ambition" (Hu Shi's "Zhenzhu Ship", inner contradiction You can imagine the pain and pain. Originally, Bai Pu's father Bai Hua had great longing for his son, hoping that Bai Pu would "stretch thousands of miles" (Bai Hua's "Shi Heng"). Bai Pu himself also had great ambitions, but suffered a lot. When Bai Pu was not successful, he surrendered his ambition and had no choice but to do so. When Bai Pu was 36 years old, Shi Tianze, the prime minister of the founding government, wanted to recommend Bai Pu to serve as an official, but Bai Pu flatly refused. Why? Look at his father Bai Hua, who served for three consecutive dynasties and was criticized by the scholar-bureaucrats. The rebuke that "the country is in danger and one cannot live with justice" ended up with a "disreputed reputation" ("Jin History·Bai Hua Biography"). It can be seen that Bai Pu's "rejection of recommendation" was by no means an easy move. Bai Pu's life, How difficult it is to live! Therefore, the sentence "reminiscing about the past and melancholy" in this poem should not be added as a footnote. Bai Pu is clearly lamenting his unlucky and difficult life path. He also expressed his appreciation for "the endless tears of heroes" , should also be understood in this sense. His "aspirations are in the long forest and lush grass" and "will have to survive" ("Qinyuanchun·Supervisor Juyuan will appoint you as the government, because of reading Ji Kang and Shan Tao" "Those who are in love with me, I will compose this word to express my gratitude"), originally out of a hidden intention that is difficult to express. At the end of the word, it is written that the sunset, the fishermen's songs are faint, and the bangs are ringing, all of which highlight the author's unprecedented heart. Some are lonely and lonely. Finally, it is the plainness and simplicity of the writing style of this poem, which expresses deep feelings in the pure and natural nature. The lyrics are not piled up, and are not verbose, but contain emotions when hurt, and are majestic and solemn. Wang Bowen commented "The words are strict and the feelings are lofty" ("Preface to the Collection of Tianlai"); Zhu Yizun said, "It originates from Su and Xin, but there is no clamor" ("Postscript to the Collection of Tianlai"), this word is well deserved.
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587: 我望山形，虎踞龙盘，壮哉建康。忆黄旗紫盖，中兴东晋，雕栏玉砌，下逮南唐。步步金莲，朝朝琼树，宫殿吴时花草香。今何日，尚寺留萧姓，人做梅妆。长江不管兴亡，谩流尽英雄泪万行。问乌衣旧宅，谁家作主，白头老子，今日还乡。吊古愁浓，题诗人去，寂寞高楼无凤凰。斜阳外，正渔舟唱晚，一片鸣榔。

works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: In the 10th year of Kangxi (1671), Xu Weiwen went to Nanjing. Chen Weisong wrote a "Preface to Xu Weiwen", instructing him to visit those who were "abnormal and hidden in painting" such as Gong Banqian and others. His visit originally had the intention of visiting his homeland. After Xu's return, he completed "Zhongshan Plum Blossoms", which immediately set off a memorial activity with many inscriptions and chants. A group of poets who cherished the sorrow of the change of dynasty wrote inscriptions for it. This is actually a gathering to commemorate the Ming Xiaoling Mausoleum in a distant place, that is, to pay tribute to the motherland. This word is one of them.
works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: In this poem, the plum blossoms in the painting are indistinguishable from reality to fantasy, and are both real and illusory. The past and present, imagination and reality are intertwined into the poem, thus expressing the vicissitudes of life and the feeling of a dream. The upper part depicts plum blossoms from different sides, highlighting the beauty of its posture and the tranquility of the wind spirit, and then associates it with the prosperous scene of Jinling in the late Ming Dynasty; the lower part uses the poetry of Liu Yuxi and Du Mu, and uses images such as crows, stone horses, flowing water, and falling flowers to express their feelings. In response to the tragedy of the Yi Dynasty, he married and imagined his friend Tian Shui as his companion, spending the rest of his life in the world, expressing his attitude that he would not serve in the new dynasty. The words combine virtuality and reality, make good use of contrast, are both emotional and lyrical, and have high rhyme and quality.
works.\u76f8\u5173\u4f5c\u54c1: 《赠徐渭文序》、《钟山梅花图》、《山园小梅》、《石头城》、《泊秦淮》、《相见欢·林花谢了春红》
works.\u76f8\u5173\u4eba\u7269: 徐渭文、云臣、南耕、京少、刘禹锡、杜牧、李煜
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587: 题徐渭文《钟山梅花图》，同云臣、南耕、京少赋。十万琼枝，矫若银虬，翩如玉鲸。正困不胜烟，香浮南内；娇偏怯雨，影落西清。夹岸亭台，接天歌管，十四楼中乐太平。谁争赏？有珠珰贵戚，玉佩公卿。如今潮打孤城，只商女船头月自明。叹一夜啼乌，落花有恨；五陵石马，流水无声。寻去疑无，看来似梦，一幅生绡泪写成。携此卷，伴水天闲话，江海余生。
漫浪江头，三听秋砧，一登故台。望烟芜莽苍，令人目断，风樯掀舞，何日眉开。把酒新亭，围棋别墅，老气当时何壮哉。江东事，百年无恙，全是时才。纷然竞付轻埃。还水绕赏心东向淮。叹阿奴侪悲，因人碌碌，乃翁材略，馀地恢恢。凤阙天高，鹭洲潮落，约取白鸥归去来。阑干外，英雄陈迹，一醁琼杯。
绮阁香销，玉砌梦残，凄凉旧台。对御沟红叶，一番木落，宫墙黄菊，几度花开。水溯岷源，山联吴会，目送征鸿安往哉。都休问，六朝人物，谁拙谁才。平生衮衮烟埃。记匹马当年荆蜀淮。叹凋零殆尽，词源已竭，消磨未去，酒量犹恢。八跪蟹肥，四腮鲈美，客有可人招不来。油幢暇，凭栏一笑，相与传杯。

works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: This poem reflects the "family affairs of silk reeling, weaving and embroidery" in Changganli at that time. It can be seen that Jinling silk weaving has a long history and exquisite craftsmanship.
works.\u76f8\u5173\u4f5c\u54c1: 《修竹新篁图》、《清光留照图》、《兰竹芳馨图》、《甘谷菊泉图》、《丛兰荆棘图》
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587:
墙里开花墙外见，篱门半覆垂杨线,
门外春流一派清，青山立在门当面。
老子栽花百种多,清晨担卖下前坡,
三间古屋无儿女,换得鲜鱼供阿婆。
缫丝织绣家家事，金凤银龙供天子,
花样新添一线云,旧机不用西湖水。
机上男儿百巧民，单衫布褐不遮身,
中原百岁无争战，免荷干戈敢怨贫。

works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: Gui Youguang passed the provincial examination six times and stayed in Nanjing many times.
works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: It describes the fresh scenery and quiet atmosphere of Linggu Temple, showing a rational and wise artistic perception.
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587:
晨出东郭门，初日照我颜。
春风吹习习，好鸟声绵蛮。
岩阿见黄屋，登披寻神山。
半日犹山麓，十里长松间。
蜿蜒芳草路，寂寞古禅关。
画廊落丹雘，朱户蚀铜镮。
殿起无梁迥，塔留玩珠攀。
苍鼠戏树捷，野鹿看人閒。
山深静者爱，日晏未知还。

works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: The author wrote this while living in Nanjing.
works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: Praise the ancient city of Nanjing and chant the scenery of Nanjing.
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587: 五里松荫合，三义石径微。客情和水淡，诗思趁花飞。是处闻天籁，何心拾地肥。宝公应许我，趺坐暂忘机。
出郊为访志公林，塔影还从静室临。象外涤襟泉有德，阶前鼓掌地成音。忽闻鸟韵惊迷性，漫倚松涛试定心。幽寂偏宜驺从去，独留明月伴讴吟。

钟山白草枯，冬月蒸宿雾。
十里无立椔，冈阜但回互。
宝城独青青，日色上霜露。
殿门达明楼，周遭尚完固。
其外有穹碑，巍然当御路。
文自成祖为，千年系明祚。
侍卫八石人，祗肃候灵辂。
下列石兽六，森然象卤簿。
自马至狮子，两两相比附。
中间特崒嵂，有二擎天柱。
排立榛莽中，凡此皆尚具。
又有神烈山，世宗所封树。
卧碑自崇祯，禁约烦圣谕。
石大故不毁，文字犹可句。
至于土木工，俱已亡其素。
东陵在殿左，先时懿文祔。
云有?二层，去门可百步。
正殿门有五，天子升自阼。
门内庑三十，左右以次布。
门外设两厨，右殿上所驻。
祠署并宫监，羊房暨酒库。
以至各廨宇，并及诸宅务。
东西二红门，四十五巡铺。
一一费搜寻，涉目仍迷瞀。
山后更萧条，兵牧所屯聚。
洞然见铭石，崩出常王墓。
何代无厄菑，神圣莫能度。
幸兹寝园存，皇天永呵护。
奄人宿其中，无乃致亵污。
陵卫多官军，残毁法不捕。
伐木复撤亭，上触天地怒。
雷震樵夫死，梁压陵贼仆。
乃信高庙灵，却立生畏怖。
若夫本卫官，衣食久遗蠹。
及今尽流冗，存两千百户。
下国有虮臣，一年再奔赴。
低徊持寸管，能作西京赋。
尚虑耳目褊，流传有错误。
相逢虞子大，独记陵木数。
未得对东巡，空山论掌故。

works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: During his tenure in Jiankang, Ma Guangzu also created many literary works about Jiankang.
works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: Although the poet felt melancholy when facing the demise of the prosperous scene in the past, he was able to express his feelings and enjoy leisurely happiness while climbing the pavilion to view the mountains, boating and drinking.
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587:
人道青溪有九曲，如今一曲仅能存。
江家宅畔成花圃，东府门前作菜园。
登阁自堪观叠嶂，汎舟犹可醉芳樽。
料应当日皆无恙，苕霅潇湘不足言

works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: The poet wrote it after feeling the cool breeze.
works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: Describe the scene of sitting in Wulongtan at night.
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587: 林阴横满地，夜影忽过墙。忘却月已转，翻疑树易长。积雨有馀气，老荷终日香。空林如积水，清夜意难忘。

works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: Praise Xie An for his significant contribution to protecting national security without fear of danger.
works.\u76f8\u5173\u4e8b\u4ef6: The battle of fat and water
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587: 朱雀航南绕香陌，谢郎东墅连春碧。鸠眠高柳日方融，绮榭飘飖紫庭客。文楸方罫花参差，心阵未成星满池。四座无喧梧竹静，金蝉玉柄俱持颐。对局含情见千里，都城已得长蛇尾。江南王气系疏襟，未许苻坚过淮水。

works.\u76f8\u5173\u4e8b\u4ef6: The battle of fat and water
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587: 朱雀航南绕香陌，谢郎东墅连春碧。鸠眠高柳日方融，绮榭飘飖紫庭客。文楸方罫花参差，心阵未成星满池。四座无喧梧竹静，金蝉玉柄俱持颐。对局含情见千里，都城已得长蛇尾。江南王气系疏襟，未许苻坚过淮水。

works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: This book is included in the second volume of "Kangxi Jiangning Mansion Chronicles" compiled by Chen Kaiyu, the prefect of Jiangning in the Qing Dynasty. works.\u65f6\u671f: early Qing Dynasty; middle Qing Dynasty
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587: 涌地千寻起，摩霄九级悬。琉璃垂法相，翡翠结香烟。缔造人功巧，流传世代迁。旷然弥远望，万象拱诸天。
人天八万四千塔，谁其造者阿育王。江南经始康僧会，舍利爪发传萧梁。永乐制九级，海藏同辉光。天风扶我一登眺，长干万户连千樯。岷峨之脉气葱郁，四面屏障森堵墙。大江一线逝东北，前淮后湖环阴阳。虎龙蟠踞虽得地，英雄割裂无完疆。岂若隋唐大一统，直以弹丸当荆扬。如来涅槃矧千劫，灵物薶瘗诚荒唐。黄旗紫盖俱已矣，空余金地供翱翔。安得神僧捧刹入云去，无令承平士女悲沧桑!
人天八万四千塔，谁其造者阿育王。江南经始康僧会，舍利爪发传萧梁。永乐制九级，海藏同辉光。天风扶我一登眺，长干万户连千樯。岷峨之脉气葱郁，四面屏障森堵墙。大江一线逝东北，前淮后湖环阴阳。虎龙蟠踞虽得地，英雄割裂无完疆。岂若隋唐大一统，直以弹丸当荆扬。如来涅槃矧千劫，灵物薶瘗诚荒唐。黄旗紫盖俱已矣，空余金地供翱翔。安得神僧捧刹入云去，无令承平士女悲沧桑!

works.\u65f6\u671f: early Qing Dynasty; middle Qing Dynasty
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587: 秣陵旧是图王地，此日鸾旗列队过。一代规模成往迹，千秋兴废逐流波。宫墙断缺迷青琐，野水湾环剩玉河。治理艰勤重殷鉴，斜阳衰草系情多。
晓望诸峰翠色新，雨馀芳草碧如茵。春光已过湔裙节，胜地浑无蜡屐人。旅病那堪花入梦，暮寒不厌酒沾唇。遥思二月秦淮柳，蘸露拖烟委曲尘。

works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: "Suiyuan Food List" is the product of his forty years of gastronomic practice. In the form of classical Chinese essays, it delicately describes the dietary conditions and cooking techniques in Jiangsu and Zhejiang during the Qianlong period. It uses a large amount of space to describe in detail the Chinese history from the 14th to the 10th century. The 326 kinds of dishes and meals popular in the north and south in the eighth century also introduced the fine wines and teas of that time. It is a very important masterpiece of Chinese food in the Qing Dynasty.

works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: At that time, the poet was passing through Nanjing on his way north, and saw the desolation in front of the ancient capital, and the prosperity of the past was gone. From this, he felt the rise and fall of his family and country, so he wrote this poem.
works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: The first four lines of this poem leap across the distance of time and space, first describing the prosperity of Nanjing in the past, and then translating that that prosperity is gone now, and what is presented in front of us is a desolate and desolate scene; the last four lines describe the emperors of the old capital. In response to the desolation of the palace and Ming Xiaoling Mausoleum, the poet tightly grasped the typical things that reflected the change of ownership of the country to express his feelings. He poured out the vicissitudes of life that had been stirring in his heart for a long time into the poem, expressing his infinite hurt for the demise of the Ming Dynasty. The feeling of pain. .The whole poem is exquisitely conceived, rich in imagination, euphemistic and implicit, concise and profound. .
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587:
车马垂杨十字街，河桥灯火旧秦淮。
放衙非复通侯第，废圃谁知博士斋。
易饼市傍王殿瓦，换鱼江上孝陵柴。
无端射取原头鹿，收得长生苑内牌。

works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: At that time, the poet was passing through Nanjing on his way north, and saw the desolation in front of the ancient capital, and the prosperity of the past was gone. From this, he felt the rise and fall of his family and country, so he wrote this poem.
works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: The first four lines of this poem leap across the distance of time and space, first describing the prosperity of Nanjing in the past, and then translating that that prosperity is gone now, and what is presented in front of us is a desolate and desolate scene; the last four lines describe the emperors of the old capital. In response to the desolation of the palace and Ming Xiaoling Mausoleum, the poet tightly grasped the typical things that reflected the change of ownership of the country to express his feelings. He poured out the vicissitudes of life that had been stirring in his heart for a long time into the poem, expressing his infinite hurt for the demise of the Ming Dynasty. The feeling of pain. .The whole poem is exquisitely conceived, rich in imagination, euphemistic and implicit, concise and profound. .
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587:
车马垂杨十字街，河桥灯火旧秦淮。
放衙非复通侯第，废圃谁知博士斋。
易饼市傍王殿瓦，换鱼江上孝陵柴。
无端射取原头鹿，收得长生苑内牌。

车马垂杨十字街，河桥灯火旧秦淮。
放衙非复通侯第，废圃谁知博士斋。
易饼市傍王殿瓦，换鱼江上孝陵柴。
无端射取原头鹿，收得长生苑内牌。
石头城上翠孱颜，虎踞龙盘在此间。形胜旧传三国志，风云长护六朝山。登高感慨谁知己，揽辔澄清亦等闲。天遣艰难走西蜀，峨嵋万里隔秦关。
石头城上翠孱颜，虎踞龙盘在此间。形胜旧传三国志，风云长护六朝山。登高感慨谁知己，揽辔澄清亦等闲。天遣艰难走西蜀，峨嵋万里隔秦关。
潮水定可信，天风难与期。清晨西北转，薄暮东南吹。以此难挂席，佳期益相思。海月破圆影，菰蒋生绿池。昨日北湖梅，开花已满枝。今朝东门柳，夹道垂青丝。岁物忽如此，我来定几时。纷纷江上雪，草草客中悲。明发新林浦，空吟谢朓诗。

works.\u65f6\u671f: middle Ming Dynasty; late Ming Dynasty
middle Ming Dynasty; late Ming Dynasty

works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587: 楼上寝，残月下帘旌。梦见秣陵惆怅事，桃花柳絮满江城，双髻坐吹笙。

works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: This poem was written when Tianbao was traveling to Jinling. This poem praises the past and laments the present, lamenting that although the situation in Jinling is like a tiger on a dragon's side, the emperors of the past dynasties have rotated around like a revolving lantern. It can be seen that the strength of a country lies in human affairs rather than natural dangers. This poem seems to be a warning to the people in power not to imitate Empress Chen’s debauchery and pursue the country.
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587: 石头巉岩如虎踞，凌波欲过沧江去。钟山龙盘走势来，秀色横分历阳树。四十馀帝三百秋，功名事迹随东流。白马金鞍谁家子，吹唇虎啸凤凰楼。金陵昔时何壮哉！席卷英豪天下来。冠盖散为烟雾尽，金舆玉座成寒灰。扣剑悲吟空咄嗟，梁陈白骨乱如麻。天子龙沉景阳井，谁歌玉树后庭花。此地伤心不能道，目下离离长春草。送尔长江万里心，他年来访南山老。

works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: In the third year of Zhishun reign of Emperor Wenzong of the Yuan Dynasty (1332), Saduchi was transferred to the imperial censor Taifu Shi of various Daoist circles in the south of the Yangtze River, and moved to Jinling (now Nanjing City). This poem was written around this time.
works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: The scenery of Zhongshan is described from various angles, giving the natural landscape historical and cultural meaning.
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587:
楼阁龙云气，苍茫第几峰。
长风万松雨，落月半山钟。
石磴盘空险，僧廊落叶重。
吾皇曾驻跸，千古说蟠龙。
金陵城东谁家窗里。落花一片天上来，随人直渡西江水。楚歌吴语娇不成，似能未能最有情。谢公正要东山妓，携手林泉处处行。

works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: Han Shiyu played the jade flute, his voice was graceful and graceful, and the sound of the flute was full of rhythm. The wind blew beautiful music around Zhongshan, and the mountain valley suddenly sounded like a dragon's roar. If the prince of the Zhou Dynasty heard the sound of the flute, he would definitely stop playing his beloved shengguan. Shi Xiang, who once taught Confucius music, would definitely stop playing his beloved yaoqin. This melodious flute sound traveled across the river on the waves, flying to the ends of the earth and nowhere to be found.
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587: 韩公吹玉笛，倜傥流英音。风吹绕钟山，万壑皆龙吟。王子停凤管，师襄掩瑶琴。馀韵度江去，天涯安可寻。
六代帝王国，三吴佳丽城。贤人当重寄，天子借高名。巨海一边静，长江万里清。应须救赵策，未肯弃侯嬴。

丹浦非乐战，负重切君临。
我皇秉至德，忘己用尧心。
愍兹区宇内，鱼鸟失飞沈。
推毂二崤岨，扬旆九河阴。
超乘尽三属，选士皆百金。
戎车出细柳，饯席樽上林。
命师诛后服，授律缓前禽。
函轘方解带，峣武稍披襟。
伐罪芒山曲，吊民伊水浔。
将陪告成礼，待此未抽簪。

works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: The book is titled "Suiyuan" where he lived, and the story ends in the fifth year of Xianfeng, with about 24,000 words.

works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: Nanjing fell.
works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: The whole poem can be divided into three major paragraphs. The first paragraph describes the destruction of the southern capital and the changes in the country, which is sad. In these poems, the poet chose typical scenes with extremely prosperous colors and tried his best to describe the atmosphere of Kyoto in its heyday, in order to strongly contrast with the tragic aftermath of the disaster that will be described in the following article. The following sixteen sentences are the focus of the entire article.
works.\u76f8\u5173\u4e8b\u4ef6: Xia Wanchun visited the Forbidden City
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587:
朝登建康城，暮宿丹阳道。黄尘落日大江流，紫陌微寒故宫草。
故宫烟雨动新愁，华屋参差成古丘。当时罗绮三千户，何处莺花十二楼？
六代繁华如在眼，真龙不动河山转。春光缥缈景阳宫，月色徘徊芳乐苑。
细雨深宫花落迟，轻风合殿香飘远。花香十里到平康，莫愁歌舞冶游郎。
侠少尽骑青络马，交游争系紫罗囊。鸳鸯两两芙蓉沼，鹦鹉双双玳瑁梁。
一朝景物如反掌，富贵风流竟长往。玄武池边暮雨来，朱雀门前春草长。美女琵琶塞上行，绮构云连空复情。行人夜雨乌衣巷，归雁秋风白下城。登临千载空回首，陈井梁宫竟谁有！游鹿争衔上苑花，流莺自啭台城柳。台城下接孝陵西，无限枝头乌夜啼。杨花风起过江去，薄暮如烟满大堤。

works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: It expresses emotions through scenery and adopts the folk song form of Zhuzhi Ci, which makes the language shallow, affectionate and natural and smooth.
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587:
一
璩床开印翠微间，朝请全稀退食便。
呵殿也堪成韵致，此中官府又神仙。 
二
尚食宫监往直庐，退闲丞相府中居。
貂珰一样中侍常，三日湖头打饭鱼。
三
同姓编氓异姓侯，上公出不辟行驺。
诸曹未识勋臣贵，每到朝陵压上头。 
四
秦淮冬尽不堪观，桃叶官舟阁浅滩。
一夜渡头春水到，家家重漆赤栏干。

works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: The author is a Qing Dynasty aristocrat with a special status and profound vision and thinking.
works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: This nostalgic poem connects the Jingling Tombs and the Twelve Tombs, summarizes the reasons for the rise and fall of the Ming Dynasty, and comments on history in a purely objective manner.
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587: 山色江声共寂寥，十三陵树晚萧萧。中原事业如江左，芳草何须怨六朝。
野花黄叶旧吴宫，六代豪华烛散风。龙虎势衰佳气歇，凤凰名在故台空。市朝迁变秋芜绿，坟冢高低落照红。霸业鼎图人去尽，独来惆怅水云中。

works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: In February of the 16th year of the Yuan Dynasty (1356), Zhu Yuanzhang defeated the Yuan general Hague in quarrying. In March, he took advantage of the victory and attacked Jiqing. He surrendered more than 30,000 Yuan soldiers and won a complete victory. He immediately occupied Moling.
works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: This nostalgic work expresses the poet's thoughts on governance after chaos and his joy at the arrival of a peaceful world.
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587: 秣陵风物近如何？兵后登临感慨多。云净石头秋嶂出，月明淮口暮潮过。离人暗滴青衫泪，商女空怀玉树歌。忽忆旧时骢马客，于今白发老山阿。

works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: Describe the majesty of Jinling and express the sigh of historical changes.
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587:
牛首开天阙，龙冈抱帝宫。
六朝春草里，万井落花中。
访旧乌衣少，听歌玉树空。
如何亡国恨，尽在大江东。

works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: Describe the majesty of Jinling and express the sigh of historical changes.

works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: The poet was dismissed.
works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: It expresses the poet's lament that "it is difficult to master the world".
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587: 经世才难就，田园路欲迷。殷勤将白发，下马照青溪。岁熟田家乐，秋风客自悲。茫茫曲城路，归马日斜时。

works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: The poet was dismissed.
works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: It expresses the poet's lament that "it is difficult to master the world".

works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: The Ming Dynasty was in the midst of internal and external troubles.
works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: Using scenes to express emotions, he not only writes about folk customs but also expresses his own hopes.
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587: 秦淮冬尽不堪观，桃叶官舟阁浅滩。一夜渡头春水到，家家重漆赤栏干。

works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: This poem describes the dusk cloud city gate in Jinling and the setting sun on the sails. It contrasts the sound of drums and horns at night with the Jingyang bell that day. It is extremely implicit and nostalgic for the past and sighs for the present.
works.\u76f8\u5173\u4f5c\u54c1: 《日下旧闻》、《经义考》、《曝书亭诗文集》、《留别岑参兄弟》、《南史·齐认帝纪》
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587: 秣陵城阙暮云封，估客帆樯落日逢。万里星霜沙塞雁，五更风雨掖门松。长江铁锁空千尺，大道朱楼定几重。此夕愁人听鼓角，惊心不似景阳钟。

works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: The game of "fighting grass" has been popular in the Qinhuai area since the Southern Dynasties. The earliest record is in the "Jingchu Age Chronicles" of the Xiaoliang period.
works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: This excellent ancient poem selects many characteristic place names of Jinling and highlights the word "Jinling" in the title, which has a distinctive local color. Using narrative techniques, the luxuriant spring grass and grass-gathering and fighting grass are described in detail and vividly. The poems come in and out of "The Songs of Chu" and "The Book of Songs", they are casually inspired, appropriate and smart, without any sense of gratification, showing profound skill. The poem is not a simple narrative, but contains deep feelings, expressing the women's yearning for love and pursuit of a happy life. The melancholy during this period is actually the shadow cast by feudal ethics on their souls. The whole poem is written freely and smoothly, which can really make people's hearts move.
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587:
乐游范内花初开，结绮楼前春早来。
春色染山还染水，春光衔柳又衔梅。
此时芳草蔞萋长，秦淮女儿多闲想。
闲想玉闺间，罗衣正试单。
芳飙入户吹帷动，巧鸟当窗揽梦残。
因娇丽日长干道，相戏相要斗芳草。
芳草可初齐，茸茸没马蹄。
芳草远如雾，望望迷人步。
将绿将黄不辨名，和烟和雾那知数。
凤凰台上旧时基，燕雀湖边当日路。
结伴踏春春可怜，花气衣香浑作烟。
谁分迟迟独落后，谁能采采不争前。
袅袅桑间路，佳期何暇顾。
悠悠秦淮水，远道不暇思。
空生谢客西堂梦，徒怨湘娥南浦离。
未鸣鶗鴂先愁歇，乍啭仓庚正及时。
正及时，先愁歇，密取畏人窥，疾行防藓滑。
入深翠湿衣，缘高香袭袜。
搴若将何为，束刍欲待谁。
茜红犹胜颊，荑白却惭肌。
薜荔裁衣安可被，菖蒲结带岂堪垂。
盈掬盈襜罗众芳，蛾飞蝶绕满衣裳。
兰皋借作争衡地，惠畹翻为角敌场。
分行花队逐，对垒叶旗张。
花花非一色，叶叶两相当。
君有麻与枲，妾有葛与藟。
君有萧与艾，妾有蘅与芷。
君有合欢枝，妾有相思子。
君有拔心生，妾有断肠死。
臝归若个中，输落阿谁里。
相向无言转自愁，芳坰过雨忽疑秋。
别本辞根何倚托，倾青委绿满郊邱。
虽残已受妍心惜，纵贱曾经纤手摘。
芍药多情且自留，蘼芜有恨从教掷。
人生宠爱几能终，人心安能采时同。
萦愁结念寻归径，接佩连裙趁晚风。
情知朽腐随泥滓，会化深萤入幕中。

works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: The game of "fighting grass" has been popular in the Qinhuai area since the Southern Dynasties. The earliest record is in the "Jingchu Age Chronicles" of the Xiaoliang period.

薜荔裁衣安可被，菖蒲结带岂堪垂。
盈掬盈襜罗众芳，蛾飞蝶绕满衣裳。
兰皋借作争衡地，惠畹翻为角敌场。
分行花队逐，对垒叶旗张。
花花非一色，叶叶两相当。
君有麻与枲，妾有葛与藟。
君有萧与艾，妾有蘅与芷。
君有合欢枝，妾有相思子。
君有拔心生，妾有断肠死。
臝归若个中，输落阿谁里。
相向无言转自愁，芳坰过雨忽疑秋。
别本辞根何倚托，倾青委绿满郊邱。
虽残已受妍心惜，纵贱曾经纤手摘。
芍药多情且自留，蘼芜有恨从教掷。
人生宠爱几能终，人心安能采时同。
萦愁结念寻归径，接佩连裙趁晚风。
情知朽腐随泥滓，会化深萤入幕中。

works.\u65f6\u671f: middle Ming Dynasty; late Ming Dynasty
works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: The poems written during the journey express the homesickness of being in a foreign land.
works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: A lyrical poem about scenery written while passing through Nanjing.
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587: 缥缈孤城见石头，长淮云水自悠悠。孤村柳色连荒驿，两岸芦花隐钓舟。残月微钟京口夜，澹烟疏雨秣陵秋。客中不尽怀乡感，南雁一声双泪流。

works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: During the long journey of thousands of miles, the poet temporarily anchored his boat under the Stone City, overlooking Jinling in the distance, and condensed what he saw, heard and thought into this poem.
works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: The first couplet narrates the story of "stopping" outside Jinling City. Judging from the title, the boat parking location is on the riverside in the west of the city. Facing the ancient stone city, he longed for it but could not reach it. Because we still need to go on an expedition and are "thousands of miles away from our hearts", there is nothing we can do. I had no choice but to look eastward to appreciate the scenery of Jinling. "Autumn Momo" and "Night Xiao Xiao" have a sad color, which contains the sorrow of traveling and the emotion caused by the ancient stone city. Next, he wrote about the river in front of him, with surging waves extending to the south of Qinhuai. The Baoen Temple Tower in the south of the city stood majestically. The poet traced back to the Six Dynasties from the contemporary Baoen Temple Tower, but he did not describe the ruins of the Six Dynasties in detail, but directly wrote about the decline of the Six Dynasties. In these two poems, the river and the solitary tower form a wonderful horizontal and vertical picture, "Thousands of peaks hang down the six dynasties." There is a contrast between the eternity of nature and the vicissitudes of human affairs, which is quite exquisite. The last two sentences follow the above, "The flowers in the backyard of a businesswoman "Music" is adapted from Du Mu's "Po Qinhuai": "Business girls don't know the hatred of the country's subjugation, but they still sing the flowers in the backyard across the river." The poetic meaning points out the reason for the decline of the Six Dynasties, which is that the ruling class is seeking pleasure and immersed in the decadent life of wealth and wealth. .
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587: 秣陵城外暂停桡，无奈离心万里遥。极浦蒹葭秋漠漠，隔淮烟雨夜箫萧。大江迭浪浮孤塔，建业千峰吊六朝。商女后庭花一曲，歌声哀怨旅魂销。

works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: Tian Han led the Nanguo Theater to perform in Nanjing and changed the story of Yan Yujin's lovelorn suicide into "Night on the Qinhuai River" to bring the theater company closer to the Nanjing audience.

works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: Xie Lingyun was finally squeezed out and became the prefect of Yongjia (the seat of government is now Wenzhou, Zhejiang) in the seventh lunar month of 422 AD (the third year of Yongchu). This poem "Neighbors Send Off to Fangshan" is about the specific scenes and ideological activities when he left the imperial capital Jiankang (today's Nanjing, Jiangsu), boarded the ship at Fangshan Pier, about fifty miles east of the capital, and said goodbye to his relatives and friends who were seeing him off. of.
works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: The poem expresses the feeling of parting and the encouragement to friends when the trip was completed, and also reveals the dissatisfaction with one's own political situation. The poet sets off the strong emotion of separation through the description of the declining forest and the autumn moon, and the emotion and scenery are combined more closely and naturally. The words he lifted without saying a word before leaving, and the instructions to friends at the end, were all written with sincerity and sincerity, unlike other poems that have so much mystical flavor and traces of carving. This poem has a total of fourteen sentences. The first four sentences and the middle four sentences each form a small paragraph, and the last six sentences form a self-contained sequence. Each two sentences in these six sentences has a different meaning. The first four sentences say that he will be the county governor, but he cannot bear to be separated from his neighbors because of his old friendship. Next, the author wrote that the ship was about to unmoor and set off. The last six lines, the third paragraph in the whole poem, point out the purpose of saying goodbye to the neighbors, which is the end of the poem. The author's true thoughts and feelings are that he does not want to leave the imperial capital of Jiankang, but he says a lot of pretentious and self-righteous words in the poem; and it is precisely in this pretense of being broad-minded and self-righteous that he reveals his feelings about being forced to He was dissatisfied with having to leave the capital after becoming the county governor.
works.\u76f8\u5173\u4e8b\u4ef6: Xie Lingyun was demoted
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587:
祗役出皇邑，相期憩瓯越。
解缆及流潮，怀旧不能发。
析析就衰林，皎皎明秋月。
含情易为盈，遇物难可歇。
积疴谢生虑，寡欲罕所阙。
资此永幽栖，岂伊年岁别。
各勉日新志，音尘慰寂蔑。

落日淡生烟，波明影碧减。
人来花槛里，酒出板桥边。
杨柳风千树，笙歌月一船。
十年重对比，回首各凄然。

落日淡生烟，波明影碧减。
人来花槛里，酒出板桥边。
杨柳风千树，笙歌月一船。
十年重对比，回首各凄然。

落日淡生烟，波明影碧减。
人来花槛里，酒出板桥边。
杨柳风千树，笙歌月一船。
十年重对比，回首各凄然。

清汉夜昭晳。
扶桑晓陆离。
发歌摐阳下。
建羽朝夕池。
摐金浮水若。
耸跸诏山祗。
一沾九霄露。
藜藿终自知。

works.\u65f6\u671f: middle Ming Dynasty; late Ming Dynasty
works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: Describe the scenery of Linggu Temple.
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587:
山门才人便悠然，十里深松上绿天，
佛刹起扉皆垒嶂，僧寮汲水尽飞泉。

works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: In the 22nd year of Shaoxing (1152), Fan Chengda went from his hometown Suzhou to Jinling (i.e. Jiankang, today's Nanjing, Jiangsu Province) to participate in the imperial examination. On the way, he wrote 12 poems and 15 poems, among which "Egret Pavilion" was one of them. One, it was written when he went to Jinling and was an early work before Fan Cheng became a Dazhong Jinshi.
works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: The most important artistic feature of this poem is euphemism. The whole poem starts from being tired of traveling and impatient of leisure to "relying on the terrace every day", then further talks about climbing the Egret Pavilion, and finally leads to seeing "Huaishan". It is an ordinary journey, without any words about feeling worried about the country, but feeling worried about the times. The will of the country lies within it. This poem is written relatively calmly and objectively, so its meaning is implicit and thought-provoking.
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587: 倦游客舍不胜闲，日日清江见倚阑。少待西风吹雨过，更从二水看淮山。
荻花芦叶老风烟，独上秋城思渺然。白鹭不知如许事，赤乌又隔几何年。六朝往事秦淮水，一笛晚风江浦船。我辈人今竟谁许，只堪渔艇夕阳边。
荻花芦叶老风烟，独上秋城思渺然。白鹭不知如许事，赤乌又隔几何年。六朝往事秦淮水，一笛晚风江浦船。我辈人今竟谁许，只堪渔艇夕阳边。
荻花芦叶老风烟，独上秋城思渺然。白鹭不知如许事，赤乌又隔几何年。六朝往事秦淮水，一笛晚风江浦船。我辈人今竟谁许，只堪渔艇夕阳边。
荻花芦叶老风烟，独上秋城思渺然。白鹭不知如许事，赤乌又隔几何年。六朝往事秦淮水，一笛晚风江浦船。我辈人今竟谁许，只堪渔艇夕阳边。
山殿秋云里，香烟出翠微。客寻朝磬至，僧背夕阳归。下界千门在，前朝万事非。看心兼送目，葭菼自依依。

works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: The first sentence responds to the title and explains the place and event. The sentence "Thousand Years" leads to the legend of Phoenix, and the second sentence "Tide Return" expresses the desolation of the empty river and the lack of Phoenix. The couplets are neatly contrasted, and the use of words such as color, place names, directions, plants, actions, etc. are strict and sophisticated, depicting a scene of luxuriant river grass, blooming apricot flowers, and full of vitality. The neck couplet pointed out that Fenghuang Terrace has a majestic and dangerous terrain and is a battleground for military strategists. This couplet has both reality and fiction, and it shows that from ancient times to the present, the Phoenix Tower is still there, the drums are wailing, and wars are going on.
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587:
千年百尺凤凰台，送尽潮回凤不回。
白鹭北头江草合，乌衣西面杏花开。
龙蟠虎踞山川在，古往今来鼓角哀。
只有谪仙留句处，春风掌管拂蛛煤。
两峰似牛首，高向碧天昂。烟寺千层峙，云梯百级长。涧寒流水细，洞古野藤香。我欲来栖隐，于此筑草堂。

独上藏龙窟，遥瞻踞虎邦。
四天围碧嶂，一气滚寒江。
净贝安禅悦，香灯冷法幢。
顿令尘思尽，高倚白云窗。
晨登瓦官阁，极眺金陵城。钟山对北户，淮水入南荣。漫漫雨花落，嘈嘈天乐鸣。两廊振法鼓，四角吟风筝。杳出霄汉上，仰攀日月行。山空霸气灭，地古寒阴生。寥廓云海晚，苍茫宫观平。
晨登瓦官阁，极眺金陵城。钟山对北户，淮水入南荣。漫漫雨花落，嘈嘈天乐鸣。两廊振法鼓，四角吟风筝。杳出霄汉上，仰攀日月行。山空霸气灭，地古寒阴生。寥廓云海晚，苍茫宫观平。
晨登瓦官阁，极眺金陵城。钟山对北户，淮水入南荣。漫漫雨花落，嘈嘈天乐鸣。两廊振法鼓，四角吟风筝。杳出霄汉上，仰攀日月行。山空霸气灭，地古寒阴生。寥廓云海晚，苍茫宫观平。

works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: The poet is walking on the empty Jilong Mountain. Through the thousands of miles of wind, smoke and slanting light, the dream-like vicissitudes of the Six Dynasties arise spontaneously, reflecting the sadness of the poet's wandering life.
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587: 秋尽荒山鸟迹稀，拂衣独上扣柴扉。屋头鹿下缘青涧，树杪僧行入翠微。千里风烟搔短鬓，六朝文物付斜晖。悠悠身世浑如此，目断天边一雁飞。

works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: Fuzhou Mountain, also known as Xiaojiuhua Mountain, is located on the west side of Taiping Gate in the east of Nanjing City. It is the first hill where the remnants of the Purple Mountain Mountain enter the city from the west. It is separated from the Ming City Wall in the north and adjacent to Xuanwu Lake. There are temples in the mountain and a tower on the top of the mountain. There is a lake beside it and a city at the foot of the mountain.

works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587: 五显神系顾野王五子，萧梁时死节者，庙在聚宝门外西街东，明万历二十八年重修。

works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: This poem was written by the poet when he passed through Jiankang (today's Nanjing) after being captured. It expresses the helplessness and sorrow of the motherland being occupied by foreign enemies.
works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: It mainly writes about the historical status and changes of Jiankang and the misfortune of the people. "The healthy children live in the quiet soil, and the new ghosts cry in the city of Tai" expresses the extremely painful sorrow of the changing times and the touching spirit of sacrifice.
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587:
金陵古会府，南渡旧陪京。
山势犹盘礴，江流已变更。
健儿徒幽土，新鬼哭台城。
一片清溪月，偏于客有情。
潮平远岸草侵沙，东晋衰来最可嗟。庾舅已能窥帝室，王都还是预人家。山寒老树啼风曲，泉暖枯骸动芷牙。欲起九原看一遍，秦淮声急日西斜。

works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: In the spring of the third year of Xiaojian's reign (456), Bao Zhaoqian became a doctor of Taixue and a member of Zhongshushe. Liu Jun loved writing articles and thought that others could not compare with him. Bao Zhao understood his thoughts and wrote articles with many vulgar words and repeated sentences. People say that Bao Zhao is exhausted of his talents and ideas, but this is not the case. In the same season, Bao Zhao wrote poems in Jiankang with Xie Zhuang, the Minister of Civil Affairs, including the poem "Three Connected Sentences with Xie Shangshu Zhuang". At the turn of spring and summer, Bao Zhao took a leave of absence for thirty days due to house repairs, and wrote the article "Request for Leave". Later, Bao Zhao wrote a poem called "Songs and Cypresses" due to illness. Due to his illness and the death of his sister Bao Linghui, he asked for an extended leave of absence for a hundred days and wrote a piece called "Requesting Leave and Starting Again." In autumn, he went up to the tower with Wang Yanxiu, Xun Yuanzhi, Xun Wanqiu and others to admire the moon, and composed a poem called "Moonlight Climbing the Tower". In October, Liu Yuanjing became the Hussar General, and Bao Zhao wrote "For Liu Ling, let the Hussar watch". In winter, Bao Zhao accompanied Emperor Xiaowu on a banquet in Zhoushan, and wrote "Two Poems on a Banquet in Zhoushan" for Liu Yuanjing.
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587:
其一
息雨清上郊，开云照中县。
游轩越丹居，晖烛集凉殿。
凌高跻飞楹，追焱起流宴。
苑含灵群，嵓庭藏物变。
明辉烁神都，丽气冠华甸。
目远幽情周，礼洽深恩遍。
其二
繁霜飞玉闼，爱景丽皇州。
清跸戒驰路，羽盖伫宣游。
神居既崇盛，嵓崄信环周。
礼俗陶德声，昌会溢民讴。
惭无胜化质，谬从云雨浮。

嵩岳基旧宇，盘岭跨南京。睿心重禅室，游驾陟层城。金略徐既动，龙骖跃且鸣。涂方后尘合，地迥前笳清。逦迤因台榭，参差憩羽旌。高随阆风极，势与元天并。气歇连松远，云升秋野平。徘徊临井邑，表里见淮瀛。祈果尊常住，渴慧在无生。
暂留石山轨，欲知芳杜情。
鞠躬荷嘉庆，瞻道闻颂声。

works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: This is a narrative poem. It is the writer pouring out his heartfelt feelings to his friends, and the "small preface" before the words is very clear in describing the story. Friends went boating on Nanhu Lake without asking the author to go with them, so feelings of resentment and admiration arose spontaneously. The author talks in the first person, which is kind and gentle. However, the author does not describe it in a straightforward manner. He still pays attention to the hierarchical structure and expresses an event in daily life with ups and downs. The use of spoken language is varied, sometimes ridiculing, and very distinctive. More importantly, these are used as a foil to remind people of the beauty of Nanhu Lake and the joy of chanting and appreciation. This kind of virtual writing method reflects the author's ingenuity.
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587: 幼霞、鹤巢、诗孙同泛南湖,联吟买醉,未尝召我也。事后更以词夸我,岂复可恕。当待秋中月满花香,更共觞我,倚此檄之。旧游如梦南湖路，华闲也会舣棹。桂楫偕游，松醪共醉，君悲今番吟眺。如何忘了。有善饭廉颇，也堪同调。一榻清风，恁般偏我已堪恼。相夸还更示我，把联吟秀句，取次赍到。字里华香，行闲酒气，险把衰翁熏倒。秋光正好。趁月晓风清，更偕华笑。补过心虔，买舟须要早。
此是晋人游集处，当时风景与今同。不干铁锁楼船力，似是蒲葵麈柄功。几簇旌旗秋色里，百年陵阙泪痕中。兴亡毕竟缘何事，专罪清谈恐未公。

works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: Wang Anshi's mother died of illness and he was dismissed from his post. He returned to Jiangning with the depression of unrealized ambitions. During the nearly three years of mourning, Wang Anshi composed a series of poems and lyrics about Jinling's history and nostalgia.
works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: Through lamenting over the relics of the subjugated country, looking back on the present and recalling the past, we can draw profound historical lessons.
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587:
结绮临春草一丘，尚残宫井戒千秋。
奢淫自是前王耻，不到龙沈亦可羞。

works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: Rouge Well is Jingyang Palace Well, also known as "Shame Well". The dry well where the conquered king Chen Empress and his beloved concubine Zhang Lihua jumped into to hide from the Sui soldiers in 589 AD. Since the Tang Dynasty, Jinling nostalgic poems have repeatedly become the material for ridicule and "poetry" by later generations. Chen Fu's "Rouge Well" is one of the most vivid, satirical and critical songs.
works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: The poet adopts a hard-to-get approach, first "putting himself in the shoes" of the empress and his concubines' hurried and desolate situation, and showing a close-up shot of "tears dripping through the green moss, looking back at the sunset in the palace", as if from their perspective. Look at it: "Thousands of miles of rivers and mountains, the sky does not care, but only one well belongs to the emperor." It is this absurd and iron-clad "fact" that has made it a "historical place" and a laughing stock through the ages: the emperor cannot "manage" the world, and the old man Heaven does not "care" about the Son of Heaven. The poet has a unique insight and clever thinking, and he knows something that no one has ever said before. The "old themes" also come up with "new tricks": finding surprises in the ordinary and sharp warnings.
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587:
泪痕滴透绿苔香，回首宫中已夕阳。
万里河山天不管，只留一井属君王。

井无水，荒龙倚。
不知如，巴马子。
仰天夜见黄姑星，水底喽喽话仰鬼，长绳卷起天河水。
井中人，不殉死，宫人斜在雷塘趾。

南朝三十六英雄，角逐兴亡尽此中。有国有家皆是梦，为龙为虎亦成空。残花旧宅悲江令，落日青山吊谢公。止竟霸图何物在，石麟无主卧秋风。
牛首见鹤林，提劲绕幽岑。春光福山外，天河苏殿阴。传灯无白日，布地有黄金。休作狂歌老，回看不住心。

works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: Describes the fresh and wild scenery seen when visiting Niushou Mountain.
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587:
青山高傍帝城隈，结客相将命酒杯。
石路草香人独往，枫林叶暗鸟频来。
江回素练云边出，岩献名花雨后开。
临眺莫言归去晚，放歌还上夕阳台。

works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: In the first year of Chunxi (AD 1174), he was appointed as the counselor of the Jiangdong Appeasement Department. When he served in Jiankang again, he went to the Shangxin Pavilion again, expressed his feelings about the scenery, poured out his accumulated depression, and wrote this masterpiece that has been passed down through the ages.
works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: The words are divided into upper and lower parts. The first film is what the poet saw and felt when he came to the scene. "Yao Cen Yuan Mu, offering sorrow and hatred, jade hairpin and snail bun." "Yao Cen Yuan Mu" is a condensation of two sentences in Han Yu's "Chengnan Couplet": "Yao Cen brings out an inch of green, and distant eyes gain double brightness." "The sunset above the building, in the sound of the broken bell, the wanderer in the south of the Yangtze River." If the first three sentences are distant views, then the second sentence "Sunset" is the close-up view: the setting sun hangs on the top of the building and is about to sink; the wild geese that have lost their flock Flying across the sky, the sound of mourning added to the miserable atmosphere. "Looking at Wu Gou, I patted the railings all over, but no one knew it, so I came to the scene." This is written after the protagonist appeared on the scene, and conveyed his rich inner world with two actions full of personality characteristics: He looked at Wu Gou He looked again and again, walked around and patted the railings, lamenting that no one understood his "meaning to come." Li He's "Nan Yuan" said: "Why don't men take Wu Gou and collect the fifty states in Guanshan?" Obviously, the two poems are the origin of the word "Wu Gou" in Xin's poem, which expresses the poet's ambition to regain the lost territory and unify the motherland. Ambition. The second film mainly uses a few allusions to express its ideas, and its ideological context is still connected to the previous film. Because "no one understands it, it means ascending", the poet couldn't help but think of many past events of his predecessors. "Don't say that the perch is worthy of being eaten. When the west wind blows, the eagle has not returned yet?" These lines are quoted from "Shishuo Xinyu Shijian". "Ask for the land and ask for the house, but if you are afraid of being embarrassed to see him, Liu Lang is talented." These lines are alluded to in "Three Kingdoms? Wei Zhi? Chen Deng Biography". "Sorrowful about the storm" refers to the writer's worries about the precarious situation of the country and the increasingly critical current situation. "Trees are like this", quoted from "Shishuoxinyu·Yu". "Qianhe is called, with a red scarf and green sleeves, to find the hero's tears." "Qianhe" actually means that there is no one, so the sentence "no one knows" should be used. The poet is lonely. Not only does no one understand him, but he also has no comforter. In the words, the author places his emotions on the east-flowing river and the boundless autumn light, on the slowly setting sun and the sad sunset. He does not express his sorrow but sees his own sorrow, which makes the emotion and scenery reach a wonderful and rootless state.

works.\u76f8\u5173\u4e8b\u4ef6: Xin Qiji visits the Heart-Enhancing Pavilion
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587: 楚天千里清秋，水随天去秋无际。遥岑远目，献愁供恨，玉簪螺髻。落日楼头，断鸿声里，江南游子。把吴钩看了，栏干拍遍，无人会、登临意。休说鲈鱼堪脍。尽西风、季鹰归未？求田问舍，怕应羞见，刘郎才气。可惜流年，忧愁风雨，树犹如此。倩何人、唤取红中翠袖，揾英雄泪？

works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: "One Hundred Odes of Jinling" is a set of seven-character quatrains written by Zeng Ji in the Southern Song Dynasty. It consists of one volume. The whole book is composed of poems reciting Jiankang monuments.
works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: The author cherishes the historical relics and expresses infinite emotion.
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587:
六代兴亡貉一丘，繁华梦逐水东流。
操蛇神向山前笑，三百年前几度舟。

works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: This song "Xihe·Jinling" is a work by Zhou Bangyan in his later years. It was the last years of the Northern Song Dynasty, and the Song Dynasty was in crisis. In the second year of Xuanhe (1120), a peasant uprising led by Fang La broke out in the south. Zhou Bangyan hurriedly traveled from Hangzhou to Yangzhou, Tianchang, and arrived in Nanjing (today's Shangqiu, Henan). He personally realized the great impact of the peasant uprising on the Northern Song Dynasty. Shocked, this can't help but make the poet think about the "motherland" and "isolated city". At the same time, the author was exiled again in his later years, which made the poet lament the vicissitudes of life. These may be the reasons why this poet who writes mostly love and travel poems is so nostalgic.
works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: This is a nostalgic poem by Zhou Bangyan. Facing the "beautiful land" of Jinling, the author witnessed the vicissitudes of nature, which aroused his feelings about the rise and fall of human affairs, expressed his political opinions and philosophical concepts, and showed the author's nostalgia for the past and his feelings. Today's thoughts and feelings.
works.\u76f8\u5173\u4f5c\u54c1: 《金陵五题》、《宋词三百首》、《石头城》、《乌衣巷》、《片玉词》、《莫愁乐》
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587: 佳丽地，南朝盛事谁记。山围故国绕清江，髻鬟对起。怒涛寂寞打孤城，风樯遥度天际。断崖树、犹倒倚，莫愁艇子曾系。空馀旧迹郁苍苍，雾沉半垒。夜深月过女墙来，伤心东望淮水。酒旗戏鼓甚处市？想依稀、王谢邻里，燕子不知何世，入寻常、巷陌人家，相对如说兴亡，斜阳里。

works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: The author was born in the late Tang Dynasty, had a bumpy official career, and witnessed the difficult state affairs, so he used the ancient times to describe the present, and sighed for the late Tang Dynasty.
works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: It describes the poet's climb up to the old stone city in late spring and looked far into the distance, expressing his nostalgia for the past and sadness for the present.
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587: 万里伤心极目春，东南王气只逡巡。野花相笑落满地，山鸟自惊啼傍人。谩道城池须险阻，可知豪杰亦埃尘。太平寺主惟轻薄，却把三公与贼臣。
束发好怡衍，弱冠颇流薄。素想终勿倾，聿来果丘壑。层峰亘天维，旷渚绵地络。逢皋列神苑，遭坛树仙阁。松墱含青晖，荷源煜彤烁。川界泳游鳞，岩庭响鸣鹤。
南朝天子射雉时，银河耿耿星参差。铜壶漏断梦初觉，宝马尘高人未知。鱼跃莲东荡宫沼，濛濛御柳悬栖鸟。红妆万户镜中春，碧树一声天下晓。盘踞势穷三百年，朱方杀气成愁烟。彗星拂地浪连海，战鼓渡江尘涨天。绣龙画雉填宫井，野火风驱烧九鼎。殿巢江燕砌生蒿，十二金人霜炯炯。芊绵平绿台城基，暖色春容荒古陂。宁知玉树后庭曲，留待野棠如雪枝。
