works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: The article is divided into three parts: it begins with the story of how Buddha Moakasyapa received the Buddha's heart seal and the outline of the sect established by Bodhidharma from the east. The story of how Master Cixurong attained enlightenment and received seals and the anecdotes about his "waiting" after his death. At the end of the story, the construction of the new tower is described. The article is completed in one go, and ends with a warning, which is as bright as a pearl.

works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: Zhang Zhu once lived in Jinling and experienced the rise and fall of the Yuan Dynasty, so many of his poems were written about sadness and chaos.
works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: Using scenery to express feelings, through the rendering of scenery with symbolic meaning, it conveys the desolate feeling of despair.
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587:
此地先皇驻六龙，鼎成龙去杳无踪。
御床罗帕尘空暗，辇道朱门草已空。
题榜侍臣留帝敕，筑亭真士记天容。
自怜不及窥弓剑，愁绝寒城响暮钟。

works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: At the end of the Southern Song Dynasty, the north fell, and Yecheng was at the forefront of the struggle against foreigners. When Liu Kezhuang came to Yecheng, facing the situation where the rivers and mountains in the north were unable to recover, he couldn't help but think about it for thousands of years and was worried about it. He couldn't express the stagnation in his heart, so he wrote this poem that mourns the past and hurts the present.
works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: This is a typical nostalgic poem. The first couplet directly inherits the rise of the word "Yecheng"; the chin couplet originates from the "ancient meaning" and turns time and space to the past; the neck couplet returns from history to reality; the last couplet combines nostalgia with severe reality, which suddenly enhances the poem's quality. artistic conception.
works.\u76f8\u5173\u4f5c\u54c1: 《后村先生大全集》、《雨华台》、《新亭》
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587: 断镞遗枪不可求，西风古意满原头。孙刘数子如春梦，王谢千年有旧游。高塔不知何代作，暮笳似说昔人愁。神州只在阑干北，度度来时怕上楼。

works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: The author chose a specific place, time and angle to observe the overall picture and scenic spots, history and reality of Xuanwu Lake. The structure of this poem is "present, ancient, and present": the first and last couplets are "present", and the two middle couplets are "ancient". From the present to the ancient, and from the ancient to the present, people are immersed in the long historical time and space. , and inspires readers to think about the "rise and fall" of history and reality. The smoke, water, sunset, and fishermen's songs in the epilogue have a broad realm and long-lasting emotions, which are quite extraordinary.
works.\u76f8\u5173\u4e8b\u4ef6: Wu Weiye visited Xuanwu Lake
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587: 覆舟西望接陂陀，千顷澄潭长绿莎。六代楼船供士女，百年版籍重山河。平川岂习昆明战？禁地须通太液波。烟水不关兴废感，夕阳闻已唱渔歌。

works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: This poem was probably written in the early winter of the seventh year of Ming Dynasty (463) by Emperor Xiaowu of the Southern Song Dynasty. In the second year of the Ming Dynasty, Bao Zhao was "abandoned and dismissed from office" and wandered north of the Yangtze River. In the sixth year of the Ming Dynasty, he joined the army and joined the army under the command of Liu Zixu, the king of Linhai in Jingzhou (now Jiangling, Hubei Province). This poem was written by Bao Zhao, who came to Beijing for sightseeing as the special envoy of King Linhai, paid a pilgrimage to the emperor, and passed through three mountains. It describes the author's complicated state of mind when he was looking at the country and thinking about his family. Sanshan is a ferry crossing of ancient Jinling. You can board a boat here when leaving the country, or you can abandon the boat and land here when returning to Beijing. But if the author wants to look at Stone City, he has to climb the hillside. The whole poem changes according to rhyme and rhyme, and is roughly divided into three sections: the eight sentences in the first section write "Going to the Three Mountains to Climb and View", the eight sentences in the middle section write "Looking at Stone City", and the last six sentences express the feeling of "returning the capital".
works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: The two sentences "source of spring" and "end of Sichuan" start from a distance, and they are summarized in the sentence structure of flowing water. The springs at the source of the Yangtze River are overflowing and the water is stable. The end of the Yangtze River is rushing with big waves and the color of the water is clear. It shows that the author returned to the capital by boat from Jingzhou. , a feeling of smoothness and stability along the way. The two sentences "morning light" and "xiaoqi" intertextualize, using the word "morning dawn" to indicate the moment of "reaching the three mountains", implying that the poet has a long journey, sailing at night in a boat, and sailing to the three mountains west of Kyoto at dawn, only to see The early morning sunshine covers the sparkling river surface where aquatic animals swim, but there is still white mist gathering among the mountains and dense forests. This is the scene when the sun has just risen. The two sentences "two rivers" and "three mountains" mean that if you look to the north, you will see that the bright Yangtze River is divided into two sections, flowing away away. The three mountains below you are densely forested, arranged one by one, standing on the bank of the river. These two sentences describe the scenery of a river and a mountain, one horizontal and one vertical, one bright and one dark, one beautiful and one majestic, with different styles, forming a unique natural landscape reflecting the rivers and mountains to the west of Jinling City. Anyone who leaves Beijing to the west or comes east to return to the capital will first see the "Two Rivers" and "Three Mountains". At this point, the poet broadens his sight again and looks at the busy scenes of "Southern Sail" and "Beibang" on the Yangtze River illuminated by the morning light. He can only see the sails rising in the south, the poles swinging in the north, or "looking at the higher mountains". It connects Jiangsu and Zhejiang, or "refers to Qihe" and connects to Shandong, which highlights Nanjing's important position as the Yangtze River waterway transportation artery. This scene description uses springs, rivers and rivers as the main image, and the writing is swaying, smooth and elastic. Beautiful. The two sentences "Guanzhi" and "jindai" turn into the joy of viewing the country in "Looking at Stone City". The so-called "guanguo" means viewing the scenic spots and majesty of Kyoto. "Guanzhi" means passes and fortresses; "Heaven" "Yi" refers to Jinling City, the imperial capital. Guandu naturally refers to Stone City, but the word "rao" and the word "hold" obviously include the numerous military fortresses around Jinling City, and in the distance there are passes surrounding Tiandu. For example, there is Anqing in the west, Zhenjiang in the east, Xuzhou in the north, Taihu Lake in the south, etc., forming a network defense circle to protect the imperial capital. The "belt" obviously refers to the Yangtze River, Qinhuai River, Xuanwu Lake, Sanshan, Zhongshan, Stone City, Yaxie City, Dongfu City, etc., surround the capital like a belt. One "circle" and one "embrace", either far or near, symbolize the surrounding pass and fortress surrounding Jinling. The whole world of nine states is like stars surrounding the North Pole, expressing the author's political attitude of respecting the imperial capital. The two sentences "Great Wall" use an exaggerated style to praise the danger of the Stone City Pass as being more dangerous than the world-famous Great Wall. In contrast, , "The Great Wall is not steep and dangerous." The Great Wall is not deep and dangerous, but the craggy stone city is steep and dangerous, like thorn branches, long and sharp, and jagged and astonishing. The two sentences "Zan Lou" focus on describing the stone The tall city towers and city walls attract people. Since the time when Sun Quan of the Eastern Wu Dynasty built the city walls and trained the navy, Stone City's importance to Jiankang and the Southern Dynasty's regime has grown day by day. "Stone City with Tigers" used to be people's view of the dangerous situation here. A vivid summary, Bao Zhao even made his own discovery: The stone city is built on three mountains, close to Tianyi, with Beijing in its lap, and the city walls are rugged. Not only does it sit on the river and lie in a ravine, but its uneven heights are enough to create a strange and dangerous situation. You see The city towers are crowded and towering, and the battlements stretch out to reach the clouds. It is enough to defeat the enemy and brave the capital, and defend the country from stubborn invaders. The stone city is the screen guard of the capital. The beautiful scenery makes people happy, the shape is inspiring, and the long journey How can a returning wanderer not be excited? Bao Zhao said: "A conqueror likes to see the country, but a wandering man is late to see home. "What does "chi" mean? "If you want to be fast and think of the other as slow, you are called Chi" ("Zhengyun"). I want to go faster, faster, but always think it is too slow, too slow. This is called "late". The word "chi" vividly expresses the wanderer's eagerness to go home. In short, the two sentences "Zheng Hu" are a link between the previous chapter and the following chapter, using the words "xi" and "chi" to awaken the poet's complex feelings about the country. The middle section contains narratives and discussions. The main image of the scenery is composed of gate towers and battlements. The brushstrokes are majestic and powerful, reflecting the strong beauty of Stone City. "Lianlian" and "误躅" are followed by "viewing the country" and "seeing home" to express feelings. The sentence "Lian Lian" refers to the performance of "Ren Jing Yin", which is the ancient "Enter the Dynasty Song", to express majesty; the sentence "Wandering" refers to singing a song about hometown to express the song of the heart. "Liu Lian" and "Wander around" are a pair of two-tone continuous words, which not only add to the rhythmic beauty of the poem, but also subtly convey the poet's complex psychology after "abandoning his official position" and "resigning from home to join the army". It is worth noting that "wangxiang", which is compared with "renjing", contains rich connotations. The so-called "hometown songs" refer to poems that express the feeling of homesickness. There are many excellent works since ancient times. From the perspective of the poetic connotation, it is probably based on the poetic meaning of "Standing and looking at my hometown, Gu Ying is pitiful and self-pity" from Lu Ji's "Going to Luodaozhong to Compose". The poet wanders alone and sighs about his hometown, implicitly revealing the pain of being frustrated in his official career, melancholy and self-pity. The four lines of "Mi Qian" lament the hard work of returning to the capital. The further we go, the more we lament the rush of time. The closer we get to the imperial capital, the more we feel the fatigue of the long waterway. It's still okay to travel "together with chariot servants" Such a lament about the hardships and dangers of the journey, then what will happen if you are alone on the broad and vast journey? Here the poet expresses the feelings of loneliness at the end of the year, the passing of time, and the difficulty of life's journey, and he expresses the joy of observing the country. The off-key riff creates a frustrated and sad aftertaste.
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587:
泉源安首流，川末澄远波。
晨光被水族，晓气歇林阿。
两江皎平迥，三山郁骈罗。
南帆望越峤，北榜指齐河。
关扃绕天邑，襟带抱尊华。
长城非壑岭，峻阻似荆芽
攒楼贯白日，摛堞隐丹霞。
征夫喜观国，游子迟见家。
流连入京引，踯躅望乡歌。
弥前叹景促，逾近倦路多。
偕萃犹如兹，弘易将谓何?

泉源安首流，川末澄远波。

works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: Zhou Bangyan spent his summer vacation at Gushe Pavilion in Zhongshan, fifteen miles east of Lishui County, and composed the poem "Ge Pu Lian".
works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: The first part of the work depicts the unforgettable and quiet scene around Gushe Pavilion in midsummer, rendering the tranquility and interest of the summer forest in the mountains. The next part describes the place where the poet lives. In a quiet environment, the author sleepwalks in his hometown, showing a feeling of homesickness.
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587:
新篁摇动翠葆。曲径通深窈。夏果收新脆，金丸落、惊飞鸟。浓霭迷岸草。蛙声闹。骤雨鸣池沼。
水亭小。浮萍破处，檐花帘影颠倒。纶巾羽扇，困卧北窗清晓。屏里吴山梦自到。惊觉。依然身在江表。
惊飞燕子魂无定，荒洲坠如残叶。树影疑人，鸮声幻鬼，欹侧春冰途滑。颓云万叠。又雨击寒沙，乱鸣金铁。似引宵程，隔溪磷火乍明灭。江间奔浪怒涌，断笳时隐隐，相和呜咽。野渡舟危，空村草湿，一饭芦中凄绝。孤城雾结。剩罥网离鸿，怨啼昏月。险梦愁题，杜鹃枝上血。
海棠才试春光小，西风便吹秋去。白石粼粼，丹林点点，装缀东皋南浦。清游顿阻。谩空有园林，可无钟鼓。一曲庭花，隔江谁与问商女。离怀浑似梦里，碧云犹冉冉，佳人何处。越岫鸡盟，秦楼燕约，争奈年华已暮。凭高吊古。算只有梅花，伴人凄楚。极目天长，淡霞明断雨。

works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: Tang Tao climbed up Jingshan Mountain on October 18, 1944. Looking south from the central pavilion, what he saw was: "The buildings are decorated with red doors, red bricks and green tiles. They are neat and a little deserted, and it already looks a little bit in decline." ( Tang Tao's "Ten Days in the Imperial City") Beiping in 1944 was still the old capital that fell. Although it was a sunny day when we visited Jingshan, the Forbidden City with its golden glazed tiles still looked "deserted" and "declined" in Tang Tao's eyes, which was different from the "splendid" and "dazzling" that Zhao Qingge praised. The author's concern for the country is scattered in the text.

works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: Wu Mei talked about Chongjiudeng Huomeng Tower and filled out a piece of "Qitian Le", and suggested that Xia Chengtao also fill in a piece.
works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: The upper part of this poem focuses on the nostalgic feeling of climbing up the building and looking into the distance. Use allusion in the first sentence. The second sentence describes the conversation between the guest and the host. The second part focuses on lyricism. This poem has the style of Zhou Bangyan's poems, which is solemn and elegant, uses repeated allusions, is euphemistic and tortuous, and is consistent with the theme.
works.\u76f8\u5173\u4e8b\u4ef6: Xia Chengtao and Wu Mei ascended the Huomeng Tower
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587: 过江人物余钟阜，湖天向谁危坐？胜日清尊，浮生笑口，忍话異州兵火。东篱败朵，倘会得悠然，看山犹可。一片斜阳，谁教哀角又吹破。袖中远游章句，石城艇子曲，肠断难和。语鹤关河，听鹃声世，衮衮衣冠江左。青山笑我，甚酒阵歌围，来吟哀些。来日聊城,看谁飞一笴。

谁怜材大难为用，宫商腹中空抱。野雉声销，长鲸睡熟，叹想昔撞人杳。何年拽倒？被牧坚讥评，菜佣调笑。不去书铭，一畦风雨卧秋草。
环天和乐待奏，听其声大远，响彻云表。铸慕凫坚，潜嗟龙哑，惨绿斑斑苔老。知音世少。慨踏破荒烟，我来凭吊。几度摩挲，夕阳人影小。

谁怜材大难为用，宫商腹中空抱。野雉声销，长鲸睡熟，叹想昔撞人杳。何年拽倒？被牧坚讥评，菜佣调笑。不去书铭，一畦风雨卧秋草。
环天和乐待奏，听其声大远，响彻云表。铸慕凫坚，潜嗟龙哑，惨绿斑斑苔老。知音世少。慨踏破荒烟，我来凭吊。几度摩挲，夕阳人影小。
雪映山眉紫，烟消树顶圆。扪萝欲上翠峰颠。忽堕半空铃语、石坛寒。径仄双崖并，泉枯落叶填。一绳雁底小江天。只在夕阳影里、倚阑看。

works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: This is a piece of mourning. There is a small preface before the poem: "Han Gao is like an imperial ancestor, and he spent his whole life with little writing, so it is like this. It is Mr. Chen who was the supervisor of Riling." What this poem expresses is not just ordinary nostalgia. The feeling is obviously filled with the feeling of injustice that talents are not appreciated and people are down and out. The first four sentences are the author's personal situation, and the last four sentences are the memory of Taizu's career and the emotion of being born at the wrong time. While containing strong dissatisfaction with the indistinguishability of the wise from the foolish in the real society, the poet has high self-expectations. The whole poem is full of melancholy emotions, bright language, and intriguing meaning.
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587:
汉高仿佛皇祖，而以少文终其身，故五云然。是日陵监略陈先事。
二百年来一老生，白头落魄到西京。
疲驴狭路愁官长，破帽青衫拜孝陵。
亭长一杯终马上，桥山万岁始龙迎。
当时事业难身遇，冯仗中官说与听。

works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: This poem, from the eyes of a wanderer far away from home, describes the touching scene of the lights on the towers, red foil and purple flutes on the banks of the Qinhuai River in the late Northern Song Dynasty. You can imagine the temporary stability and prosperity back then.
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587:
官柳动春条，秦淮生暮潮。
楼台见新月，灯火上双桥。
隔岸开朱箔，临风弄紫箫。
谁怜远游子，心旆正摇摇。

works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: Describe the quiet environment of Zutang Mountain built in the deep mountains.

works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587: 长廊卷幔得闲凭，南国秋容望不胜。香阁梵音传远磬，石幢寒影护悬灯。山深疑有长生药，寺古应多入定僧。人语忽然飘下界，始知身在白云层。
萋萋野草带斜晖，江左风流事总非。燕子不归门巷改，行人还说旧乌衣。

白云红树路纡萦，古殿长廊次第行。
南望水连桃叶渡，北来山枕石头城。
一尘不到心源净，万有俱空眼界清。
竹院逢僧旧曾识，旋披禅衲为相迎。

works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: After Bai Pubu lived in Jinling, he indulged in landscapes and expressed his feelings about life. He lingered in the ancient capital of Jinling, admiring the vicissitudes of ancient and modern times, and embodying the illusive feeling of life in his poems about the history and nostalgia for the past.
works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: The poet goes against the traditional concept of women corrupting the country, but expresses his pity and sympathy for a beautiful woman who was killed when the dynasty fell, and then expresses his helplessness and sentimentality about the fleeting beauty of things. Shining light of human concern.
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587:
霜水明秋，霞天送晚，画出江南江北。满目山围故国，三阁余香，六朝陈迹。有庭花遗谱，惨哀音，令人嗟惜。想当时，天子无愁，自古佳人难得。
惆怅龙沉宫井，石上啼痕，犹点胭脂红湿。去去天荒地老，流水无情，落花狼藉。恨青溪犹在，渺重城，烟波空碧。对西风，谁与招魂，梦里行云消息。
昔年展诵青邱诗，天界佛寺名乃知。洪武创业造此寺，徵辟名士始居之。我游金陵十一次，今年始获到此地。壁破犹存龙虎文，门扃疑匿狐狸魅。草深觅路问村娃，老僧赤脚迎供茶。小树扶疏也檐宇，满阶不见辛夷花。

works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: There are two good stories about Xu Mian that have been passed down through the ages. One is that during a night gathering with his disciples, a Yu Song asked him for an official position. There is a sigh of "selflessness". The second is that he has been in a prominent position for more than thirty years (Xu Mian was in Liangguan, Shangshu Pushe, and Zhongshu Ling), but "the family has no savings." Therefore, I always tried to persuade him to gather wealth, but he replied: "People will leave money to their descendants. I will leave it to my children in a clean way." - This is really worthy of being cast into stone! Perhaps it is because of being "selfless", not greedy and open-minded, that Xu Mian's poems are often written in a graceful and peaceful way, as quiet as the breeze. "Medan Chu Xinting Zhu Shi" has this characteristic. This poem was written in the Qi Dynasty, when the poet was serving as the capital of the Linhai King's Office. It was still early morning in spring, and the poet was already driving on Xinting Road in Jiankang and Xin'an. Also traveling with him in "Jie Zhen" was the poet Xie Tiao, who wrote the famous line "The remaining sleet scattered into Qi, the clear river is as quiet as practice". After reading the complete poem, we can see that what this poem mainly describes is the scenery seen when driving to Shintei Nagisa. The poet's joy seems to be caused by this beautiful scenery. Perhaps, a landscape is a mood. Scenery is a reflection of each person's character and taste. They will each have a different state due to their different interests. Xu Mian's poem seems to be a reflection of his character and taste. As mentioned before, the poet is an honest and upright official. Therefore, when he viewed the scenery of the new pavilion, the images he took in, whether it was mountain flowers reflecting the sun, mist in the maple forest, or jagged yellow birds, all contained a state of leisure, tranquility, and elegance, forming a kind of A refreshing and pleasant place of comfort. In this poetic realm, we feel the poet's peaceful and elegant mood. From this point of view, Xu Mian's poem is very close to the poetic style of Tao Yuanming from Jin Dynasty.
works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: The whole poem begins with the brisk rhythm of "Driving Ling Zao Shu (referring to the road in the suburbs)". The word "Ling" describes the speed of the carriage and horse, which corresponds to the poet's enthusiasm for traveling and expresses the joy of the morning breeze blowing on his face. Then a gorgeous scene came into view: the mountain roadside was flourishing, and there were endless wildflowers in bloom. When illuminated by the rays of the "first day", they all shone brightly. Because this is the visual image of carriages and horses traveling, the sentence "Mountains and flowers reflect the early sun" not only has the effect of grazing colors and mountain flowers reflecting up and down, but also has an endless and dizzying sense of movement. How can we enjoy such beautiful scenery while running in a hurry? The poet therefore quickly "grabbed the bridle" and allowed the carriage and horse to move slowly here. Unexpectedly, I discovered that I was now on the river bank, overlooking the clear river water, flowing peacefully in the morning light. The two sentences "holding the bridle and wandering around" describe the scene outside the body. It expresses the poet's specific state of mind at this time: the word "qi" shows the leisure and contentment of adapting to the situation; the word "complex value" floats the surprise and joy of unintentionally facing the tranquil river flow - - It is really affectionate and can be reached, the scene makes people feel excited, the words are calm and the realm is immediately revealed. Now, the poet has set his sights on the distant hillside. There is a secluded maple forest there, from which gray morning mist floats out from time to time, giving it a leisurely feeling as if "the clouds are unintentionally coming out of the clouds". And in the glow, the yellow birds that were playing with each other suddenly flew by, and their melodious chirping seemed to tell the poet the joy of "all the birds are happy to have someone to trust". The two sentences "The maple forest is full of mist, and the yellow birds are jagged", one is still and the other is moving, showing the transformation of the scene of the poet looking up from a distance; on the quiet background of the maple forest in the early morning light, the bright and jagged feathers of the yellow bird's wings are dotted The color creates a contrasting color contrast and a sound effect where there is silence. Shintei Nagisa in the morning light is shown to be both silent and full of vitality. In such a picturesque environment, how can the poet not be so excited? If this beautiful scene was just a poet traveling alone, it would naturally seem a bit deserted. But readers should be aware that traveling with Xu Mian was the poet Xie Tiao, who had a close affinity with him. He was a great man with a "spiritual heart, a beautiful mouth" and a "brilliant elegance that reflected people"! How much fun would it be for two people of the same age to travel together hand in hand, singing and poetry to each other when the mood strikes? The two sentences "Qi and things are like this" describe exactly the poet's state of mind. "Heart expectancy" is added with "heavy", which shows that the poet's joy lies not only in the beauty of "Qiwu", but also in the joy of looking forward to each other with his friends. No wonder the poem's conclusion changes from the leisurely and gentle attitude of the previous article, and finally sings "A spring embankment spreads out, and the final meaning is clear". "Xing" means enjoying one's leisure, while "Xie" means "satisfaction". Not to mention how comfortable I was during this trip to Shintei Nagisa, my mood was so full and joyful the whole morning! Although these two sentences are written on plain paper. Readers may wish to imagine this as the poet's words to his family when he returns. The meaning is still unfinished, and the dancing state is faintly visible at the end of the pen.
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587:
驱车凌早术，山华映初日。
揽辔且徘徊，复值清江谧。
杳霭枫树林，参差黄鸟匹。
气物宛如斯，重以心期逸。
春堤一游衍，终朝意殊悉。

works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: This poem describes the distant view of Zhongshan Mountain with a straight pen. Such as "the wild tree is short" and "the woodcutter is thin" and so on, deliberately seeking similar shapes. Compared with the poetry of the Tang and Song Dynasties, it also has a childish taste.
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587:
冠者五六人，携手岩之际。
散意百仞端，极目千里睇。
叠岫乍昏明，浮云时卷闭。
遥看野树短，远望樵人细。

works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: The poet climbed the tower and looked into the distance, thinking about the historical events of Jinling over the past four hundred years.
works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: The author's helplessness and hidden worries about the current situation are implicitly expressed between the lines of the poem's lament about the past.
works.\u76f8\u5173\u4f5c\u54c1: 《入朝曲》、《登金陵凤凰台》、《滕王阁序》
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587: 自古帝王州，郁郁葱葱佳气浮。四百年来成一梦，堪愁。晋代衣冠成古丘。绕水恣行游。上尽层城更上楼。往事悠悠君莫问，回头。槛外长江空自流。

works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: Criticize the atrocities committed by officers and soldiers against the common people.
works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: By describing the story of a Lanling girl who dared to fight to the death against a general who kidnapped her, it exposed the darkness of society at that time, lashed out at the atrocities committed by officers and soldiers, and expressed the poet's sympathy for the ravaged people and his indignation against the officials who acted recklessly.
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587: 将军既解宣州围，铙歌一路行如飞。行行东至濑水上，乃营金屋安玉扉。步障十重列纨绮，流苏百结垂珠玑，天吴紫凤贴地满，珊瑚玉树灯相辉。灵蠵之柈大蠡盏，椒花酿熟羊羔肥。坐中貂锦半时贵，眼下繁华当世稀。道是将军毕婚礼．姬姜旧聘今于归。兰陵道远蹇修往，春水吴船凭指挥。良辰风日最明媚，雪消沙暖晴波翠。双桥儿女竞欢声，新年梅柳酣春意。卓午遥闻鼓吹喧，前津已报夫人至。将军含笑下阶行，众客无声环堵侍。彩船刚舣将军门，船中之女隼入而猱奔。结束雅素谢雕饰，神光绰约天人尊，若非瑶池陪辇之贵主，定是璇宫宵织之帝孙。颀身屹以立，玉貌惨不愠。敛袖向众客：“来此堂者皆高轩，我亦非化外，从头听我分明言：我是兰陵宦家女，世乱人情多险阻。一母而两兄，村舍聊僻处。前者冰畦自灌蔬，将军过之屡延伫。提瓮还家急闭门，曾无一字相尔汝。昨来两材官，金币溢筐筥。谓有赤绳系，我母昔口许，兹用打桨迎，期近慎勿拒。我兄稍谁何，大声震柱础。露刃数十辈，狼虎纷伴侣。一呼遽坌集，户外骇行旅。其势殊讧讧，奋飞难远举。我如不偕来，尽室惊魂无死所。我今已偕来，要问将军此何语?”女言缕缕中肠焚，突前一手揕将军，一手有剑欲出且未出：“我言是真是假汝耳闻不闻?我惟捉汝姑苏去，中丞台下陈诉所云云。请为庶入上达尧舜君，古来多少名将钟鼎留奇芬，一切封侯食邑赐钱赐绢种种国恩外，是否听其劫掠良闺弱息为策勋?诏书咫尺下五云，万一我嫁汝，汝意岂不欣?不有天子命，断断不能解此纷。汝如怒我则杀我，譬如幺幺细琐扑落粪土一蚤蚊。不则我以我剑夺汝命，五步之内颈血立溅青纯裙。门外长堤无数野棠树，树下余地明日与筑好色将军坟。一生一死速作计，奚用俯首不语局促同斯文?”将军平日叱咤雷车殷，两臂发石无虑千百斤，此时面目灰死纹，赪如中酒颜熏熏。帐下健儿腾恶氛，握拳透爪齿咬龈。将军在人手，仓猝不得分，投鼠斯忌器，无计施戈矜。将军左右摇手挥其群，目视众客似乞片语通殷勤。众客惊甫定，前揖女公子：“聆女公子言，怒发各上指。要之将军心，始愿不到此。求婚固有之，篡取敢非理。卤莽不解事，罪在使人耳。若两材官者，矫命必重箠。如今无他言，仍送还乡里。将军亲造门，肉袒谢万死。敬奉不腆仪，堂上佐甘旨。事过如烟云，太空本无滓。请即回舟行，食言如白水!”女视众客笑且颦：“诸君视我黄口侲。彼今大失望，野性讵肯驯?山魖寻仇雠，蓄念愈不仁。慨从军兴来，处处兵杀民。杀民当杀贼，流毒滋垓垠。兰陵官道上，若辈来往频。不在霜之夕，则在雨之晨。我家数间屋，猎猎原上薪，我家数口命，惨惨釜内鳞。弹指起风波，转眼成灰尘。与其种后祸，终作衔哀磷。阎罗知有无，夜台冤谁伸?何如叫九重，天必无私纶。或竟辣手作，公论自有真。明知我此来，螳斧当巨轮。宁犹计瓦全，惜此区区身?诸君调停词，蔓甚我弗遵。”众客更前揖：“请勿变色瞋。将军负贤名，毛羽夙所珍，壹意希儒风，裘带殊恂恂。此举大不韪，一旦传闻新，万口鸣不平，可知詈申申，恶声来有由，欲辨难鼓唇。白璧自污之，罔值钱一缗。悔过方不遑，恨无障面巾。江东诸父老，相见惭相亲，况敢犯众怒，兴戎自婚姻。得罪名教尽，不复能为人。斯人非寻常，四方战贼多苦辛，大才虽非管乐匹，英风犹自奢颇伦。女公子既世家裔，幸为朝廷宽假熊罴臣。他日之事愿以百口保，某也官府某也乡缙绅。”翕然长跪代请命：“惟女公子为仙为佛为天神!”女知众客意难拂，乃曰“我为诸君屈，诸君前说姑置之，我与诸君借一物。我闻彼有善马名白鱼，日行千里犹徐徐。我之发兰陵，辞家计已四日余。老母痛苦常倚闾，两兄中庭握手空唏嘘。若乘此马归到家，可及今日日落初。自今我亦弃敝庐，卜邻别有秦人墟，桃花林中奉板舆，从兄去读黄石书，武陵隔绝痴儿渔。三日五日间，我既迂所居。秣陵蒋尉祠，归马其何如?将军此马不数驭，至此惟恐女不去。急呼从者牵马前，四足霏霜耳披絮。女一顾此马，眉宇色差豫。撒手始释将军衣，身未及腾鞍已据。一声长谢破空行，电掣星流不知处。女行数日军无骚，将军振旅胆气豪。钟山之旁营周遭，宾僚迎拜将军劳。斗酒劝釂新蒲萄，钲笳杂奏声欢呶。云中匹马尘甚嚣，清光无恙来滔滔，千金一诺券果操，将军迎絷归其曹。马汗如血长嘶号，背上有物臃肿拳曲纵横束缚三尺高，乃是材官当日将去之聘礼，封还不失分厘毫。聘礼脱尽处，薤叶多一刀，刀光摇摇其锋能吹毛。将军坐此几日夜睡睡不牢。

works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: Criticize the atrocities committed by officers and soldiers against the common people.
works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: By describing the story of a Lanling girl who dared to fight to the death against a general who kidnapped her, it exposed the darkness of society at that time, lashed out at the atrocities committed by officers and soldiers, and expressed the poet's sympathy for the ravaged people and his indignation against the officials who acted recklessly.
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587: 将军既解宣州围，铙歌一路行如飞。行行东至濑水上，乃营金屋安玉扉。步障十重列纨绮，流苏百结垂珠玑，天吴紫凤贴地满，珊瑚玉树灯相辉。灵蠵之柈大蠡盏，椒花酿熟羊羔肥。坐中貂锦半时贵，眼下繁华当世稀。道是将军毕婚礼．姬姜旧聘今于归。兰陵道远蹇修往，春水吴船凭指挥。良辰风日最明媚，雪消沙暖晴波翠。双桥儿女竞欢声，新年梅柳酣春意。卓午遥闻鼓吹喧，前津已报夫人至。将军含笑下阶行，众客无声环堵侍。彩船刚舣将军门，船中之女隼入而猱奔。结束雅素谢雕饰，神光绰约天人尊，若非瑶池陪辇之贵主，定是璇宫宵织之帝孙。颀身屹以立，玉貌惨不愠。敛袖向众客：“来此堂者皆高轩，我亦非化外，从头听我分明言：我是兰陵宦家女，世乱人情多险阻。一母而两兄，村舍聊僻处。前者冰畦自灌蔬，将军过之屡延伫。提瓮还家急闭门，曾无一字相尔汝。昨来两材官，金币溢筐筥。谓有赤绳系，我母昔口许，兹用打桨迎，期近慎勿拒。我兄稍谁何，大声震柱础。露刃数十辈，狼虎纷伴侣。一呼遽坌集，户外骇行旅。其势殊讧讧，奋飞难远举。我如不偕来，尽室惊魂无死所。我今已偕来，要问将军此何语?”女言缕缕中肠焚，突前一手揕将军，一手有剑欲出且未出：“我言是真是假汝耳闻不闻?我惟捉汝姑苏去，中丞台下陈诉所云云。请为庶入上达尧舜君，古来多少名将钟鼎留奇芬，一切封侯食邑赐钱赐绢种种国恩外，是否听其劫掠良闺弱息为策勋?诏书咫尺下五云，万一我嫁汝，汝意岂不欣?不有天子命，断断不能解此纷。汝如怒我则杀我，譬如幺幺细琐扑落粪土一蚤蚊。不则我以我剑夺汝命，五步之内颈血立溅青纯裙。门外长堤无数野棠树，树下余地明日与筑好色将军坟。一生一死速作计，奚用俯首不语局促同斯文?”将军平日叱咤雷车殷，两臂发石无虑千百斤，此时面目灰死纹，赪如中酒颜熏熏。帐下健儿腾恶氛，握拳透爪齿咬龈。将军在人手，仓猝不得分，投鼠斯忌器，无计施戈矜。将军左右摇手挥其群，目视众客似乞片语通殷勤。众客惊甫定，前揖女公子：“聆女公子言，怒发各上指。要之将军心，始愿不到此。求婚固有之，篡取敢非理。卤莽不解事，罪在使人耳。若两材官者，矫命必重箠。如今无他言，仍送还乡里。将军亲造门，肉袒谢万死。敬奉不腆仪，堂上佐甘旨。事过如烟云，太空本无滓。请即回舟行，食言如白水!”女视众客笑且颦：“诸君视我黄口侲。彼今大失望，野性讵肯驯?山魖寻仇雠，蓄念愈不仁。慨从军兴来，处处兵杀民。杀民当杀贼，流毒滋垓垠。兰陵官道上，若辈来往频。不在霜之夕，则在雨之晨。我家数间屋，猎猎原上薪，我家数口命，惨惨釜内鳞。弹指起风波，转眼成灰尘。与其种后祸，终作衔哀磷。阎罗知有无，夜台冤谁伸?何如叫九重，天必无私纶。或竟辣手作，公论自有真。明知我此来，螳斧当巨轮。宁犹计瓦全，惜此区区身?诸君调停词，蔓甚我弗遵。”众客更前揖：“请勿变色瞋。将军负贤名，毛羽夙所珍，壹意希儒风，裘带殊恂恂。此举大不韪，一旦传闻新，万口鸣不平，可知詈申申，恶声来有由，欲辨难鼓唇。白璧自污之，罔值钱一缗。悔过方不遑，恨无障面巾。江东诸父老，相见惭相亲，况敢犯众怒，兴戎自婚姻。得罪名教尽，不复能为人。斯人非寻常，四方战贼多苦辛，大才虽非管乐匹，英风犹自奢颇伦。女公子既世家裔，幸为朝廷宽假熊罴臣。他日之事愿以百口保，某也官府某也乡缙绅。”翕然长跪代请命：“惟女公子为仙为佛为天神!”女知众客意难拂，乃曰“我为诸君屈，诸君前说姑置之，我与诸君借一物。我闻彼有善马名白鱼，日行千里犹徐徐。我之发兰陵，辞家计已四日余。老母痛苦常倚闾，两兄中庭握手空唏嘘。若乘此马归到家，可及今日日落初。自今我亦弃敝庐，卜邻别有秦人墟，桃花林中奉板舆，从兄去读黄石书，武陵隔绝痴儿渔。三日五日间，我既迂所居。秣陵蒋尉祠，归马其何如?将军此马不数驭，至此惟恐女不去。急呼从者牵马前，四足霏霜耳披絮。女一顾此马，眉宇色差豫。撒手始释将军衣，身未及腾鞍已据。一声长谢破空行，电掣星流不知处。女行数日军无骚，将军振旅胆气豪。钟山之旁营周遭，宾僚迎拜将军劳。斗酒劝釂新蒲萄，钲笳杂奏声欢呶。云中匹马尘甚嚣，清光无恙来滔滔，千金一诺券果操，将军迎絷归其曹。马汗如血长嘶号，背上有物臃肿拳曲纵横束缚三尺高，乃是材官当日将去之聘礼，封还不失分厘毫。聘礼脱尽处，薤叶多一刀，刀光摇摇其锋能吹毛。将军坐此几日夜睡睡不牢。

春流半绕凤凰台。十年花月夜、汛金杯。玉箫鸣咽画船开。清风起，移櫂上秦淮。
客梦五更回。清砧迎塞雁、渡江来。景阳宫井断苍苔。无人处，秋雨落宫槐。

works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: During Bai Pu's growth, there was a person who had a very important influence on him, and this was Yuan Haowen. After Nanjing was captured in 1233, there were cries of murder. Yuan Haowen escaped from the capital with Bai Pu, a child prodigy whom he regarded as "Yuan Bai is connected with the old family, and all the gentlemen are only Ru Xian". Since then, Bai Pu has had a long life. Live with him for a while. Bai Pu was smart and eager to learn since he was a child. At this time, he had the careful guidance of the university scholar Jiayuan Haowen. As a result, his studies made rapid progress, and he was already outstandingly talented and famous in his teens. However, he was born in troubled times, and the pain of losing his mother at an early age was still lingering in his chest. Therefore, he had no intention of making a great contribution and refused the strong recommendation of his friends. He only devoted himself to poetry, drinking, and enjoying the mountains and rivers. This choice of life path deprived the world of a cautious official, but gave birth to a literary master who remains famous to this day. Bai Pu traveled around various places, including Dadu (today's Beijing) twice, and also visited Shuntian (today's Baoding), Shouchun (today's Shouxian, Anhui), Huaizhou (today's Qinyang, Henan) and other places. He wandered in poetry and wine with many talented people and celebrities, most of whom were famous drama or sanqu writers at that time, and it was at this time that Bai Pu's creation began.
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587: 望参差、冶城烟树。故人知在琳宇。绣衣来就论文饮，随意割鸡炊黍。欢乐处。忘尔汝。清谈况有神仙侣。一杯缓举。放远目增明，遥岑出翠，俯仰几今古。红尘梦，不到丹台紫府。寻真偶得佳趣。两株翠柏参天起，千亩渭川烟雨。君已许。向此地结亭，为我开窗户。朝来暮去。待细揽烟霞，平分风月，挥洒锦囊句。

works.\u65f6\u671f: Late Ming Dynasty; Early Qing Dynasty

works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587: 金粉飘零野草新，女墙日夜枕寒津。兴亡莫漫悲前事，淮水而今尚姓秦。
金粉飘零野草新，女墙日夜枕寒津。兴亡莫漫悲前事，淮水而今尚姓秦。
南朝词臣北朝客，归来唯见秦淮碧。池台竹树三亩馀，至今人道江家宅。

works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: Wang Anshi came to the Yangtze River and watched the changing winds and thousands of sails on the vast river. He created Qijue "On the River" to express his tranquil and tranquil mood.
works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: The first two sentences of the poem describe the scene seen while boating on the river. The next two sentences turn from the clouds to the green mountains by the river. The twists and turns of the green mountains seem to be blocking the poet's way forward. When the poet was wondering, the flickering shadows of sails in the distance came into view. This was an exciting and exciting picture.
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587:
江北秋阴一半开，晓云含雨却低回。
青山缭绕疑无路，忽见千帆隐映来

works.\u65f6\u671f: Late Ming Dynasty; Early Qing Dynasty
works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: Sheshan Mountain and Qixia Temple are famous landscapes in Nanjing. The author uses cyan as the main color, and the stone surface is either cracked or dyed, showing the volume of its backward and solid blocks and the characteristics of the mountains. The large blank space in the picture cleverly expresses the misty and ethereal clouds. At the same time, it blends with the layers The layers of dyed rocks and trees echo the density, enhancing the changes in virtual and solid levels.

works.\u65f6\u671f: Late Ming Dynasty; Early Qing Dynasty
works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: Feeling nostalgic for my homeland when I'm in the scene.
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587: 断碣残碑谁勒铭，六朝还见草青青。天高风急雁归塞，江回月明人倚亭。忆昔覆亡城已没，到今荒僻路难经。春衣湿尽伤心泪，赢得渔歌一曲听。

works.\u65f6\u671f: Late Ming Dynasty; Early Qing Dynasty; Middle Qing Dynasty
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587: 白下西风落叶侵，重来此地一登临。清笳皓月秋依垒，野烧寒星夜出林。万古河山应有主，频年戈甲苦相寻。从教一掬新亭泪，江水平添十丈深。

works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: The author was unwilling to die in the Ming Dynasty, but could not resist the Qing Dynasty. His ideals in his prime could not be realized, so he had to place his grief, anger and helplessness in the mountains and rivers.
works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: The author felt helpless after his country was destroyed, his family was ruined, and his career was frustrated.
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587: 舞风矶燕如赤尾，吹浪江豚也白头。水阔天高愁骋望，寻思但是莫登楼。

works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: The author wrote it with his feelings when he went to the Yuhuatai.
works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: It expresses the author's thoughts of national subjugation.
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587: 生平四十老柴荆，此日麻鞋拜故京。谁使山河全破碎？可堪翦伐到园陵！牛羊践履多新草，冠盖雍容半旧卿。歌泣不成天已暮，悲风日夜起江生。

works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: Xie Hun wrote it after visiting the West Lake in Danyang (now Danyang, Jiangsu Province).
works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: The scenery of Xichi is described in a concise and vivid style, and the poet's infinite yearning and nostalgia for enjoying the sightseeing tour with his relatives and friends are expressed in a strong tone. The whole poem has fresh language and meticulous depiction of landscapes. It can be regarded as a new and innovative work in the poetry world of the Eastern Jin Dynasty where Xuanyan poetry style dominated. The structure of the poem is naturally divided into three sections. The first four sentences are about what I thought before traveling. From "Xiaoyaoyue City Si" to "Moving to Yinfangke" is the second part, traveling through the West Lake. The third paragraph expresses the feelings of traveling to Xichi. It can be seen from this poem that the landscapes written by Xie Huan are no longer mechanically copied from metaphysics like Sun Chuo's, but truly embody the comfort and understanding generated by people's dissolution of limited life into eternal nature. The whole poem has fresh language and meticulous depiction of landscapes. It can be regarded as a new and innovative work in the poetry world of the Eastern Jin Dynasty where Xuanyan poetry style dominated.
works.\u76f8\u5173\u4e8b\u4ef6: Xie Hun travels to Xichi
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587:
悟彼蟋蟀唱，信此劳者歌。
有来岂不疾，良游常蹉跎。
逍遥越城肆，愿言屡经过。
回阡被陵阙，高台眺飞霞。
惠风荡繁囿，白云屯曾阿。
景昃鸣禽集，水木湛清华。
褰裳顺兰沚，徙倚引芳柯。
美人愆岁月，迟暮独如何？
无为牵所思，南荣诫其多。

石激悬流雪满湾，五龙潜处野云闲。
暂收雷电九峰下，且饮溪潭一水间。
浪引浮槎依北岸，波分晓日浸东山。
回瞻四面如看画，须信游人不欲还。

works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: This poem was written by Yu Youren when he escaped from danger in Kaifeng and fled to Shanghai in 1904 and went to Nanjing. He was 25 years old.
works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: The first half records his escape from danger, and the tone is rejoicing. The second half describes his journey south, and the emotion turns to sadness and indignation. Four sentences are written in succession, and the emotion is deep.
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587:
虎口余生敢自矜，天留铁汉卜将兴。
短衣散发三千里，亡命南来哭孝陵。

works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: The wonderful dances and songs on the Qinhuai River in Nanjing in the late Ming Dynasty and the waterside brothels beside the Changban Bridge once embellished the prosperity of an era. In March of the seventeenth year of Chongzhen (1644), a great revolution tore apart the dancing clothes and singing fans in this hometown of the Six Dynasties. The beautiful women in pink (title) and those Jin Chang Xi Kes who prided themselves on being talented and famous people finally suffered the disaster of destruction and dispersed. In the past, it was a land of golden powder that played all night long. Now, the bustling things have ceased, and the past is gone. As far as you can see, you can see a desolate and dilapidated scene. Faced with this situation, as a poet who has personally experienced this dream of rise and fall and is overwhelmed by the sorrow of Shuili Maixiu, how can he not be sad and have mixed feelings! Yu Huai's "Jinling Miscellaneous Feelings" is a work that uses the rise and fall of the Six Dynasties as a metaphor for real events, nostalgic for the past, regretting the present, and expressing aspirations.
works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: The first three couplets of the poem describe the brothel prostitutes endlessly indulging in orchestral singing and dancing and wandering around the landscape. In fact, they reveal the empty and decadent life of the feudal kings and scholar-bureaucrats and the decline of the entire social atmosphere. In the last couplet, "The golden hairpins in the Wu Palace, the beams and drums in the courtyard, and the poplars and swallows leisurely strolling together", the poet returned to reality from his racing thoughts and reveries, but saw poplars falling in the snow, swallows pruning willows, and the spring light.
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587:
六朝佳丽晚烟浮，擘阮弹筝上酒楼
小扇画鸾乘雾去，轻帆带雨入江流。
山中梦冷依宏景，湖畔歌残倚莫愁。
吴殿金钗梁院鼓,杨花燕子共悠悠。

works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: During the Qingli period, he served as Wai Lang, a member of the Yu Department of the Ministry of Finance, and was assigned to Nanjing.
works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: From the distant trees of Pingwu in front of me, I recall the prosperity of the six generations and express my emotion about the vicissitudes of life.
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587:
六代繁华一瞬间，平芜远树不胜间。
倚楼天暝云如幕，知是琅琊幕府山。

works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: Focus on rendering the scene of crisp autumn air and majestic mountains and rivers, with bold and vigorous brushstrokes and magnificent momentum. The last couplet expresses his heart directly, showing the poet's extraordinary courage and boldness.
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587:
绿江村落带林泉，幕府秋晴爽气高。
苍岭烟岚开日驭，紫宵宫阙泻银涛。
地达多景诗无暇，客是同心与亦豪。
道眼向来谁了了，笑真仅海一鸿毛。

works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: "Zhongshan Plum Blossoms" symbolizes the founding of the Zhu Ming Dynasty, and this is also the purpose of "Zhongshan Plum Blossoms" written by Xu Yuanquan after the Yi Dynasty.

works.\u65f6\u671f: Late Ming Dynasty; Early Qing Dynasty
works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: Chen Yuanyuan once entered the palace, and was later acquired by Tian Hongyu, the father of Emperor Chongzhen's Tian Guifei, and was given to Liaodong Commander-in-Chief and Pingxi Bo Wu Sangui as a concubine. Li Zicheng's peasant uprising army captured Beijing and Chen Yuanyuan was captured. Out of personal hatred, Wu Sangui led Qing troops into the pass, counterattacked Beijing, and regained Chen Yuanyuan. Wu Weiye was the second-ranked scholar in the Ming Dynasty and a former editor of the Hanlin Academy. He hated Wu Sangui for inviting wolves into the house, so he wrote "Yuanyuanqu", which satirized Wu Sangui.
works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: This poem reflects a series of major historical events in the late Ming and early Qing dynasties through the gathering, separation and separation of the famous prostitute Chen Yuanyuan and Wu Sangui in the late Ming and early Qing dynasties, and euphemistically condemns Wu Sangui's rebellious behavior. The whole poem cleverly links Wu Sangui and Chen Yuanyuan with Wu Wang Fucha and Xi Shi. At the same time, it also uses many historical allusions into the poem, thus giving the poem a deep sense of history.
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587: 鼎湖当日弃人间，破敌收京下玉关，恸哭六军俱缟素，冲冠一怒为红颜。红颜流落非吾恋，逆贼天亡自荒宴。电扫黄巾定黑山，哭罢君亲再相见。相见初经田、窦家，侯门歌舞出如花。许将戚里箜篌伎，等取将军油壁车。家本姑苏浣花里，圆圆小字娇罗绮。梦向夫差苑里游，宫娥拥入君王起。前身合是采莲人，门前一片横塘水。横塘双桨去如飞，何处豪家强载归。此际岂知非薄命，此时唯有泪沾衣。薰天意气连宫掖，明眸皓齿无人惜。夺归永巷闭良家，教就新声倾坐客。坐客飞觞红日暮，一曲哀弦向谁诉?白晰通侯最少年，拣取花枝屡回顾。早携娇鸟出樊笼，待得银河几时渡?恨杀军书抵死催，苦留后约将人误。相约恩深相见难，一朝蚁贼满长安。可怜思妇楼头柳，认作天边粉絮看。遍索绿珠围内第，强呼绛树出雕阑。若非壮士全师胜，争得蛾眉匹马还?蛾眉马上传呼进，云鬟不整惊魂定。蜡炬迎来在战场，啼妆满面残红印。专征萧鼓向秦川，金牛道上车千乘。斜谷云深起画楼，散关月落开妆镜。传来消息满江乡，乌桕红经十度霜。教曲伎师怜尚在，浣纱女伴忆同行。旧巢共是衔泥燕，飞上枝头变凤凰。长向尊前悲老大，有人夫婿擅侯王。当时只受声名累，贵戚名豪竞延致。一斛明珠万斛愁，关山漂泊腰肢细。错怨狂风飏落花，无边春色来天地。尝闻倾国与倾城，翻使周郎受重名。妻子岂应关大计?英雄无奈是多情。全家白骨成灰土，一代红妆照汗青。君不见馆娃初起鸳鸯宿，越女如花看不足。香径尘生乌自啼，屧廊人去苔空绿。换羽移宫万里愁，珠歌翠舞古梁州。为君别唱吴宫曲，汉水东南日夜流!

works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: Lu You was called back to Lin'an from Sichuan. In February of that year, he left Chengdu and headed east along the Yangtze River, passing by Jiankang in the autumn. This poem was written by him when he climbed up to the Heart-Enhancing Pavilion to look at the surrounding scenery, looking back on his life, and feeling worried about his country.
works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: This poem first describes the scenery and then expresses the emotions, and the hierarchical arrangement is very clear: the first four sentences describe the "Eastward Journey", leaving the Three Gorges to Jinling to view the scenery; the last four sentences describe the scenery of Jinling, thus reaching the "lonely minister"'s "worrying time" meaning".
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587: 蜀栈秦关岁月遒，今年乘兴却东游。全家稳下黄牛峡，半醉来寻白鹭洲。黯黯江云瓜步雨，萧萧木叶石城秋。孤臣老抱忧时意，欲请迁都泪已流。

works.\u65f6\u671f: Late Ming Dynasty; Early Qing Dynasty
works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: After the fall of the country, Gong Xian, as a remnant, was filled with gloomy and melancholy emotions. He took the stage to overlook his motherland and the vast and vast river scenery, which made him feel relaxed. Such aesthetic feelings calmed down his angry mood.
works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: This poetic painting was created with the poetic inspiration of "The platform is higher than the city gate, and you can see the open river". It depicts the scene of Gong Xian and his friend Fei Mi going up to the platform and looking into the distance.
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587: 与尔倾杯酒，闲登山上台。台高出城阙，一望大江开。日入牛羊下，天空鸿雁来。六朝无废址，满地是苍苔。

works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: It satirizes that Emperor Yang of the Sui Dynasty imitated Empress Chen's example of arrogance, extravagance and lewdness, which led to the destruction of the country.
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587: 玉树歌阑海云黑，花庭忽作青芜国。秦淮有水水无情，还向金陵漾春色。杨家二世安九重，不御华芝嫌六龙。百幅锦帆风力满，连天展尽金芙蓉。珠翠丁星复明灭，龙头劈浪哀笳发。千里涵空澄水魂，万枝破鼻飘香雪。漏转霞高沧海西，颇黎枕上闻天鸡。鸾弦代雁曲如语，一醉昏昏天下迷。四方倾动烟尘起，犹在浓香梦魂裹。后主荒宫有晓莺，飞来只隔西江水。

works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: It satirizes that Emperor Yang of the Sui Dynasty imitated Empress Chen's example of arrogance, extravagance and lewdness, which led to the destruction of the country.
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587: 玉树歌阑海云黑，花庭忽作青芜国。秦淮有水水无情，还向金陵漾春色。杨家二世安九重，不御华芝嫌六龙。百幅锦帆风力满，连天展尽金芙蓉。珠翠丁星复明灭，龙头劈浪哀笳发。千里涵空澄水魂，万枝破鼻飘香雪。漏转霞高沧海西，颇黎枕上闻天鸡。鸾弦代雁曲如语，一醉昏昏天下迷。四方倾动烟尘起，犹在浓香梦魂裹。后主荒宫有晓莺，飞来只隔西江水。

works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: It describes the spring scene of Xuanwu Lake with full verve and expresses the poet's deep feelings, praise and yearning for Xuanwu Lake.
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587: 武湖头春可怜,绕堤花柳媚遥天。风微凫鹭参差下,日丽楼台柴翠连。龙气深秋晴似雨,马蹄芳草暖浮烟。白云自是神仙署,谁道蓬莱弱水边。

works.\u4f5c\u54c1\u540d: 《登梅岗望金陵，赠族侄高座寺僧中孚》
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587: 钟山抱金陵，霸气昔腾发。天开帝王居，海色照宫阙。群峰如逐鹿，奔走相驰突。江水九道来，云端遥明没。时迁大运去，龙虎势休歇。我来属天清，登览穷楚越。吾宗道门秀，特异鸾凤骨。众星罗青天，明者独有月。冥居顺生理，草木不剪伐。烟窗引蔷薇，石壁老野蕨。吴风谢安屐，白足傲履袜。几宿一下山，萧然忘干谒。谈经演金偈，降鹤舞海雪。时闻天香来，了与世事绝。佳游不可得，春风惜远别。赋诗留岩屏，千载庶不灭。

works.\u4f5c\u54c1\u540d: 《登梅岗望金陵，赠族侄高座寺僧中孚》

works.\u540d\u5b57: 李白、吴道子、颜真卿、赵孟頫

works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: This poem was written shortly after the author settled in Jinling, so he calls himself "a guest for a long time" and "sorrowful at home".
works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: The distant view, the sunset, and the boat in the middle of the poem are all painted by the painter. The last two sentences translate the meaning of Liu Yong's words "Where can I wake up tonight", but the realm is broader and there is a sense of vastness.
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587:
江天忽无际，一舸在中流。远岫已将没，夕阳犹未收。
自怜为客惯，转觉到家愁。别酒初醒处，苍烟下白鸥。
到眼无埃壒，苍茫入素秋。万家斜照外，千古大江流。金碧翔霄表，虬龙压石头。长安称雁塔，此亦旧皇州。
到眼无埃壒，苍茫入素秋。万家斜照外，千古大江流。金碧翔霄表，虬龙压石头。长安称雁塔，此亦旧皇州。

works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: Huiji Temple is located in the north of Tangquan Street, Pukou District, Nanjing City. It was first built in the Southern Dynasties. The existing relics in the temple include foundation stones, inscriptions, ancient wells and three ancient ginkgo trees, all of which are over a thousand years old. It is said that they were planted by Xiao Tong, the prince of Xiaoliang in the Southern Dynasty. .
works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: In a 36-line song, the poet recorded its natural appearance and historical origins, as well as related legends and anecdotes in a fascinating and quite accurate way. He chanted the powerful vitality and outstanding integrity of this "rare auspiciousness", traced the footsteps of the predecessors, and inspired others. Houkun's Reverie is actually a masterpiece that praises Jinsiwuhuatianbao and the local culture of Liangpu.
works.\u76f8\u5173\u4e8b\u4ef6: During the Liu and Song Dynasties of the Southern Dynasties, Emperor Wu Liu Yuwan came to visit; during the Xiao Liang Dynasty, Prince Zhaoming Xiao Tong studied here.
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587: 卅四年秋，偕汉吾、季仲由狮子林至汤泉惠济寺，访所谓银杏者。树凡三本，大可十二人围，小亦七八人围，相传南唐时物，或曰北宋物也。爰鉤其约略，并长句存之。汤泉镇阴惠济寺，中有银杏希世瑞。于寺东隅奇生一，于寺西陬偶生二。长者巍巍伯氏行，仲者雍穆拱名次。修容叔氏更奇特，岸然离立耸孤志。怒枝高亚力撑天，繁荫宓勃香萝地。肉死皮皴蚀冻雨，乳垂子落滴寒翠。荒渺幽迥忘岁年,生不南唐定宋季。可怜莘老与熙载,不能详此入碑记。忆昔寺蒙恩宠赐，昭明游息之所寄。良夜几闻读书声，芳园时发春草思。星移世易人物换，独余老木煦生意。千年俄顷真如醉，邯郸惊醒卢生睡。樗散幸为无用材，斧斤乃不遭妒忌。古干人谁三代器，破瓦心惊九朝事。黄蒿芃芃跳狐狸，青磷隐隐号魑魅。菀枯空作身世悲，兴亡屡下山河泪。前之览者知凡几，后之览者又将至。我今对树发长叹，苦留怀古几行字。

脂车总驰轮，泛舟理飞棹。
丝染墨悲叹，路歧杨感悼。

悟彼蟋蟀唱，信此劳者歌。
有来岂不疾，良游常蹉跎。
逍遥越城肆，愿言屡经过。
回阡被陵阙，高台眺飞霞。
惠风荡繁囿，白云屯曾阿。
景昃鸣禽集，水木湛清华。
褰裳顺兰沚，徙倚引芳柯。
美人愆岁月，迟暮独如何。
无为牵所思，南荣戒其多。

辞满岂多秩，谢病不待年。
偶与张邴合，久欲还东山。
圣灵昔回眷，微尚不及宣。
何意冲飙激，烈火纵炎烟。
焚玉发昆峯，余燎遂见迁。
投沙理既迫，如邛愿亦愆。
长与欢爱别，永绝平生缘。
浮舟千仞壑，揔辔万寻巅。
流沫不足险，石林岂为艰！
闽中安可处，日夜念归旋。
事踬两如直，心惬三避贤。
托身青云上，栖岩挹飞泉。
盛明荡氛昏，贞休康屯邅。
殊方咸成贷，微物豫采甄。
感深操不固，质弱易版缠。
曾是反昔园，语往实款然。
曩基即先筑，故池不更穿。
果木有旧行，壤石无远延。
虽非休憩地，聊取永日闲。
卫生自有经，息阴谢所牵。
夫子照情素，探怀授往篇。

白珪尚可磨，斯言易为缁。
虽抱中孚爻，犹劳贝锦诗。
寸心若不亮，微命察如丝。
日月垂光景，成贷遂兼兹。
出宿薄京畿，晨装抟鲁飔。
重经平生别，再与朋知辞。
故山日已远，风波岂还时。
苕苕万里帆，茫茫终何之？
游当罗浮行，息必庐霍期。
越海凌三山，游湘历九嶷。
钦圣若旦暮，怀贤亦凄其。
皎皎明发心，不为岁寒欺。

泉源安首流。川末澄远波。晨光被水族。晓气歇林阿。两江皎平逈。三山郁骈罗。南帆望越峤。北榜指齐河。关扃绕天邑。襟带抱尊华。长城非壑崄。峻岨似荆芽。攒楼贯白日。摛堞隐丹霞。征夫喜观国。游子迟见家。流连入京引。踯躅望乡歌。弥前叹景促。逾近勌路多。偕萃犹如兹。弘易将谓何。
泉源安首流。川末澄远波。晨光被水族。晓气歇林阿。两江皎平逈。三山郁骈罗。南帆望越峤。北榜指齐河。关扃绕天邑。襟带抱尊华。长城非壑崄。峻岨似荆芽。攒楼贯白日。摛堞隐丹霞。征夫喜观国。游子迟见家。流连入京引。踯躅望乡歌。弥前叹景促。逾近勌路多。偕萃犹如兹。弘易将谓何。
丹墀设金屏，瑶榭陈王床。温宫冬开燠，清殿夏含霜。弱蕊布遐馥，轻叶振远芳。弥望少无际，肆睇周华疆。象阙对驰道，飞廉瞩方塘。邸寺送晖曜，槐柳自成行。通川溢轻舻，长街盈方箱。顾此爝火微，胡颜厕天光。
崇堂临万雉，层楼跨九成。瑶轩笼翠幌，组幕翳云屏。阶上晓露洁，林下夕风清。蔓藻嬛绿叶，芳兰媚紫茎。极望周天险，留察浃神京。交渠纷绮错，列植发华英。士女眩街里，轩冕曜都城。万轸杨金镳，千轴树兰旌。

风观要春景，月谢迎秋光。
沿波被华若，随山茂贞芳。

灵山纪地德，地险资岳灵。终南表秦观，少室迩王城。翠凤翔淮海，衿带绕神坰。北阜何其峻，林薄杳葱青。
发地多奇岭，干云非一状。合沓共隐天，参差互相望。郁律构丹巘，崚嶒起青嶂。势随九疑高，气与三山壮。
即事既多美，临眺殊复奇。南瞻储胥观，西望昆明池。山中咸可悦，赏逐四时移。春光发陇首，秋风生桂枝。
多值息心侣，结架山之足。八解鸣涧流，四禅隐岩曲。窈冥终不见，萧条无可欲。所愿从之游，寸心于此足。
君王挺逸趣，羽旆临崇基。白云随玉趾，青霞杂桂旗。淹留访五药，顾步伫三芝。于焉仰镳驾，岁暮以为期。

works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: It was written in the spring of the second year of Emperor Jianwu of Qi Ming Dynasty (495) when Xie Zao was on his way to become the prefect. Xinlinpu is located southwest of Jinling, the capital, not far from the capital. It shows that Xie Tiao had just set out on his journey when he wrote this poem.
works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: At this time, the poet was in a very conflicted mood. Although he was favored by King Sui and Emperor Ming of Qi due to his outstanding literary talent, and his official career was relatively smooth; however, he had suffered slander and tasted the taste of backstabbing. Therefore, the two thoughts of settling for an honorary position and retreating bravely were always entangled in his chest. He always wants to find a compromise between the two. This poem reflects his mentality. The first half describes the scenery seen during the trip to the river, which hints at the feeling of leaving the country and leaving home; the second half describes the thoughts of living in a secluded place far away from harm, which is quite self-comforting. "Having a feeling of happiness and wealth, and restoring the interest of Cangzhou" is exactly what he hopes for.
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587: 江路西南永，归流东北鹜。天际识归舟，云中辨江树。旅思倦摇摇，孤游昔巳屡。既欢怀禄情，复协沧洲趣。嚣尘自兹隔，赏心于此遇。虽无玄豹姿，终隐南山雾。

works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: It depicts the prosperous scenery seen when returning to the capital Jiankang by boat.
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587: 洛城何郁郁，杳与云霄半。前望苍龙门，斜瞻白鹤馆。槐垂御沟道，柳缀金堤岸。

works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: It depicts the prosperous scenery seen when returning to the capital Jiankang by boat.
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587: 洛城何郁郁，杳与云霄半。前望苍龙门，斜瞻白鹤馆。槐垂御沟道，柳缀金堤岸。

works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: Describe the scenery of Zhoushan Mountain.
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587: 紫川通太液，丹岑连少华。堂皇更隐映，松灌杂交加。荇蒲浮新叶，渔舟绕落花。浴童竞浅岸，漂女择平沙。极望伤春目，回车归狭斜。

works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: As an important minister of Emperor Wu of Liang Dynasty, Liu Yun was often ordered to compose poems. He was invited to "Climb Jingyang Tower" with Xiao Yan.
works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: The first two sentences describe the magnificent scene that you can see when you climb up to Jingyang Tower, which is permeated with the refreshing air of autumn and the exhilarating atmosphere of the vastness. "The poplars grow tall and the trees grow high in autumn." The condensed sentences form a strong emotional tension. The last two sentences praise Emperor Wu of Liang for being wise. The master of the divine horse will definitely be able to establish the great achievements of the heroic king.
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587: 太液沧波起，长杨高树秋。翠华承汉远，雕辇逐风游。

works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: The poet's lyrical work was written when he returned from Chang'an and approached the capital city of Jiankang.
works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: The first four sentences explain his capture and release, which is a painful review for the poet. The next four sentences are about the word "risk heart", describing the love affairs, connecting the past and the present in time, and writing from the beginning to Fangshan in space. The last two sentences are the direct expression of the poet's extreme sadness after experiencing the chaos.
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587: 秦军坑赵卒，遂有一人生。虽还旧乡里，危心曾未平。淮源比桐柏，方山似削成。犹疑屯虏骑，尚畏值胡兵。空村馀拱木，废邑有颓城。旧识既已尽，新知皆异名。百年三万日，处处此伤情。
神襟悯行迈，歧路怆徘徊。遥瞻十里陌，傍望九城台。凤管流虚谷，龙骑藉春荄。晓光浮野映，朝烟承日回。沙文浪中积，春阴江上来。柳叶带风转，桃花含雨开。圣情蕴珠绮，札命表英才。顾怜珷玞质，何以俪琼瑰。

works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: This is a poem written by Emperor Wu of Liang, the "Emperor Bodhisattva", after visiting the Great Aijing Temple in Zhongshan.
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587: 曰予受尘缚。未得留盖缠。三有同永夜。六道等长眠。才性乏方便。智力非善权。生住无停相。剎那即徂迁。叹逝比悠稔。交臂乃奢年。从流既难反。弱丧谓不然。二苦常追随。三毒自烧然。贪痴养忧畏。热恼坐焦煎。道心理归终。信首故宜先。驾言追善友。回舆寻胜缘。面势周大地。萦带极长川。棱层叠嶂远。迤逦隥道悬。朝日照花林。光风起香山。飞鸟发差池。出云去连绵。落英分绮色。坠露散珠圆。当道兰靡。临阶竹便娟。幽谷响嘤嘤。石濑鸣溅溅。萝短未中揽。葛嫩不任牵。攀缘傍玉涧。褰陟度金泉。长途弘翠微。香楼间紫烟。慧居超七净。梵住踰八禅。始得展身敬。方乃遂心虔。菩提圣种子。十力良福田。正趣果上果。归依天中天。一道长死生。有无离二边。何待空同右。岂羡汾阳前。以我初觉意。贻尔后来贤。
高轩聊骋望，焕景入川梁。波横山渡影，雨罢叶生光。日移花色异，风散水纹长。

works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: This poem was written by Chen Shubao, Shangshu Pushe Jiang, and Youshan Temple.
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587: 时宰磻溪心，非关狎竹林。鹫岳青松绕，鸡峰白日沈。天迥浮云细，山空明月深。摧残枯树影，零落古藤阴。霜村夜乌去，风路寒猿吟。自悲堪出俗，讵是欲抽簪。

works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: This poem was written by Chen Shubao, Shangshu Pushe Jiang, and Youshan Temple.
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587: 时宰磻溪心，非关狎竹林。鹫岳青松绕，鸡峰白日沈。天迥浮云细，山空明月深。摧残枯树影，零落古藤阴。霜村夜乌去，风路寒猿吟。自悲堪出俗，讵是欲抽簪。
霢霂时雨霁，清和孟夏肇。栖宿绿野中，登顿丹霞杪。敬仰高人德，抗志尘物表。三空豁已悟，万有一何小。始从情所寄，冥期谅不少。荷衣步林泉，麦气凉昏晓。乘风面泠泠，候月临皎皎。烟崖憩古石，云路排征鸟。披径怜森沈，攀条惜杳袅。平生忘是非，朽谢岂矜矫。五净自此涉，六尘庶无扰。
霢霂时雨霁，清和孟夏肇。栖宿绿野中，登顿丹霞杪。敬仰高人德，抗志尘物表。三空豁已悟，万有一何小。始从情所寄，冥期谅不少。荷衣步林泉，麦气凉昏晓。乘风面泠泠，候月临皎皎。烟崖憩古石，云路排征鸟。披径怜森沈，攀条惜杳袅。平生忘是非，朽谢岂矜矫。五净自此涉，六尘庶无扰。

works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: The host who was bidding farewell felt especially sad for this far away farewell. He stood at the head of the river and looked far into the distance, as if he wanted to follow the shadow of the boats returning to the boat and see through the destination where his friend was going.
works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: This is a typical farewell song.
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587: 凤吹临伊水，时驾出河梁。野燎村田黑，江秋岸荻黄。隔城闻上鼓，回舟隐去樯。神襟爱远别，流睇极清漳。
林香雨气新，山寺绿无尘。遂结云外侣，共游天上春。鹤鸣金阁丽，僧语竹房邻。待月水流急，惜花风起频。何方非坏境，此地有归人。回首空门外，皤然一幻身。
系舟仙宅下，清磬落春风。雨数芝田长，云开石路重。古房清磴接，深殿紫烟浓。鹤驾何时去，游人自不逢。
明徵君旧宅，陈后主题诗。迹在人亡处，山空月满时。宝瓶无破响，道树有低枝。已是伤离客，仍逢靳尚祠。
明徵君旧宅，陈后主题诗。迹在人亡处，山空月满时。宝瓶无破响，道树有低枝。已是伤离客，仍逢靳尚祠。
置酒延落景，金陵凤凰台。长波写万古，心与云俱开。借问往昔时，凤凰为谁来。凤凰去已久，正当今日回。明君越羲轩，天老坐三台。豪士无所用，弹弦醉金罍。东风吹山花，安可不尽杯。六帝没幽草，深宫冥绿苔。置酒勿复道，歌钟但相催。

works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: It vividly depicts the night scene in the river from Zhenglu Pavilion (in today's Nanjing) to Guangling (today's Yangzhou), expressing the poet's love for the mountains and rivers of his motherland and his joy of traveling.
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587: 船下广陵去，月明征虏亭。山花如绣颊，江火似流萤。
苍苍金陵月，空悬帝王州。天文列宿在，霸业大江流。绿水绝驰道，青松摧古丘。台倾鳷鹊观，宫没凤凰楼。别殿悲清暑，芳园罢乐游。一闻歌玉树，萧瑟后庭秋。
携妓东土山，怅然悲谢安。我妓今朝如花月，他妓古坟荒草寒。白鸡梦后三百岁，洒酒浇君同所欢。酣来自作青海舞，秋风吹落紫绮冠。彼亦一时，此亦一时，浩浩洪流之咏何必奇。

works.\u4f5c\u54c1\u540d: 《玩月金陵城西孙楚酒楼，达曙歌吹，日晚乘醉着紫绮裘乌纱巾，与酒客数人棹歌秦淮，往石头访崔四侍御》
《玩月金陵城西孙楚酒楼，达曙歌吹，日晚乘醉着紫绮裘乌纱巾，与酒客数人棹歌秦淮，往石头访崔四侍御》

works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587: 天东日出天西下，雌凤孤飞女龙寡。青溪白石不相望，堂中远甚苍梧野。冻壁霜华交隐起，芳根中断香心死。浪乘画舸忆蟾蜍，月娥未必婵娟子。楚管蛮弦愁一概，空城舞罢腰支在。当时欢向掌中销，桃叶桃根双姊妹。破鬟矮堕凌朝寒，白玉燕钗黄金蝉。风车雨马不持去，蜡烛啼红怨天曙。

works.\u4f5c\u54c1\u540d: 《摄山栖霞寺明征君之碑铭辞》

works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587: 冷烟轻澹傍衰丛，此夕秦淮驻断蓬。栖雁远惊沽酒火，乱鸦高避落帆风。地销王气波声急，山带秋阴树影空。六代精灵人不见，思量应在月明中。
南山势回合，灵境依此住。殿转云崖阴，僧探石泉度。龙蛇争翕习，神鬼皆密护。万壑奔道场，群峰向双树。天花飞不著，水月白成路。今日观身我，归心复何处。

works.\u4f5c\u54c1\u540d: 《与沈十九拾遗同游栖霞寺上方于亮上人院会宿》
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587:
其一
摄山标胜绝，暇日谐想瞩。
萦纡松路深，缭绕云岩曲。
重楼回树杪，古像凿山腹。
人远水木清，地深兰桂馥。
层台耸金碧，绝顶摩净绿。
下界诚可悲，南朝纷在目。
焚香入古殿，待月出深竹。
稍觉天籁清，自伤人世促。
宗雷此相遇，偃放从所欲。
清论松枝低，闲吟茗花熟。
一生如土梗，万虑相桎梏。
永愿事潜师，穷年此栖宿。
其二
偶来人境外，心赏幸随君。
古殿烟霞夕，深山松桂薰。
岩花点寒溜，石磴扫春云。
清净诸天近，喧尘下界分。
名僧康宝月，上客沈休文。
共宿东林夜，清猿彻曙闻。

works.\u4f5c\u54c1\u540d: 《与沈十九拾遗同游栖霞寺上方于亮上人院会宿》
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587:
其一

其二
偶来人境外，心赏幸随君。
古殿烟霞夕，深山松桂薰。
岩花点寒溜，石磴扫春云。
清净诸天近，喧尘下界分。
名僧康宝月，上客沈休文。
共宿东林夜，清猿彻曙闻。
又解征帆落照中，暮程还过秣陵东。二年辛苦烟波里，赢得风姿似钓翁。
相州吹角欲斜阳，匹马摇鞭宿内黄。僻县不容投刺客，野陂时遇射雕郎。云中粉堞新城垒，店后荒郊旧战场。犹指去程千万里，秣陵烟树在何乡。
秋烟漠漠雨濛濛，不卷征帆任晚风。百口寄安沧海上，一身逃难绿林中。来时楚岸杨花白，去日隋堤蓼穗红。却到故园翻似客，归心迢递秣陵东。

秣陵江上多离别，雨晴芳草烟深。路遥人去马嘶沉。青帘斜挂，新柳万枝金。
隔江何处吹横笛？沙头惊起双禽。徘徊一向几般心。天长烟远，凝恨独沾襟。

无相景幽远，山屏四面开。凭师领鹤去，待我挂冠来。
药为依时采，松宜绕舍栽。林泉自多兴，不是效刘雷。

works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: This poem mainly expresses the feeling of missing people far away. In the first part, the inhabitants of a luxurious house with jade and red doors wasted away the beautiful scenery. They locked the door all day long and lived in solitude. However, it was late and cold in the night, and good dreams were impossible. The second part was about the long distance and heavy news. , the return date is uncertain, and there is no basis for meeting each other. The sound of the anvil in the moonlight at night cannot comfort the lovesickness, but only adds to the pain of lovesickness. The whole word uses expression techniques such as reflection, association, and rendering, and the image is concrete and vivid, full of life.
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587:
玉砌花光锦绣明，朱扉长日镇长扃。
夜寒不去寝难成，炉香烟冷自亭亭。
残月秣陵砧，不传消息但传情。
黄金窗下忽然惊，征人归日二毛生。

后主亡家不悔，江南异代长春。
今日景阳台上，闲人何用伤神。

works.\u4f5c\u54c1\u540d: 《游蒋山题辛夷花寄陈奉礼（本约陈同游，不至）》
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587:
今岁游山已恨迟，山中仍喜见辛夷。
簪缨且免全为累，桃李犹堪别作期。

works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: He built a garden in Baitang, his residence, located exactly between the east gate of Jiangning Fucheng and Zhongshan. He named the garden "Banshan Garden" and called himself "Banshan Jushi".
works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: The poet lived and built gardens in the inaccessible mountains and forests, feeling the tranquility and beauty of the natural mountains and rivers, thus expressing his desire for seclusion and transcending the world.
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587:
自予营北渚，数至两山间。
临路爱山好，出山愁路难。
山花如水净，山鸟与云闲。
我欲抛山去，山仍劝我还。
祗应身後冢，亦是眼中山。
且复依山住，归鞍未可攀。

works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: Wang Anshi often visited Zhongshan to enjoy the leisure and tranquility.
works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: There are 8 words "mountain" in the 28 words in the whole poem, which highlight the eternity and leisurely nature of mountains, and permeate the poet's deep feelings for mountains.
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587:
其一
终日看山不厌山，买山终待老山间。
山花落尽山长在，山水空流山自闲。
其二
两山松栎暗朱藤，一水中间胜武陵。
午梵隔云知有寺，夕阳归去不逢僧。

works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: In his later years, Wang Anshi built a "half-mountain garden" in Zhongshan and lived a life of retreat in the mountains and forests. His poetic style was profound and graceful, and his expression skills and language were exquisite and sophisticated.
works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: Wang Anshi was looking for seclusion and admiring the scenery, wandering among the flowers and grass, "looking down at the pure green, and standing there on a small stand with a delicate fragrance."
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587:
月映林塘静，风含笑语凉。
俯窥怜绿净，小立伫幽香。
携幼寻新菂，扶衰坐野航。
延缘久未已，岁晚惜流光。

works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: The apricot blossoms blooming near the water are unique and elegant, complementing the enchanting and beautiful flowers on the trees. With the help of the description of quiet flowers, you can express your own life emotions.
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587:
一陂春水绕花身，花影妖娆各占春。
纵被春风吹作雪，绝胜南陌碾成尘

works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: Wen Tianxiang continued his journey north, and his close friend Deng Yan was allowed to stay. When bidding farewell to Wen Tianxiang at Jinlingyi, Deng Yan wrote the poem "Nian Nujiao" as a gift, and Wen Tianxiang composed the poem "酹江月·合" as a reward.
works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: The whole poem uses tragic and passionate language to show that despite the hardships he endured in the anti-Yuan struggle, he is still determined to uphold national integrity; now that he is in prison, his ambition and ambition are still there, and he expresses unlimited confidence in the future of the country. , the ending expresses the attachment and farewell to Deng Yan.
works.\u76f8\u5173\u4e8b\u4ef6: Farewell to Jinling Station, Deng Yan wrote the poem "Niannujiao" as a tribute
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587:
乾坤能大，算蛟龙、元不是池中物。风雨牢愁无着处，那更寒虫四壁。横槊题诗，登楼作赋，万事空中雪。江流如此，方来还有英杰。
堪笑一叶飘零，重来淮水，正凉风新发。镜里朱颜都变尽，只有丹心难灭。去去龙沙，江山回首，青山如发。故人应念，杜鹃枝上残月。

works.\u4f5c\u54c1\u540d: 《菩萨蛮·金陵赏心亭为叶丞相赋》
works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: The author was serving as the counselor of the Jiankang Appeasement Department. This poem was given to Ye Heng when the author came to the Shangxin Pavilion. Ye Heng (1114-1175), courtesy name Mengxi, was a native of Jinhua, Wuzhou (now part of Zhejiang). He is a famous figure who fought against the Jin Dynasty. He served successively as the Councilor for Political Affairs, the Right Prime Minister and the Privy Envoy.
works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: This word is both nostalgic and lyrical. Through the unique imagination, it contains the author's deep sense of sadness and nostalgia, expressing the sadness of life of wandering and having no way to serve the country. This poem was given to Ye Heng by the author when he came to the Heart-Appreciating Pavilion. It not only expresses feelings of nostalgia for others, but also contains a sense of personal life experience, and the emotional expression is relatively obscure. The first film is about mountains. Let me start by saying that Qingshan is like thousands of horses rushing towards it. I want to meet and talk to the elegant tourists. As soon as I start writing, I feel strange and strange, which is unbelievable. The two sentences "Misty Rain" suddenly change: because the misty rain disappeared and blocked the way, the mountains and people were disappointed. In the strange imagination, there is infinite confusion. Since the title clearly states "For Prime Minister Ye", it is not difficult to appreciate the nostalgia between the lines. The next piece is about gull. First, it is a common saying that people's hair turns gray due to sorrow.
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587:
青山欲共高人语。联翩万马来无数。烟雨却低回。望来终不来。
人言头上发。总向愁中白。拍手笑沙鸥。一身都是愁。

works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: "Jingkang Difficulty" broke the dream of free and easy Shuiyun, and he suffered the pain of displacement while fleeing south with his family, and the tone of the poem changed to misery, pathos and anger.
works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: The first film describes the poet's coming to relieve his worries, but in fact, his worries are indispensable. The second film speaks directly from the heart, expressing the sadness that things and people have changed, and dreams are hard to keep. Finally, "The Yangtze River cannot stop flowing eastward" reveals the author's helpless lament about the country's declining power.

works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: "One Hundred Odes of Jinling" is a set of seven-character quatrains written by Zeng Ji in the Southern Song Dynasty. It consists of one volume. The whole book is composed of poems reciting Jiankang monuments.
works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: The author cherishes the historical relics and expresses infinite emotion.
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587:
老瞒虎裂横中州，何物生儿作仲谋。
四十帝中功第一，坏陵无主使人愁。

works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: Li Bai traveled south to Jinling, and Qilu "Climbed the Phoenix Tower of Jinling". In the Northern Song Dynasty, Guo Xiang followed in Taibai's footsteps and also took the stage to compose poems.
works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: The poet went up to the stage and looked into the distance, feeling the sadness of the illusory human affairs and the allegory and melancholy of the vicissitudes of life and the sudden disappearance of prosperity. This poem has the charm of Taibai's poetry and has its own characteristics, expressing a unique nostalgic feeling.
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587:
高台不见凤凰游，浩浩长江入海流。
舞罢青娥同去国，战残白骨尚盈丘。
风摇落日催行棹，湖拥新沙换故洲。
结绮临春无处觅，年年荒草向人愁。

works.\u65f6\u671f: Northern Song Dynasty; Southern Song Dynasty
works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: It describes the autumn scene that the author saw, "Where is the Central Plains, as far as the eye can see thousands of miles away, there are heavy dusk clouds", which expresses the depression of unrealized ambitions and the longing for the homeland.
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587: 泽国又秋晚，天际有飞鸿。中原何在，极目千里暮云重。今古长干桥下，遗恨都随流水，西去几时东。斜日动歌管，萸菊舞西风。江南岸，淮南渡，草连空。石城潮落、寂寞烟树锁离宫。且斗尊前酒美，莫问楼头佳丽，往事有无中。却笑东山老，拥鼻与谁同。

works.\u65f6\u671f: Northern Song Dynasty; Southern Song Dynasty
works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: It describes the autumn scene that the author saw, "Where is the Central Plains, as far as the eye can see thousands of miles away, there are heavy dusk clouds", which expresses the depression of unrealized ambitions and the longing for the homeland.
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587: 泽国又秋晚，天际有飞鸿。中原何在，极目千里暮云重。今古长干桥下，遗恨都随流水，西去几时东。斜日动歌管，萸菊舞西风。江南岸，淮南渡，草连空。石城潮落、寂寞烟树锁离宫。且斗尊前酒美，莫问楼头佳丽，往事有无中。却笑东山老，拥鼻与谁同。

works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587: 泽国又秋晚，天际有飞鸿。中原何在，极目千里暮云重。今古长干桥下，遗恨都随流水，西去几时东。斜日动歌管，萸菊舞西风。江南岸，淮南渡，草连空。石城潮落、寂寞烟树锁离宫。且斗尊前酒美，莫问楼头佳丽，往事有无中。却笑东山老，拥鼻与谁同。

works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: He Zhu revisited Dinglin Temple in Nanjing.
works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: Describing the spring scene, "The east wind opened the door for me first" describes the vitality of early spring.
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587:
破冰泉脉漱篱根，坏衲遥疑挂树猿。
蜡屐旧痕寻不见，东风先为我开门。

works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: In the first year of Deyou in the Southern Song Dynasty (1275), Kong Wenyu, the fifty-third grandson of the Kong family, moved south with his father and brother and settled in Youshan Township, Lishui County (today's Qiqiao Town, Gaochun County).
works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: Taking "the fate of the world changes, poetry and etiquette never cease" as the general guide, he traces the history of family changes, points out the reasons for leaving his hometown, migrating and settling in Youshan Township, tells his own behavior and opinions in running the family, and expresses himself Respect for the ancestors, longing for the hometown, and the desire to strive to inherit the Kong family tradition.
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587:
世运有变迁，诗礼无歇绝。
凫绎云勃兴，雁湖波皎洁。
中原势已倾，南渡疆复裂。
故乡隔烽烟，回首空鸣咽。
我闻游子山，曾留圣人辙。
栖皇适楚都，救世至今切。
爰居复爰处，俎豆幸无缺。
循墙守旧铭，子弟勤诵说。
耕稼馁在中，勿叹谋生拙。
忧道不忧贫，饥寒励豪杰。

works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: When the Southern Song Dynasty collapsed, Jinling's nostalgic poems were more about the pain of death and the sadness of separation.
works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: In "Touching Fish: Climbing the Phoenix Tower", "Let the snow in the north blur the sky, let the east wind change the green, all the time is spent in dreams and drunkenness", the pain of the country's destruction and the death of the family is heart-breaking.

works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: During his tenure in Jiankang, Ma Guangzu also created many literary works about Jiankang.
works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: He enthusiastically eulogized the historical scenery of Jiankang and reflected the grand atmosphere of rejuvenating the country and beautiful politics.
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587:
长江兮淙淙，踞虎兮蟠龙。
秀群英兮礼乐，览千古兮焉穷。
蹇谁留兮青溪，穆将愉兮寿宫。
思至德兮肇苍姬，避圣嗣兮兴句吴。
竟长干兮游五湖，烨客星兮隐东庐。
坐根石兮定吴都，怀仲父兮秦淮隅。
燎赤壁兮伟北图，忆尚书兮西明居。
孝感兮冰鱼，鹿苑兮儒书。
起乌衣兮见夷吾，运百甓兮恢宏橅。
忠孝兮父子，将相兮叔侄。
登冶城兮想高世，酌贪泉兮徒西壁。
兴文兮雷刘，著书兮陶萧。
大节兮霜凛凛，谪仙兮风飘飘。
云龙上下兮东野，桃花流水兮致尧。
肆荥阳兮忠愤，相先民兮迢迢。
天昌宋兮将有曹，平江南兮斧不膏。
德乖崖兮桑本袤，美中丞兮蓉幕高。
神明兮待制，忠恕兮肤使。
春风兮寿元气，图绘兮回天意。
出师门兮道与南，建留都兮垂万世。
仗征钺兮江无波，死封疆兮人知义。
采石兮功之奇，紫阳兮道之继。
佐乃翁兮南轩，开厥后兮壹是。
泽斯民兮西山，俨元凯兮是似。
庀管钥兮北门，思尚友兮古人。
建芳馨兮堂庑，合荃芷兮盈庭。
袅秋风兮桂枝，缭荷屋兮杜衡。
荐菊兮寒泉，采藻兮落成。
浴兰汤兮沐华，望美人兮并迎。
芳菲菲兮满堂，灵之来兮如云。
聊逍遥兮容与，集琳琅兮锵鸣。
吉日兮辰良，蕙蒸兮椒浆。
元勋兮钜德，日月兮齐光。
介民兮景福，昭昭兮未央。
高山兮景行，千秋兮难忘。

works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: The poet focuses on the rise and fall of the Six Dynasties, pointing out that prosperity is like a dream, and everything is like a passing cloud, returning to nothingness and disillusionment; then he pours his deep nostalgia into the scenery, and combines the scenery with emotions, the sense of history and the sense of reality, and combines the sense of history with the sense of reality. The contrast between the changeable nature of the river and the eternity of the Qinhuai River shows the desolation and sadness of the impermanence of life, and the illusion and helplessness that everything in the world will be submerged in historical time and space.
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587:
六代繁华难重问，此时兴废动吟情。
空流一带秦淮水，朝暮惟闻鸣咽声。

works.\u4f5c\u54c1\u540d: 《赴岭表过金陵蒋山泉老召食阻雨不及往》
works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: The 59-year-old Su Shi was demoted to Yingzhou. Before he could take office, he was demoted to Ningyuan Army Jiedu Deputy Envoy and Huizhou resettlement. He passed by Jinling on his way to his appointment.
works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: The poet does not regard being demoted thousands of miles away from an official position as being sent to the wilderness and suffering punishment, but as an elegant thing for a scholar to explore the secrets and adventures. At the end of the poem, he jokingly joked with an old friend, "When he was young, he painted pictures of Jiangshan, and he called the layman to be the Duke of Quan." This shows the author's open-mindedness and humorous taste.
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587:
今日江头天色恶，炮车云起风欲作。
独望钟山唤宝公，林间白塔如孤鹤。
宝公骨冷唤不闻，却有老泉来唤人。
电眸虎齿霹雳舌，为子吹散千峰云。
南行万里亦何事，一酌曹溪知水味。

works.\u4f5c\u54c1\u540d: 《赴岭表过金陵蒋山泉老召食阻雨不及往》
works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: The 59-year-old Su Shi was demoted to Yingzhou. Before he could take office, he was demoted to Ningyuan Army Jiedu Deputy Envoy and Huizhou resettlement. He passed by Jinling on his way to his appointment.

works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: Su Shi's wife Wang Runzhi believed in Buddhism and died in the capital. On the eve of his death, he left behind his last words and asked his son to walk, leap, and pass to paint a statue of Amitabha.
works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: Su Shi and his son went to Qingliang Temple to worship the statue of Amitabha

works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: Su Shi had a close relationship with the elders of Qingliang Temple.
works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: It praises the elders of Qingliang Temple for being "safe and well-mannered in their youth, and ruthless when encountering things with new syntax." The last sentence uses the allusion of Bodhidharma's "crossing the river with a reed" to express his free and open-minded feelings.
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587:
过淮入洛地多尘，举扇西风欲污人。
但怪云山不改色，岂知江月解分身。
安心有道年颜好，遇物无情句法新。
送我长芦舟一叶，笑看雪浪满衣巾。

works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: When the Southern Song Dynasty collapsed, the Jinling nostalgic poems he wrote were more about the pain of death and the sadness of millet separation, revealing the helplessness and sorrow of the broken mountains and rivers and the sinking of China.
works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: It deeply reveals the reasons for the collapse of the Southern Song Dynasty, and also expresses the helplessness and sorrow of the broken mountains and rivers and the sinking of China.
works.\u76f8\u5173\u4f5c\u54c1: 《滕王阁诗》、《再游玄都观诗序》、《声声慢》、《玉树后庭花》、《夜泊秦淮》
works.\u76f8\u5173\u4eba\u7269: 陈后主、庾亮、王导、王衍、周颛
works.\u76f8\u5173\u4e8b\u4ef6: The Southern Song Dynasty was destroyed, the Eastern Wu Dynasty was destroyed, and the powerful officials of the Eastern Jin Dynasty fought against each other.
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587:
金陵故都最好，有朱楼迢递。嗟倦客、又此凭高，槛外已少佳致。更落尽梨花，飞尽杨花，春也成憔悴。问青山、三国英雄，六朝奇伟。
麦甸葵丘，荒台败垒。鹿豕衔枯荠。正朝打孤城，寂寞斜阳影里。听楼头、哀笳怨角，未把酒、愁心先醉。渐夜深，月满秦淮，烟笼寒水。
凄凄惨惨，冷冷清清，灯火渡头市。慨商女不知兴废。隔江犹唱庭花，余音亹亹。伤心千古，泪痕如洗。乌衣巷口青芜路，认依稀、王谢旧邻里。临春结绮。可怜红粉成灰，萧索白杨风起。
因思畴昔，铁索千寻，谩沈江底。挥羽扇、障西尘，便好角巾私第。清谈到底成何事。回首新亭，风景今如此。楚囚对泣何时已。叹人间、今古真儿戏。东风岁岁还来，吹入钟山，几重苍翠。

works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: This poem was written by Wang Ye in his later years.
works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: The scenery in front of us is full of desolate and melancholy atmosphere, which seems to be an image portrayal of the gloomy decline of the Southern Song Dynasty court, and the twilight of the day and the end of the road. He longed for the Southern Song Dynasty kings to be proactive and regain the Central Plains, and the poets to serve the country and be ambitious like Zu Ti.
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587: 龙蟠虎踞，今古帝王州。水如淮，山似洛，凤来游。五云浮。宇宙无终极，千载恨，六朝事，同一梦休。更莫问间愁。风景悠悠。得似青溪曲，著我扁舟。对残烟衰草，满目是清秋。白鹭汀洲。夕阳收。黄旗紫盖，中兴运，钟王气，护金瓯。驻游跸，开行殿，夹朱楼。送华辀。万里长江险，集鸿雁，列貔貅。扫关河，清海岱，志应酬。机会何常，鹤唳风声处，天意人谋。臣今虽老，未遣壮心休。击楫中流。

works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: and Zhao Lianao Jinling nostalgic.
works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: It expresses the helplessness and sorrow of broken mountains and rivers and sinking land in China.
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587: 翠微亭上醉，搔短发、舞缤纷。问六朝五姓，王姬帝胄，今有谁存。何似乌衣故垒，尚年年，生长儿孙，今古兴亡无据，好将往史俱焚。招魂。何处觅东山，筝泪落清樽。怅石城暗浪，秦淮旧月，东去西奔。休说清谈误国，有清谈，还有斯文。遥睇新亭一笑，漫漫天际江痕。

works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: During the Shaoding period, Wu Qian served as the Shaoqing of the Taifu and the Commander-in-Chief of Huaixi. He moved to the Taifu and had the power to set up envoys along the Yangtze River, knowing Jiankang Prefecture, and staying in Jiangdong to comfort him.
works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: It describes the grand scene of Jinling in spring with red flowers and green willows, music and music, and tourists in full swing. The words "Jiangnan Guest" during the break highlight the sentimentality of the poet's bumpy career and wandering around. The second film refers to the past to reflect on the present, with the poet's sorrow about his life experience of unrealized aspirations and wasted time permeating the lines.

works.\u4f5c\u54c1\u540d: 《三月三日上忠襄坟因之行散得绝句》
works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: When Yang Wanli was serving as deputy envoy to Jiangdong in Nanjing, he visited the grave of his great uncle Yang Bangyi.
works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: Through the depiction of the bronze camels and thorns in the old capital of Jinling, "The Jin Palace and the Wu Palace are still green grass, and the Wang Pavilion and the Xie Pavilion are full of orioles" to express nostalgia for the past.
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587:
长干桥外即乌衣，今着屠沽卖菜儿。
晋殿吴宫犹碧草，王亭谢馆尽黄鹂。

works.\u4f5c\u54c1\u540d: 《陪留守余处恭、总领钱进思、提刑傅景仁游清凉寺即古石头城》
works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: Yang Wanli transferred the deputy envoy to Jiangdong, Nanjing.
works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: The first half of this poem describes the majesty of Stone City, and the second half expresses nostalgia for the past. The poet is deeply worried about the lonely battle situation and spiritual decadence of "Su Jun's battlefield is now wild and grassy, while Zhongmou's temple is ancient and dusty".
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587:
万里长江天上来，石头却欲打江回。
青山外面周如削，紫府中间划洞开。
苏峻战场今草莽，仲谋庙貌古尘埃。
多情白鹭洲前水。月落潮生声自哀。

works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: He was appointed as the Shaoqing of Sinong Temple and was sent to live in Guangde Army (now Guangde County, Anhui Province). On the way to be demoted, he passed by Jinling and parked by the Qinhuai River at night, feeling depressed.
works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: The poem first describes the scene. The author is moved by the scene, thinking about the history of the Six Dynasties, and suddenly feels the illusion of coming and going without a trace. The second film uses the melancholy scenery to reflect on the rights and wrongs of history, and also evokes the melancholy of wandering in life.

思往事，愁如织。怀故国，空陈迹。但荒烟衰草，乱鸦斜日。玉树歌残秋露冷，胭脂井坏寒螀泣。到如今、只有蒋山青，秦淮碧！

works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: It mainly describes the desolation and ruin of Stone City, expressing the author's nostalgia for the past. It uses a set of tragic scenes to express the emotion that the wind and clouds are fleeting, but the green mountains are always there.
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587:
石头城上，望天低吴楚，眼空无物。指点六朝形胜地，惟有青山如壁。蔽日旌旗，连云樯橹，白骨纷如雪。一江南北，消磨多少豪杰。
寂寞避暑离宫，东风辇路，芳草年年发。落日无人松径里，鬼火高低明灭。歌舞尊前，繁华镜里，暗换青青发。伤心千古，秦淮一片明月！

works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587:
胭脂山头平劈破，一道长溪此中过。
匠人十万斧凿功，疑是六丁天所课。
危桥挂空若生成，石壁千仞开锦屏。
两边罗列八九里，吴艎蜀艑西东征。
四方职贡纷联络，扬子大江风浪恶。
九重宵旰劳圣心，特策有司亟兴作。
左通银墅右沙河，滔滔湛湛皆恩波。
可怜伊昔六代时，无有及斯竟若何。
老臣挟书朝帝所，拭目奇观喜欲舞。
稽首作歌颂神武，磨厓镌刻昭万古。

works.\u521b\u4f5c\u80cc\u666f: I visited famous mountains and rivers, interacted with talented knights and knights, and created works while in Jinling.
works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: What the poet sees is the decadent and desolate scene of the old capital. The loss of heroic spirit makes people feel melancholy. The lines of the words are permeated with the sorrow of the country's subjugation, which is nostalgic for the past and hurts the present.
works.\u8bd7\u6b4c\u5168\u6587:
坏墙幽径草青青，何处园林是旧京？
海气或生山背雨，江潮不到石头城。
英雄消歇无人语，形势周遭夕照明。
回首长干思无限，水风杨柳作秋声。

works.\u5185\u5bb9\u7b80\u4ecb: It describes the unique and complex feelings of visiting a clear stream, and depicts the chic image of a fisherman in a small boat amid the cold waves. He might as well be open-minded and pursue a life of joy, simplicity and authenticity.

木落霜清，水底见、金陵城郭。都莫问、南朝兴废，人生哀乐。载酒时时寻伴侣，倚栏处处皆楼阁。对溪云、试放醉时狂，浑如昨。
沙洲外，轻鸥落，风帘下，扁舟泊。更寒波摇漾，绿蓑青箬。为向九原江总道，繁华何似今凉薄。怕素衣、京洛染缁尘，从新濯。
